Over the years, URA has spearheaded efforts to strengthen urban planning and design workflows through the application of urban science, geospatial and data analytics, modelling and simulation (M&S), and AI technologies. 

In 2022, URA was designated as the Whole-of-Government (WOG) Urban Planning & Design Technology Centre of Excellence (URBEX), to lead the planning and design sector in building up these strategic capabilities. The Design & Planning Lab (DPLab) is a multi-disciplinary team in URA which drives URBEX efforts. In recognition of our work, URA was conferred the 2024 Esri Special Achievement in Geographic Information System (GIS) Award for demonstrating vision, leadership and innovation in the use of GIS technology. URA was also awarded the Esri Singapore GeoInnovation Award in 2024 for being amongst Singapore’s most progressive organisations in the use of geospatial solutions to transform the future. Our flagship product, ePlanner (a web-based geospatial platform that brings digital planning tools to WOG) also won the Excellence Award at the Singapore Public Service’s Science, Technology and Engineering Conference 2024. 

In DPLab, you will work alongside talented individuals with expertise in wide-ranging domains including data policy, governance, strategy and engineering, product strategy, business analysis, software engineering, cloud engineering, urban design technologies, data science, AI/Machine Learning, and M&S. You will also work closely with planners, architects, and policymakers to co-create digital solutions that contribute to enhancing the liveability, sustainability and resilience of our city-state.

agency: Ministry of Sustainability and the Environment
job_description:
At MSE and our family of agencies – PUB, NEA, and SFA – we drive sustainability and resilience initiatives that improve the everyday lives of Singaporeans. As part of the MSE Family, you will be at the forefront of innovation, leading efforts to safeguard water resources, protect public health, and ensure food safety. Through partnerships across sectors, we tackle evolving challenges while fostering an inclusive, safe, and supportive workplace. Join us in shaping Singapore’s sustainable future and making a lasting impact on the everyday lives of generations to come.

The Research, Futures & Planning Division (RFPD) functions as a sense-maker across MSE and our 3 statutory boards. We ensure that the MSE family:
•	Plans effectively for the future, through identifying driving forces, challenges and opportunities that impact or will impact our work.
•	Supports the development and implementation of policies and decisions, through a combination of analytics, decision science and behavioural insights.
•	Continually enhances the way our officers work, through driving a culture of innovation, use of technology and data, and process improvement.
•	Allocates resources efficiently and effectively, according to the Ministry's strategic priorities and through a framework for prioritisation and evaluation.  

Within RFPD, the Strategic and Resource Planning (SRP) team oversees: 
•	Planning – We drive Ministry planning through annual work plans, performance accountability, parliamentary processes, senior management retreats, and enterprise risk management.
•	Futures – We identify relevant driving forces, challenges and opportunities, and translate them to actionable insights for policy divisions and statutory boards to plan for.
•	Resource Management – We plan and manage key Ministry resources, including funding and manpower, to ensure optimal allocation in support of MSE's strategic priorities.
•	Infrastructure Coordination – We coordinate the infrastructure planning and reporting of infrastructure projects, covering future infrastructure plans and project prioritisation.
•	Sustainability – We coordinate the sustainability planning and reporting for the MSE family, including decarbonisation plans to drive GreenGov.SG policies and targets.
•	Secretariat – We also represent MSE at inter-agency platforms and coordinate Ministry family positions for whole-of-government issues.

job_title: Assistant Director/Executive Manager/Manager (Nuclear Regulatory Ecosystem Dept)
job_description:
The Radiation Protection and Nuclear Science Group in the National Environment Agency (NEA) is the national authority for radiation protection in Singapore and serves to protect the public, radiation workers and the environment from the harmful effects of radiation. Its Nuclear Safety Division is responsible for developing capabilities, plans and ecosystems to support national nuclear safety & security assessment, radiological emergency response, and nuclear regulatory development efforts. The division is also responsible for ensuring that Singapore fulfils its obligations to international conventions and agreements with respect to the safe and peaceful use of nuclear material and technology.

Singapore is studying the potential deployment of nuclear energy as part of its long-term energy strategy. Singapore will also need to prepare for the possibility of nuclear power deployment by other countries in the region. These endeavours involve planning and strategizing the development of legal and regulatory infrastructure required for a potential nuclear power programme and building up capabilities in nuclear safety, emergency preparedness and response and radiation protection.

You will be a key member of the team that will play a pivotal role in engaging domestic, regional and international stakeholders with the intent of supporting overall WOG public communication and education efforts, to educate and engage the media, public and various stakeholders on nuclear energy and safety.

This is a rare opportunity to be at the forefront of Singapore’s nuclear energy journey.

About Urban Redevelopment Authority (URA)
The Urban Redevelopment Authority (URA) is Singapore’s land use planning and conservation authority. Our mission is to make Singapore a great city to live, work and play. We plan and facilitate the physical development of Singapore, in partnership with agencies and the community, to create a vibrant, sustainable and cosmopolitan city of distinction.

About URBEX and DPLab
Over the years, URA has spearheaded efforts to strengthen urban planning and design workflows through the application of urban science, geospatial and data analytics, modelling and simulation (M&S), and AI technologies. In 2022, URA was designated as the Whole-of-Government (WOG) Urban Planning & Design Technology Centre of Excellence (URBEX), to lead the planning and design sector in building up these strategic capabilities.  
The Design & Planning Lab (DPLab) is a multi-disciplinary team in URA which drives URBEX efforts. In recognition of our work, URA was conferred the 2024 Esri Special Achievement in Geographic Information System (GIS) Award for demonstrating vision, leadership and innovation in the use of GIS technology. URA was also awarded the Esri Singapore GeoInnovation Award in 2024 for being amongst Singapore’s most progressive organisations in the use of geospatial solutions to transform the future. Our flagship product, ePlanner (a web-based geospatial platform that brings digital planning tools to WOG) also won the Excellence Award at the Singapore Public Service’s Science, Technology and Engineering Conference 2024. 
In DPLab, you will work alongside talented individuals with expertise in wide-ranging domains including data policy, governance, strategy and engineering, product strategy, business analysis, software engineering, cloud engineering, urban design technologies, data science, AI/Machine Learning, and M&S.  You will also work closely with planners, architects, and policymakers to co-create digital solutions that contribute to enhancing the liveability, sustainability and resilience of our city-state.

job_title: Senior/ Executive Meteorologist (Forecast Application Development Dept)
job_description:
The Forecast Application Development Department develops tools, products and services to support weather monitoring and forecast operations at Meteorological Service Singapore. The Department’s work involves analysing weather radar and environmental satellite data, post-processing numerical weather predictions and developing data-driven models to improve weather nowcasts and forecasts. To fulfil its mission, the Department also participates in stakeholder engagement and research collaboration activities.

We are looking for experienced individuals to develop and implement innovative weather solutions to support operational weather services, specialised stakeholder/user sectors and the public. The role requires deep meteorological expertise (in areas such as Numerical/AI Weather Prediction and specialised meteorological observing systems) as well as the ability to apply methods in related disciplines such as data science, geographic information systems and software development. You will work with users to translate their requirements for weather services into operational products and services. You will also have opportunities to participate in international conferences and seminars.

job_title: Temp 1 Year - Senior Assistant Exec (Contact Centre Ops & Planning) ServiceSG
job_description:
The operating environment of the Public Service is undergoing fundamental and rapid change. The issues we face are complex and multi-faceted, which impact the way the Public Service operates and organises itself. To prepare the Public Service for these changes, we have begun the journey towards Public Sector Transformation (PST).

The Contact Centre team in ServiceSG aims to work with agencies who own these hotlines to streamline and integrate these contact centres, especially those that provide related services. The goal is to establish a central ServiceSG contact centre that can address the queries of citizens across over 400 citizen related services, serving as an “outsourced shared service” for selected agencies. agency: Ministry of Sustainability and the Environment
job_description:
At MSE and our family of agencies – PUB, NEA, and SFA – we drive sustainability and resilience initiatives that improve the everyday lives of Singaporeans. As part of the MSE Family, you will be at the forefront of innovation, leading efforts to safeguard water resources, protect public health, and ensure food safety. Through partnerships across sectors, we tackle evolving challenges while fostering an inclusive, safe, and supportive workplace. Join us in shaping Singapore’s sustainable future and making a lasting impact on the everyday lives of generations to come.

Climate change poses an asymmetrical challenge for Singapore – we contribute only around 0.1% of global carbon emissions, but will be disproportionately impacted by its effects.
 
In Feb 2021, the Government launched the Singapore Green Plan 2030, a whole-of-nation movement to advance Singapore’s national agenda on sustainable development. In Oct 2022, Singapore announced that it would raise its national climate target to achieve net zero emissions by 2050. 
 
There is much work to be done to realise the climate aspirations of Singapore. The available mitigation options have trade-offs, and need to be considered in the context of the overall triple transition of carbon, energy and the economy. Your work will therefore revolve around carefully weighing trade-offs, whether that be in exploring new possibilities for Singapore or ensuring existing policies remain fit-for-purpose.

In this role, you will be the key commercial and contractual backbone of our project teams, ensuring that projects are delivered within budget and that all procurement and contract management activities are handled with rigour and professionalism. You will work closely with project managers, engineers, and external contractors to provide end-to-end quantity surveying and contracts services across the full project lifecycle — from early-stage cost planning through to final account settlement. This is a hands-on role that requires both technical competence and strong attention to detail, and offers the opportunity to contribute meaningfully to a diverse portfolio of development projects.

agency: Home Team Science and Technology Agency (HTX)
job_description:
HTX is the world’s first Science and Technology agency that integrates a diverse range of scientific and engineering capabilities to innovate and deliver transformative and operationally ready solutions for homeland security. As a statutory board of the Ministry of Home Affairs and integral to the Home Team, HTX works at the forefront of science and technology to empower Singapore’s frontline of security. Our shared mission is to amplify, augment and accelerate the Home Team’s advantage and secure Singapore as the safest place on planet earth.

You will be part of the AI Platform Team — a group of passionate developers and engineers who believe that DevSecOps is a culture and that embracing AI is the future. The team is charged with transforming the way HTX delivers AI-infused software, leveraging the latest technologies to enable agile software development, zero-trust security, continuous DevSecOps, and artificial intelligence.

As Head, MLOps, you will lead a team of engineers in designing, developing, and maintaining secure end-to-end DevSecOps solutions and processes. This is a hands-on technical leadership role sitting at the intersection of systems engineering, applied ML, and enterprise governance — pivotal in ensuring the security, scalability, and operational efficiency of software development and engineering workflows across HTX.

You will lead the design, architecture, and delivery of HTX's enterprise-scale AI platform, spanning the inference stack that serves in-house and open-source LLMs, and the agentic AI platform that enables officers across Home Team Departments to build, govern, and automate their own workflows. 

Through platform engineering, you will enhance developer experience and productivity while safeguarding organisational assets.

You will own the architectural direction of the AI platform, influence senior leadership on AI strategy, and build the team and partnerships needed to deliver at scale across on-prem, intranet, and air-gapped environments.

job_title: Assistant Director / Deputy Director Product Operations, DXD (MOE)
job_description:
About Government Technology Agency
The Government Technology Agency (GovTech) is the lead agency driving Singapore’s Smart Nation initiatives and public sector digital transformation. As the Centre of Excellence for Infocomm Technology and Smart Systems (ICT & SS), GovTech develops the Singapore Government’s capabilities in Data Science & Artificial Intelligence, Application Development, Smart City Technology, Digital Infrastructure, and Cybersecurity.

At GovTech, we offer you a purposeful career to make lives better. We empower our people to master their craft through continuous and robust learning and development opportunities all year round. Our GovTechies embody our Agile, Bold and Collaborative values to deliver impactful solutions. GovTech aims to transform the delivery of Government digital services by taking an "outside-in" view, putting citizens and businesses at the heart of everything we do.

Play a part in Singapore’s vision to build a Smart Nation and embark on your meaningful journey to build tech for public good. Join us to advance our mission and shape your future with us today! Learn more about GovTech at tech.gov.sg.
Role Overview
As an Assistant Director / Deputy Director of Product Operations in DXD (Digital Excellence & Products Division), you are accountable for designing, implementing, and driving adoption of product operations capabilities within a core digital vertical portfolio. This may include parents-facing applications, teacher professional learning platforms, student learning ecosystems, or national placement and registration services (e.g. OnePlacement).
You work closely with Product, Design, and Engineering leads to embed effective operating models, strengthen delivery discipline, and improve launch and operational readiness across your portfolio. You enable teams through clear processes, operational analytics on delivery and adoption, and fit-for-purpose governance.
The role requires strong operational ownership and systems thinking, using delivery and user engagement signals to drive execution, mitigate risk, and continuously improve outcomes across teams.
Target Portfolio Scope
You will be accountable for end-to-end product operations within a single assigned core digital vertical, which may be one of the following:
  • Parents-facing applications and services
  • Teacher professional learning and development platforms
  • Student Learning Space and related ecosystem products
  • National placement and registration services (e.g. OnePlacement)
Within this scope, you will partner closely with business owners (e.g. schools, HQ divisions, Academy of Singapore Teachers) and align with central DXD standards and practices.
Key Responsibilities:
Lead Product Operations Implementation
  • Design, implement, and embed product operations practices across product teams in the portfolio
  • Establish standardized processes, tools, and best practices that become the operating model for product delivery
  • Lead change management efforts to shift teams from traditional operations toward product operations practices
  • Conduct regular maturity assessments and drive continuous improvement of operational capabilities
Design and Implement Data-Driven Product Operations
  • Implement and maintain portfolio-level metrics on delivery health, launch readiness, adoption, usage, and stability
  • Build automated reporting and dashboarding systems that provide real-time visibility to product leadership
  • Implement data governance and quality assurance processes that ensure reliable, trusted insights
Establish Lightweight Governance Frameworks
  • Develop lightweight, scalable governance frameworks that provide consistent product delivery oversight across DXD
  • Establish risk management processes including pre-launch readiness assessments and mitigation tracking
  • Design quality assurance and delivery standards that reduce launch risks without creating bottlenecks
  • Implement incident management and escalation protocols that maintain accountability while supporting rapid response
Build and Lead Product Operations Capability
  • Lead, mentor, and develop product operations specialists and associates to build a strong team
  • Coach product teams on effective product operations practices
  • Support the development of product operations competency frameworks that can scale across MOE
  • Contribute to thought leadership and knowledge sharing on product operations practices
Own Launch and Change Readiness
  • Own and drive execution of launch readiness and coordination for major releases
  • Lead dependency management, timeline alignment, and risk mitigation across teams
  • Partner business owners to ensure operational readiness, communications, and adoption support
  • Conduct post-launch retrospectives and embed learnings into future cycles
Strengthen Voice of Customer and Engagement Operations
  • Implement systematic approaches to capture and act on user feedback and engagement signals
  • Translate insights into prioritised operational and delivery improvements
  • Maintain closed-loop feedback processes to track resolution and improvement over time
What Success Looks Like
  • Product teams deliver more predictably with clear visibility of risks and progress
  • Launches are smoother, with fewer last-minute issues and stronger adoption
  • Product leaders have reliable operational and adoption insights to support decisions
  • Governance enables confidence without unnecessary overhead
  • Product operations is recognised as a trusted enabler of consistent, high-quality delivery
  • User feedback and engagement signals visibly shape ongoing improvement
What we are looking for:
  • Strong Operations Leadership: Experience and proven ability to design, implement, and embed product operations within a product portfolio
  • Data-Driven Thinking: Advanced fluency in product analytics, metrics framework design, and insight generation
  • Systems Thinking: Demonstrated capability to design scalable solutions that work across complex team environments
  • Stakeholder Leadership: Strong ability to influence and align diverse stakeholders around operational improvements
  • Organizational Navigation: Comfort operating within government and public-sector constraints
  • Team Building: Passion for developing high-performing teams and fostering collaborative product operations communities
Education:
  • Bachelor's degree in Business Administration, Information Systems, Engineering, or related fields
  • Master's degree preferred in Business Administration (MBA), Information Systems, or Operations Management
Experience:
  • 6-10 years of professional experience in product operations, operations management or delivery roles
  • Demonstrated leadership experience managing operational teams or driving portfolio-wide initiatives
  • Proven track record of designing and implementing scalable processes and governance frameworks
  • Experience in product-focused, agile, or technology environments
  • Background supporting educational technology or government digital services is advantageous
Technical Competencies:
  • Intermediate / advanced proficiency in data analysis and visualization tools (SQL, Python, Tableau, or similar)
  • Strong understanding of agile methodologies, modern delivery toolchains, and product development workflows
  • Expertise in operational metrics framework design and insight generation
  • Familiarity with common product operations tools and platforms

GovTech is an equal opportunity employer committed to fostering an inclusive workplace that values diverse voices and perspectives, as we believe it is key to innovation.

Our employee benefits are based on a total rewards approach, offering a holistic and market-competitive suite of perks.

We champion flexible work arrangements (subject to your job role) and trust you to manage your time to deliver your best.

Role Summary
We are looking for a Lead Product Manager to build Ministry of Education (MOE)'s geospatial and spatial modelling capabilities to strengthen long-term infrastructure and space planning. The role focuses on developing models, tools, and a geospatial foundation that helps MOE anticipate future demand, optimise school locations, and make evidence-based planning decisions.

Key Responsibilities

  1. Build MOE’s Spatial Modelling Engine (Primary)
    • Develop spatial models that forecast future education demand based on housing growth, demographics, migration patterns, land-use plans, and accessibility.
    • Create multi-year scenarios to support decisions on new schools, mergers, campus shifts, capacity planning, and infrastructure renewal.
    • Integrate datasets and insights from other government agencies such as URA, SLA, LTA, DOS, HDB, and GovTech into MOE’s planning logic.
    • Translate planning and policy needs into clear modelling requirements.
  2. Establish MOE’s Geospatial Data & Platform Foundation (Secondary)
    • Build and maintain the authoritative geospatial data layers for MOE (school locations, travel-time layers, catchments, urban constraints, environmental layers).
    • Develop internal APIs, standards, and governance rules to ensure accurate, consistent, and accessible geospatial data.
    • Work with engineering and data teams to set up the geospatial platform that powers analytic tools and downstream products.
  3. Integrate Geospatial Capabilities into MOE Products (Secondary)
    • Embed mapping and location-intelligence features into existing MOE planning and operating tools such as School Cockpit+
    • Improve decision-making workflows using map-based interfaces and visual analytics.
    • Work with planners, policy officers, and product teams to ensure geospatial tools meet real operational needs.
What Success Looks Like
  • A working spatial-modelling engine that planners trust for long-term demand forecasting.
  • A central geospatial platform used across MOE for planning and operational decisions.
  • Planning teams have clear, data-backed insights to guide school infrastructure decisions.
  • Geospatial features are embedded meaningfully into key MOE products.
  • Strong cross-agency partnerships that improve data quality and modelling accuracy.
Candidate Profile
  • Experience developing geospatial products, planning tools, or modelling systems.
  • Familiarity with spatial data (GIS platforms such as ArcGIS, routing engines, geospatial APIs, 3D/2D mapping).
  • Able to work with complex demographics, land-use, and transport data.
  • Strong collaboration skills with planners, policymakers, engineers, and analysts.
  • Product mindset with the ability to translate planning questions into functional tools and datasets.

GovTech is seeking an experienced and strategic Talent Acquisition Team Lead to drive our recruitment efforts and build high-performing teams that will transform Singapore's digital government landscape. Establish clear performance metrics aligned with GovTech's rapid growth objectives and conduct regular development conversations to build recruitment expertise in government technology hiring.

Strategic Recruitment Planning - Partner with GovTech leadership and product teams to understand our digital transformation roadmap and translate ambitious technology initiatives into comprehensive talent acquisition strategies. Develop workforce planning for critical technology capabilities, anticipate skills gaps in emerging areas like artificial intelligence and quantum computing, and create proactive sourcing strategies for niche technical roles that support Singapore's Smart Nation vision.

End-to-End Recruitment Management - Oversee the full recruitment lifecycle for senior technology leaders, technical and corporate roles, from initial briefing through successful onboarding into GovTech's mission-driven culture. Manage relationships with hiring managers across diverse technology domains and ensure candidates understand the unique opportunity to build technology that serves 5.9 million Singaporeans. Public Sector Talent Brand Development - Champion GovTech's employer brand within Singapore's competitive technology talent market, highlighting our unique value proposition of meaningful work, cutting-edge technology, and significant societal impact. Process Optimisation & Technology - Drive continuous improvement of recruitment processes specifically tailored to government hiring requirements whilst maintaining efficiency and candidate quality. Implement data-driven approaches to measure recruitment effectiveness against GovTech's ambitious hiring targets. Champion adoption of recruitment technologies that comply with government procurement and security standards.

Proven track record of successfully recruiting across multiple functions and seniority levels. Experience with technical recruiting for a matrixed organization will be preferred.

Leadership Capabilities - Demonstrated ability to lead, motivate, and develop high-performing teams. Strong coaching and mentoring skills with experience in performance management. Excellent communication and interpersonal skills with the ability to influence at all organisational levels.

Technical Expertise - Proficiency with applicant tracking systems, recruitment databases, and sourcing tools. Strong understanding of employment legislation and best practice recruitment processes. Experience with data analysis and reporting to drive recruitment insights.

Strategic Thinking - Ability to think strategically about talent acquisition challenges and develop innovative solutions. Strong business acumen with understanding of how recruitment supports broader organisational objectives. Experience in workforce planning and talent pipeline development.

What the role is:

As a Talent Acquisition Sourcer at GovTech Singapore, you will play a pivotal role in identifying and engaging top-tier tech talent to drive our mission of building Tech for Public Good. You will be a hands-on partner, focusing on the crucial initial stages of the recruitment lifecycle, and contributing to the transformation of how GovTech attracts and secures the best minds in the industry. What we are looking for:

We are seeking a highly motivated and results-oriented individual with a passion for connecting talent with purpose.

"To mould the future of our nation"
At the Ministry of Education (MOE), we believe in nurturing every learner and shaping the future of Singapore through education. We are committed to providing learners with a balanced and well-rounded education — supported by financial stewardship that ensures every dollar invested delivers meaningful outcomes for our nation. Through prudent financial management and innovative solutions, MOE enables quality education, from maintaining world-class facilities to funding impactful learning programmes across the system.
As the Lead Product Manager for Corporate Finance, you will define and lead the product strategy, roadmap, and measurable outcomes for MOE’s central corporate finance systems. You will establish product OKRs and success metrics, drive the digital transformation of finance operations across HQ and schools, and collaborate closely with multidisciplinary teams. In this role, you will also mentor junior product managers and analysts, contributing to a high-performing, user-focused product team.

Corporate Finance Revolutionizing Education through Tech for Public Good
We play a pivotal role in shaping Singapore's future, guiding the financial heartbeat of over 350 schools, empowering 33,000 teachers, and nurturing more than half a million students. Imagine the impact of streamlining the vast and currently decentralized financial operations that underpin this monumental effort – from budgeting and procurement to billing and payments, each school independently managing a complex web of activities.
We're embarking on a digital transformation of corporate finance, not just for efficiency, but to unleash the full potential of our educators and students. By simplifying processes, standardizing operations, and leveraging cutting-edge technology, we'll slash administrative burdens and transaction costs. This will unlock powerful data analytics, enabling smarter resource allocation and maximizing impact where it matters most. Our vision is a future-ready finance system: streamlined, scalable, and built on industry best practices. This isn't just a job; it's a chance to apply tech for public good, directly shaping a brighter future for Singapore's education landscape.
Our Team
Step into a dynamic environment where financial innovation meets educational impact. Our team is made up of passionate individuals committed to solving challenges and driving meaningful change. We embrace diversity of thought, encourage experimentation, and foster a culture of continuous learning. As part of this team, you will lead and collaborate with UX designers, finance officers, and solution providers — forward-thinking professionals equally invested in your growth and in transforming MOE’s corporate finance services.

Key Responsibilities
1. Define and Drive Corporate Finance Strategy, Roadmap, and Outcomes
  • Develop and own the product vision, strategy, and multi-year roadmap aligned with MOE’s corporate finance digitalisation priorities.
  • Establish, track, and deliver on product OKRs and success metrics to drive adoption, impact, and continuous improvement.
  • Prioritise initiatives that maximise value creation and usability for both MOE HQ and schools.

2.Enhance Decision-Making and Financial Integrity through Data and Automation

  • Strengthen finance operations by embedding data analytics to support strategic planning, such as budget forecasting and expenditure tracking.
  • Redesign financial reporting through a dynamic Chart of Accounts (COA) to enable intuitive, multi-dimensional financial insights.
  • Implement machine learning models to proactively flag anomalies and reduce financial risk through early detection of high-risk transactions.

3. Coach and Develop the Corporate Finance Product Team

  • Mentor and develop junior product managers and analysts to build internal capabilities and foster a high-performance, user-focused culture.
  • Promote cross-functional collaboration and continuous learning across the finance and digital teams.
Required Qualifications and Experience
  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Data Science, Business Administration, or a related field; Master’s degree preferred.
  • Minimum of 5 years of experience in Product Management, preferably in FinTech, SaaS, or government digital services.
  • Strong track record of launching, scaling, and evolving impactful digital products.
  • Experience with agile methodologies and full product lifecycle management.
  • Excellent communication, stakeholder engagement, and influence skills.
  • Knowledge of UX/UI design principles and user-centered design.
  • Passion for public good and interest in the education sector is a strong plus.

Key Competencies

  • Product Leadership: Ability to define strategy, own outcomes, and lead multi-year product execution.
  • Data-Driven Decision-Making: Able to translate data into actionable insights and financial intelligence.
  • User-Centric Thinking: Designs with users in mind, balancing operational workflows with intuitive UX.
  • Systems Thinking: Comfortable working across a complex organisation with diverse stakeholders and processes.
  • Coaching & Team Growth: Skilled in mentoring others and building high-performing product teams.

job_title: Principal / Lead Product Manager, TradeNet, DEP
job_description:
GovTech is Singapore's lead agency for digital government and the Smart Nation initiative. We build and deploy technology that meaningfully improves lives — for citizens, businesses, and the public sector.

Digital Economy Products (DEP) is a division within GovTech that operates with the pace and rigour of a modern product company. Our teams tackle high-stakes problems across Singapore's public sectors — Healthcare, Manpower, Trade and Finance, National Development, Sustainability, and more — and build digital products that serve millions.

Many of Singapore's most critical government systems were built decades ago — pioneering for their time, but now running on aging technology, tightly bound to vendor dependencies, and struggling to keep pace with evolving policy and industry demands. We are bringing these platforms in-house and rebuilding them from the ground up — rethinking not just the technology, but the operating models, user experiences, and regulatory workflows they underpin.

Two flagship programmes are currently underway:

TradeNet — Singapore's National Single Window for trade permit processing. Launched in the late 1980s, it processes over 9 million permit applications annually across import, export, and transshipment, contributing to approximately 10% of national tax revenues. Designated as critical IT infrastructure, TradeNet is being brought in-house to secure Singapore's technology sovereignty over one of its most consequential trade systems.

CoreNet X — Singapore's national digital platform for regulatory approval of building works. It unifies eight regulatory agencies into a single BIM-based submission and processing system, transforming how the built environment sector interacts with government.

Both programmes sit at the intersection of complex regulation, enterprise-scale technology, and high public-sector impact. As a Principal Product Manager at DEP, you will be at the centre of this transformation — working at the frontier of how government systems are redesigned, rebuilt, and reimagined for the future.

Responsibilities

  • Define and own the product strategy for modernisation of your key system(s).
  • Translate complex business, technical, and policy insights into clear roadmaps that align teams and drive measurable outcomes.
  • Establish success metrics (OKRs, KPIs) and ensure alignment with the policy, business, and operational objectives.
  • Build strong stakeholder alignment through evidence, trust, and well-earned relationships — resolving complex dependencies and securing senior management support for product strategy and investments.
  • Develop deep system and domain expertise, serving as the subject-matter expert for your product(s) — bringing informed perspective to agency escalations, regulatory tradeoffs, and decisions that shape product approach across government and industry stakeholders.
  • Champion adoption of emerging technologies and industry best practices to improve team effectiveness and impact.
  • Lead and mentor a team of talented product managers, setting the bar for product thinking, execution rigour, and professional development of each team member.
  • Lead hiring of other product leaders for your team and outside, identify and coach future leaders
  • Lead end‑to‑end product execution of a major product —from ideation to launch to iteration—ensuring high standards of quality, security, accessibility, and compliance.
  • Collaborate with engineering, design, research, operations, and data teams to deliver cohesive, scalable user experiences.

Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree and 10+ years of product management or equivalent experience with strong demonstrated impact in the consumer/enterprise/platform ecosystem.
  • Track record of delivering complex products at scale with measurable business impact.
  • Excellent communication and leadership skills, capable of inspiring teams and influencing senior leaders.
  • Strong stakeholder management skills — ability to navigate complex, multi-agency environments and build alignment across government agencies, vendor partners, and industry players.
  • Strong analytical skills with the ability to leverage data to inform product decisions.
  • Proven experience hiring, managing, mentoring, and coaching a team of product managers.

Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience modernising or re-platforming complex enterprise systems, B2B applications, or legacy platforms.
  • Domain expertise in trade/customs or building/construction industries.
  • Experience in process optimisation, regulatory technology, or government digital transformation.
  • Prior engineering experience working on complex web applications or enterprise systems.

What the role is

GovTech is the lead agency driving Singapore’s Smart Nation initiatives and public sector digital transformation. As part of the Singpass team, you will be working on the bedrock of Singapore’s digital ecosystem.
Singpass enables high assurance authentication, digital signatures, and secure data sharing (Myinfo) for millions of users. Working here means building infrastructure where security, reliability, and trust are the foundation of everything we do. We don’t just write code; we solve complex problems to ensure that national digital services are seamless, secure, and resilient for every citizen.

What you will be working on

As a Software Engineer at Singpass, you won't be confined to a single layer of the stack. We view our engineers as holistic problem solvers. You will:

  • Solve End-to-End Problems: Participate in the full software development lifecycle, from brainstorming solutions with product managers and fellow engineers, to deploying and monitoring code in production.
  • Build & Prototype: Develop and iterate on features for Singpass, ensuring they meet our high standards for security and usability.
  • Write Robust Code: Craft clean, maintainable, and secure-by-design code. While we currently use Java and TypeScript, we are increasingly standardizing on Go for new services.
  • Learn Cloud Native Engineering: Manage and monitor cloud deployments (AWS/GCC) with a focus on system integrity and operational safety.
  • Collaborate Across Disciplines: Work closely with UX designers, cybersecurity specialists, and fellow engineers to understand user needs and technical constraints.

What we are looking for

We value potential and proactivity over years of experience. We are looking for candidates who:

  • Are Proactive Self-Starters: You don’t wait for a ticket to be assigned; you identify areas for improvement, ask "why," and take the initiative to learn what you don't know.
  • Are Versatile Problem Solvers: You see yourself as an engineer first, not just a frontend or backend specialist. You are willing to dive into any part of the system to fix a bug or build a feature.
  • Possess Strong Fundamentals: You have a solid grasp of computer science basics (data structures, algorithms, and networking) and a passion for writing high-quality code.
  • Have a Security-First Mindset: You understand that working on national infrastructure requires extreme attention to detail and a disciplined approach to risk.
  • Are Excellent Communicators: You can explain your logic clearly and enjoy collaborating in a team environment.

What it is like working here

  • High Impact: Your work directly affects the daily lives of millions of people in Singapore.
  • Continuous Learning: You will be mentored by senior engineers and have access to robust learning and development opportunities to master your craft.
  • Polyglot Environment: We use the best tool for the job. You’ll have the chance to work with Go, TypeScript, Java, and modern cloud technologies.
  • Mission-Driven Culture: We are "Digital to the Core, and Serve with Heart." We care deeply about the public good and the responsibility of safeguarding national digital identity.

Requirements

  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Computer Engineering, or a related field (or equivalent practical experience).
  • Experience with at least one modern programming language (e.g., Go, Java, TypeScript, Python).
  • A portfolio of projects (personal, academic, or open-source) that demonstrates your ability to build and ship software.
  • Evidence of a proactive mindset. Tell us about a time you took the lead on a project or taught yourself a complex new skill.

About Product Operations at Singpass


The Singpass team enables trusted, secure digital transactions for every citizen, resident, and
business in Singapore.
The Product Operations team sits at the intersection of product, user and partner experience,
and execution. We enable product teams to move faster and make better decisions by
improving processes, strengthening feedback loops, and building systems that scale.
As a Product Operations Specialist, you will play a key role in ensuring that products are
operationally ready, insight-driven, and continuously improving. You will work closely with
colleagues from Product, Design, Engineering, and frontline teams to translate user needs into
actionable insights and scalable solutions.
This role is ideal for someone who thrives in ambiguity, enjoys connecting dots across teams,
and is motivated by improving how products are built and delivered.


🔧 What You’ll Work On


1. Product Delivery & Operational Readiness
● Partner with cross-functional teams to ensure products and features are operationally
ready for launch
● Support rollout planning, internal enablement, and post-launch monitoring
● Identify risks and gaps early, ensuring smooth product delivery


2. Insights & Feedback Loops
● Establish and maintain structured feedback loops across user, partner, and system
touchpoints
● Synthesise data from multiple sources (e.g. support, behavioural data, user feedback,
system signals such as logs, monitoring, and operational metrics)
● Translate insights into clear recommendations that influence product decisions


3. Process & Workflow Optimisation
● Design and improve workflows that increase efficiency and reduce manual effort
● Standardise processes across teams to improve consistency and scalability
● Identify opportunities for automation and operational improvements


4. Data & Visibility
● Define and track key operational and product metrics, including system performance and
reliability indicators

[What you will be working on]

We are looking for a Design Leader to join our team and help drive the evolution of public services that matter. You'll bring strategic vision, design leadership, and organizational transformation skills to ensure we're solving the right problems and building high-performing design teams that deliver solutions with impact.

This role is ideal for someone who can drive impact through building new digital products and services, catalysing organizational change, and operating effectively at the intersection of technology, policy and operations.

What you'll do:

  • Lead design strategy and execution across an agency portfolio, ensuring excellence and impact across multiple concurrent workstreams spanning service design, product design, and design research

  • Drive discovery work to uncover root causes, user needs, and systemic barriers, bringing a rigorous approach to clearly frame problems before exploring possible solutions

  • Guide teams to understand and reimagine end-to-end user experiences across systems, processes, touch-points, and stakeholders

  • Partner with engineering leaders, product managers, policymakers and operations to bridge user needs with technical and business realities, ensuring solutions are both meaningful to users and implementable at scale

  • Build and mentor design teams across disciplines, fostering a culture of craft, collaboration and continuous improvement

  • Shape and evolve design-led frameworks for discovery, problem definition, solutioning and experimentation within your organization

  • Build design capability within your agency and contribute to the wider public sector design practice, embedding design as a strategic strength

  • Define and track measures of success to show the impact of design across product development and service delivery

[What we are looking for]

  • 10+ years designing and shipping digital solutions end-to-end — from insight, to product and service delivery, to GTM and impact

  • 5+ years leading and growing design teams, with proven success building design culture and capability across disciplines

  • Experience leading design across complex, multi-stakeholder environments (B2B, enterprise, or platform experience is particularly relevant)

  • Demonstrated ability to manage multiple concurrent projects and portfolios while maintaining quality and impact

  • Mastery across design disciplines — problem framing, research, service design, product design, prototyping and storytelling — turning insight into action and inspiring others around it

  • Comfort working at the edge of tech and design — fluent enough in APIs, system integrations, data-driven interfaces, and permissions models to co-create with engineers

  • Commitment to accessibility and inclusive design (WCAG), ensuring services work for everyone

  • Strong communicator and storyteller who builds trust quickly and brings people together at all organizational levels

  • Entrepreneurial mindset with a drive to create impact for public good

What will make you stand out:

  • Experience working in both start-up and enterprise environments, and in both in-house product teams and as an external consultant

  • Proven success launching platform-scale or low-code/no-code products that empower non-technical users to create and adapt services

  • Track record of hiring, developing and retaining design talent at scale

  • Experience building design capabilities — from running training programmes to shaping communities of practice

  • Work that shows real-world impact — whether in civic tech, public sector, or other mission-driven organizations

You'll thrive here if you:

  • Enjoy making sense of complex, ambiguous challenges across multiple domains simultaneously

  • Relish expanding your impact from individual products to entire service ecosystems and organizational capability

  • Can shift fluidly between strategic leadership, hands-on guidance, and organizational transformation

  • Are energised by embedding with a team, understanding their domain deeply, and making things happen

  • Focus on outcomes and long-term value, bringing rigorous thinking to government transformation

  • Influence through building trust, clarity, and collaboration across all levels of organization


Why this role matters
Design at GovTech is about more than interfaces. It's about shaping services that people trust and rely on, and building the foundations of how the government works in an AI-enabled society. As Design Leader, you'll define what good looks like, build the teams and capabilities needed to deliver it, and help the government grow its capacity to deliver products and services that are not only functional — but clear, inclusive, and human.

GovTech is an equal opportunity employer committed to fostering an inclusive workplace that values diverse voices and perspectives, as we believe it is key to innovation.

Our employee benefits are based on a total rewards approach, offering a holistic and market-competitive suite of perks.

We champion flexible work arrangements (subject to your job role) and trust you to manage your time to deliver your best.

What You Will Be Doing

Strategic Vision and Leadership

You will develop and articulate a compelling product vision and strategy aligned with MOM's overall goals, leading the product team to achieve this vision. You will oversee the entire product lifecycle from ideation to launch and post-launch optimisation, ensuring timely delivery of high-quality products. Working with the team, you will identify new opportunities and problems to solve for the agency's digital transformation, building deep knowledge of MOM's business and operations to support transformation initiatives.

Product Development and Roadmap Management

You will create and maintain a comprehensive product roadmap that prioritises features and enhancements based on business impact, customer feedback, and market demand. Leading the development of the digital roadmap, you will introduce appropriate digital solutions in partnership with cross-functional business teams, whole-of-government partners, and industry stakeholders to achieve greater business outcomes. You will evaluate and recommend suitable technology whilst aligning with GovTech's technology directions, including cloud-based solutions, enterprise architecture, modernisation of legacy systems, and adoption of the SG Tech Stack.

Research and Customer Advocacy

You will conduct thorough market analysis and customer research to identify needs, trends, and opportunities, utilising insights to inform product development and positioning. Partnering with MOM's business teams, you will elicit citizen, business, and agency needs through human-centric and visual methods. You will act as the voice of the customer within the organisation, ensuring that customer needs and feedback are integrated into product planning and development. Applying Design Thinking and Service Design methodology and tools, you will identify current state gaps and challenges, and develop future state design and solutions.

Delivery and Operations

You will identify and adopt the appropriate development and delivery approach, whether outsourced, co-sourced, or in-house development. Working with fellow Govtechies and MOM's teams, you will develop user stories, functional and technical specifications, and acceptance criteria. You will ensure product readiness for smooth deployment, adoption, and operations. Analysing the impact of requested changes, you will propose improvements to continuously address changing business needs and work with support teams to understand and address technical problems through effective operations management.

Performance and Innovation

You will define and track key performance indicators or OKRs to measure product success and inform decision-making, using data-driven insights to make informed adjustments to the product strategy. Fostering a culture of continuous improvement and innovation, you will stay abreast of industry trends and emerging technologies to drive product evolution. You will lead organisation-wide digital, design, and data capabilities development, digital innovation, and continuous improvement to build a high-performing team.

Collaboration and Stakeholder Management

You will work closely with engineering, design, customer support teams, and various divisions within MOM to ensure alignment and effective communication throughout the product development process. You will engage with tripartite partners, other government agencies, senior leadership, and external stakeholders to align on product goals, manage expectations, and secure buy-in for your product initiatives.

What We Are Looking For

You should have a diploma or degree in Computer Science, Infocomm Technology, Computer or Electronics Engineering, or related disciplines, with at least 5 years of experience in product management and a proven track record of managing successful products.

You possess strong leadership and team management skills, with the ability to lead and mentor product teams. Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and decision-making abilities are essential, particularly with application in discovery sessions and prototyping. You have strong analytical, conceptualisation, and facilitation skills.

Excellent communication and interpersonal skills are required, with good stakeholder engagement abilities and the capacity to influence and pitch ideas effectively. You should be comfortable navigating complex organisational structures and working with diverse stakeholders including tripartite partners.

Experience with agile methodologies and product management tools is expected. You should have working knowledge of AI tools and concepts, and familiarity with modern technology trends and their application in government services.

You must be able to work effectively in a fast-paced, dynamic environment with a driven and motivated personality. The ability to rapidly learn, apply, and execute is crucial for success in this role.

What We Offer

The opportunity to make a meaningful impact on Singapore's workforce and employers, work with talented cross-functional teams on challenging problems that affect the labour market, exposure to cutting-edge technologies and modern product practices, collaboration with tripartite partners and diverse stakeholders, and professional development opportunities including training and conferences.

job_description:
The Government Technology Agency (GovTech) is the lead agency driving Singapore’s Smart Nation initiatives and public sector digital transformation. Job Summary

GovTech is seeking a strategic and mission-driven Lead/Senior Product Manager to spearhead the AI Safety and Governance pillar within PlatformAI. As AI adoption scales across the public sector, your mission is to ensure that every AI-enabled application is secure, fair, and accountable by design.

You will own a dual-track roadmap:

  • For Developers: Building the "Safety Stack"—a suite of automated Evals, Guardrails, and Red-teaming tools that allow teams to deploy LLMs with speed and confidence.
  • For Governance and Observability: Architecting the "Governance Layer"—observability tools and risk-posture dashboards that provide a unified view of AI compliance across the whole-of-government (WOG) ecosystem.

Key Responsibilities

1. Defining the Sovereign Safety Standard

  • Define the long-term strategy for AI Safety-as-a-Service, transitioning governance from a manual checklist to an automated platform feature.
  • Synthesize emerging global frameworks and local AI governance policies into platform features that make compliance "invisible" for developers.
  • Establish the government’s standard for AI risk levels, determining the safety benchmarks required for different use cases (e.g., citizen-facing vs. internal productivity).

2. Building the Guardrails & Audit Trails

  • Lead the development of automated evaluation engines that stress-test products and models for prompt injection, PII leakage, toxicity, and hallucination before and after deployment.
  • Partner with Engineering to integrate safety checks directly into CI/CD pipelines, ensuring that "Safety-by-Design" accelerates rather than hinders deployment.
  • Build real-time monitoring tools to detect "jailbreak" attempts and model drift, ensuring rapid mitigation of live AI risks.

3. Bridging Policy & Code

  • Act as the primary liaison between Policy/Legal teams and Engineering teams to ensure technical tools meet policy and legal intent.
  • Cultivate a culture of safety across GovTech by publishing playbooks, standardizing red-teaming exercises, and educating teams on the evolving LLM vulnerability landscape.

4. Trust-Centric Metrics & Accountability

  • Define and track KPIs such as Mean Time to Detect (MTTD) vulnerabilities, Policy Coverage %, and Guardrail Efficacy Rates.

What You Will Bring

  • Senior Product Leadership: Proven experience managing complex technical products, specifically those focused on Security, Governance, or AI/ML infrastructure.
  • AI Safety Domain Expertise: Deep understanding of Generative AI risks (e.g., prompt injection, training data poisoning, algorithmic bias) and the current state of AI safety research.
  • The "Translator" Skillset: Exceptional ability to distil abstract ethical principles into concrete technical requirements and APIs.
  • Analytical Rigor: A background in using data-driven evaluation frameworks (e.g., RAG evaluation, perplexity scoring, or model benchmarking) to measure performance and risk.
  • Developer Empathy: A deep understanding of developer workflows and a commitment to making safety tools an "accelerant" rather than a "roadblock."

What we offer you:

GovTech is an equal opportunity employer committed to fostering an inclusive workplace that values diverse voices and perspectives, as we believe that diversity is the foundation to innovation.   

Our employee benefits are based on a total rewards approach, offering a holistic and market-competitive suite of perks. These include leave benefits to meet your work-life needs and employee wellness programs.

You will lead the "AI Uplift," setting the standards for how the government builds, deploys, and trusts artificial intelligence.

  • Drive the end-to-end product lifecycle for the most complex initiatives within the programme, from initial discovery and "messy" problem-solving to large-scale deployment.
  • Balance the "Trust Deficit" in AI by making critical calls on model accuracy, hallucination management, and operational readiness for critical government tasks.

3. Agency Engagement & Discovery

  • Translate broad operational challenges into concrete technical requirements for engineering teams, ensuring solutions are operationally compliant and "effortless to use."

  • Establish "Best-in-Class" AI Product Management frameworks for GovTech, including success metrics focused on time-saved, friction reduction, and accuracy.

This role goes beyond implementation — we’re looking for an engineer who can collaborate with designers, product managers, and legal officers to deliver resilient, maintainable, and usable systems that will serve Singapore’s legal infrastructure for years to come.

  • Legal tools development
    Build standalone tools for legal research, drafting, and advisory tasks
  • Structured document format migration
    Migrate legacy legal documents from a proprietary XML schema to the open Akoma Ntoso (AKN) standard to enable structured, machine-readable legal content.

job_description:
The Government Technology Agency (GovTech) is the lead agency driving Singapore’s Smart Nation initiatives and public sector digital transformation. [Job Summary]
GovTech is looking for a Lead/Senior Localization Product Manager to spearhead the adaptation and deployment of our AI Programme products across the diverse technological landscape of the public sector. While our core mission is to transition the government to an "AI-augmented" state, the reality of public service involves a complex web of varying data classifications and disparate technology stacks.


In this role, you are responsible for ensuring that our high-impact AI products are not just "built," but are successfully localized and integrated into environments ranging from commercial cloud to highly secured, air-gapped government infrastructures. You will set the gold standard for how AI remains performant and compliant, regardless of the stack it sits on.


[Key Responsibilities]

1. Strategic Adaptation & Cross-Stack Vision

  • Develop a multi-year product roadmap for the localization and porting of AI products across different government technology stacks (e.g., GCC, private clouds, and on-premise environments).
  • Define the strategy for "Data-Centric Localization," ensuring AI capabilities are optimized to handle different data classification tiers (e.g. Restricted, Confidential, Secret) without compromising product performance.

2. Product Execution & Technical Governance

  • Lead the end-to-end lifecycle for adapting complex AI products into restricted environments, solving for "messy" infrastructure dependencies and network constraints.
  • Manage the "Compliance vs. Capability" balance: Make critical product decisions on how to maintain product performance (i.e accuracy, latency) within hardware-constrained or secure-siloed environments.
  • Oversee the deployment of modular AI capabilities (Generative AI, Agentic workflows) as portable "Government-as-a-Platform" components.

3. Localization & Adaptation

  • Partner with technical leads to map out Government unique technology stacks and security requirements, identifying barriers to AI adoption.
  • Translate high-level AI product features into specific technical "localization requirements" for engineering teams (e.g., containerization needs, local model hosting, and offline processing).
  • Ensure that AI interventions remain "effortless to use" even when deployed within high-security, high-friction environments.

4. Mentorship & Product Localization Excellence

  • Mentor Product Managers on the nuances of product-market fit within government silos, fostering a culture of "Infrastructure-Aware Design."
  • Establish frameworks for "Best-in-Class" AI Localization, including metrics for deployment velocity across stacks and performance parity between open and restricted environments.

[What You Will Bring]

  • Strategic Depth: 10+ years of experience in Product Management, specifically leading the deployment of platform products or enterprise software across heterogeneous IT environments.
  • Technical Fluency: A strong grasp of AI/ML architectures (LLMs, RAG, Agentic workflows) coupled with a deep understanding of DevOps and Infrastructure (Kubernetes, Hybrid Cloud, Data Residency requirements).
  • Systems Thinking: The ability to visualize how a single AI product can be decomposed and re-assembled to fit different security enclaves without losing its "multiplier effect."
  • Stakeholder Influence: Exceptional ability to navigate conversations between "Innovation-focused" AI researchers and "Security-focused" infrastructure architects to find a path to deployment.
  • Mission Focus: A passion for solving the unique challenges of the public sector, where data security and citizen impact are the ultimate success metrics

[What you will be working on]

As a software engineer, you will:

  • Build prototypes to demonstrate technological opportunities
  • Design system architectures
  • Write production quality code
  • Manage deployments to cloud infrastructure
  • Maintain systems to a high level of reliability

Because our team focuses on pushing new initiatives, you will also have to:

  • Identify potential projects that improve the public good
  • Design novel systems that work around bureaucratic constraints
  • Advocate and explain these technical ideas to other government agencies

We are also looking for experienced software engineers! In addition to the above, more experienced software engineers will be expected to:

  • Establish best practices for developer operations
  • Provide technical leadership across multiple product teams
  • Share your expertise and mentor other engineers
  • Help with recruiting

You're not just here to write code, but also to figure out what we should be building and how we should build it. You will work on meaningful projects that solve problems pertinent to our society. The public sector is full of opportunities where even the simplest software can have a big impact on people’s lives. Rather than work on commercial ventures commonly found in the startup scene, we're here to improve how we live as a society through what we can offer as a government.

Your job will be to bring expertise and capability to the public sector. Sometimes this means coding new systems from scratch. Other times this means using the best solutions the development community has to offer. We use cloud services, open-source software, and commodity hardware as far as possible. These often include, but are not limited to: GitHub, AWS, React Native, MongoDB, and Stripe amongst others. Knowing what to build and what to reuse lets us avoid wasting time on solved problems and focus on delivering actual value.

What it is like working here:

  • Rapid Prototyping - Instead of spending too much time debating ideas, we prefer testing them. This identifies potential problems quickly, and more importantly, conveys what is possible to others easily.
  • Ownership - In addition to technical responsibilities, this means having opinions on what is being done and having ideas on what should be done next. Building something that you believe in is the best way to build something good.
  • Continuous Learning - Working on new ideas often means not fully understanding what you are working on. Taking time to learn new architectures, frameworks, technologies, and even languages is not just encouraged but essential.

[What we are looking for]

  • Have a demonstrated ability to build software - Prior work and personal projects count for much more than specific degrees.
  • Write code to solve abstract programming problems - Clear thinking, the ability to debug, and understanding the limits of an approach matter much more than getting the “correct” answer.
  • Talk and reason about code with other engineers - We work as a team, so you need to be able to not just solve problems, but have other people understand your solutions.
  • Take initiative to make things happen - Our job is to push for change in government, so we need to challenge the status quo and not wait for instruction.
  • Want to work for the public good - We are public servants; we serve the public. This sometimes means ignoring political pressures and misguided policies. We need people who will push back if something is not in public interest.

Overall, we look for people who have ability, initiative, good communication, and strong values.

A product manager to manage an analytics and AI system for Singapore Customs.

Job Summary
As a Product Manager at GovTech, you will play a pivotal role in transforming government services through data-driven insights. You will bridge the gap between business needs and technical solutions, leveraging data analytics to identify opportunities for improvement, optimize processes, and enhance decision-making across various government initiatives.

Job Responsibilities

  • Collaborate with various government agencies and stakeholders to understand their business processes, challenges, and data requirements.
  • Translate complex business problems into clear, actionable data analytics requirements and user stories.
  • Perform in-depth data analysis using various analytical tools and techniques to identify trends, patterns, and insights from large datasets.
  • Develop and implement data visualization dashboards and reports to communicate key findings and recommendations to both technical and non-technical audiences.
  • Evaluate the effectiveness of digital services and initiatives using data-driven metrics and provide recommendations for optimization.
  • Work closely with data engineers, data scientists, and developers to ensure the successful implementation of data solutions.
  • Conduct stakeholder interviews, workshops, and user acceptance testing (UAT) to validate solutions and ensure they meet business needs.
  • Stay abreast of industry best practices in data analytics, business intelligence, and digital government.
  • Contribute to the continuous improvement of GovTech's data analytics capabilities and frameworks.

Job Qualifications

  • Bachelor's degree in Information Systems, Computer Science, Business Analytics, Statistics, Economics, or a related quantitative field.
  • Minimum of 3 years of experience as a Product Manager, with a strong focus on data analytics projects.
  • Proficiency in data manipulation and analysis tools (e.g., SQL, Python, R).
  • Experience with data visualization tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI, QlikView).
  • Strong understanding of data warehousing concepts, ETL processes, and data modeling.
  • Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and critical thinking skills.
  • Ability to effectively communicate complex technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders.
  • Proven ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced environment.
  • Experience in the public sector or with government digital services projects is a plus.
  • Familiarity with agile methodologies is advantageous.

As a product manager, you will also have to plan and manage product budget, schedule, backlogs, vendors and stakeholders.

The job scope include

  • Overall incharge of the product development
  • writing paper for funding
  • drafting contracts document, calling tender when required and conducting tender evaluation with a group of appointed working group
  • overall incharge of managing security implementation and ensuring governance in compliance with the government and the agency's security policies and guidelines.
  • overall incharge of any audits conducted on the product that include the ICT implementation and security controls, funding and procurement aspect.
  • organising meetings
  • manage contracts and cntractors
  • scan market for latest technology to scale the product
  • liaise with product vendors/ principals including inviting suitable product vendors to demonstrate and present their product to product owners and stakeholders, plan adoption of the product after obtaining endorsement from stakeholders
  • work with a team of data engineers, software engineer and sdata analysts to ensure product implementation and scaling
  • manage budget and schedule to meet the target

We are seeking a Data Engineer to join the team at Enterprise Singapore.


You will be responsible for designing, building, and maintaining reliable data pipelines and curated datasets to support analytics and data science use cases.
The role ensures data quality, accessibility, reliability, and maintainability across multiple data sources and platforms.
The role works closely with analysts, data scientists, product managers and product owners to enable effective use of data for business and operational needs.

What you will be working on

  • Collaboration with cross functional teams to translate data requirements into technical solutions and support data usage needs.
  • Collaborate with government agency’s IT teams on technology stack, infrastructure and security alignment.
  • Architect and build ingestion pipelines to collect, clean, merge, and harmonise data from different source systems.
  • Day-to-day monitoring of databases and ETL systems, e.g., database capacity planning and maintenance, monitoring, and performance tuning; diagnose issues and deploy measures to prevent recurrence; ensure maximum database uptime.
  • Construction, testing, and maintenance of reusable data models and data structures to support downstream analytics and data science workloads.
  • Design and build secure mechanisms for end users and systems to access data in data warehouse.
  • Research, propose and develop new technologies and processes to improve agency data infrastructure.
  • Collaborate with data stewards to establish and enforce data governance policies, best practices and procedures.
  • Maintain data catalogue to document data pipelines, datasets, metadata, and dependencies.
  • Execution of data quality checks, validation processes, and basic reconciliation to ensure data accuracy and consistency.
  • Implement and enforce data security best practices, including access control, encryption, and data masking, to safeguard sensitive data.
  • Monitoring and troubleshooting of data pipeline performance, reliability, and operational issues.
  • Strong attention to data accuracy, reliability, maintainability, and operational robustness.

What we are looking for

  • Deep understanding of system design, data structure and algorithms, data modelling, data access, and data storage.
  • Demonstrated ability in using cloud technologies such as AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud.
  • Experience in architecting data and IT systems.
  • Experience with distributed data technologies such as Spark, Hadoop.
  • Proficiency in programming languages such as Python, Java, or Scala.
  • Proficiency in writing SQL for databases.
  • Familiarity with building and using CI/CD pipelines.
  • Familiarity with DevOps tools such as Docker, Git, Terraform.
  • Sound understanding of ETL and ELT concepts, data modeling principles, and data warehousing practices.
  • Ability to collaborate effectively with analysts, data scientists, product managers and product owners.

Preferred requirements

  • Bachelor’s Degree, preferably in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Data Engineering, Information Technology, or related disciplines.
  • Experience in designing, building, and maintaining batch and real-time data pipelines.
  • Experience with Databricks.
  • Experience with implementing technical processes to enforce data security, data quality, and data governance.
  • Familiarity with government systems and government's policies relating to data governance, data management, data infrastructure, and data security.
  • Proficiency in SQL and experience with relational databases.
  • Familiarity with cloud-based data platforms and services.
  • Familiarity with ETL and ELT tools, orchestration frameworks, and data pipeline monitoring practices.
  • Familiarity with version control systems and CI and CD practices.

As a Design Manager, you will play a crucial role in driving digital transformation and modernising early childhood development services through service design and design excellence. You will be responsible for leading modernisation initiatives that redesign service delivery for families, educators, and early childhood professionals, while setting design standards across multiple digital products and platforms across Ministry of Social and Family Development (MSF).

You will manage and lead designer(s) under your care to deliver transformative design solutions that align with Early Childhood Development Agency's (ECDA) modernisation objectives. Your leadership will be instrumental in reimagining how early childhood services are delivered digitally, ensuring accessible, efficient, and user-centred experiences that meet the evolving needs of Singapore's families and educators.

As an Assistant Director in Design Management, you will:

  • Define and drive service design strategy supporting ECDA's digital transformation and service modernisation goals. Champion innovative design approaches to enhance early childhood service delivery.
  • Influence and educate key decision makers, early childhood professionals, and policy stakeholders on modern design principles and digital transformation opportunities. Advocate for user-centred approaches that modernise traditional service touchpoints.
  • Partner with product managers, developers, policy teams, and early childhood stakeholders to ensure unified delivery of modernised digital services. Ensure service design is prominently featured throughout the modernisation process.
  • Work with functional leads to plan product roadmaps that prioritise modernisation initiatives. Scope required effort for digital transformation projects and plan work with your design team to maximise impact on service modernisation outcomes.
  • Maintain oversight over design work to ensure alignment with ECDA's modernisation objectives and adherence to government digital service standards. Drive adoption of modern design systems and usability guidelines across all digital touchpoints.
  • Manage design work, ensuring external partners deliver solutions that advance ECDA's modernisation agenda while meeting established design and usability standards.
  • Propose, establish and track metrics that measure both product performance and modernisation success together with the Product team, including digital adoption rates, service efficiency improvements, and user satisfaction with modernised services.
  • Be prepared to work directly on design deliverables for critical modernisation projects, demonstrating best practices and innovative approaches to digital service transformation.
  • Lead and mentor your designers in modern design practices, emerging technologies, and government digital transformation principles. Ensure continuous learning and growth while driving exceptional quality.
  • Plan career paths for your designers that include expertise in digital transformation, modern government service design, and innovative user experience practices relevant to early childhood services.

[What we are looking for]

  • 10+ years designing and shipping digital solutions end-to-end — from insight, to product and service delivery, to GTM and impact
  • 5+ years leading and growing design teams, with proven success building design culture and capability across disciplines
  • Experience leading design across complex, multi-stakeholder environments (B2B, enterprise, or platform experience is particularly relevant)
  • Demonstrated ability to manage multiple concurrent projects and portfolios while maintaining quality and impact
  • Mastery across design disciplines — problem framing, research, service design, product design, prototyping and storytelling — turning insight into action and inspiring others around it
  • Comfort working at the edge of tech and design — fluent enough in APIs, system integrations, data-driven interfaces, and permissions models to co-create with engineers
  • Commitment to accessibility and inclusive design (WCAG), ensuring services work for everyone
  • Strong communicator and storyteller who builds trust quickly and brings people together at all organizational levels
  • Entrepreneurial mindset with a drive to create impact for public good

Pilot new initiatives in your work

Actively seek out new ways and methods to improve your fellow designers’ efficiency, effectiveness and craft

Actively contribute to design learning and sharing sessions
