TradeNet is Singapore’s key platform that enables businesses to import, export, and move goods through one of the world’s busiest ports,currently supporting 970 Billion in annual trade revenue..   • Proven experience designing and shipping products with complex, ideally within multi-stakeholder or regulated environments

At Accounting and Corporate Regulatory Authority (ACRA), we are always on the lookout for high performing individuals who share our aspiration of making Singapore the best place for business.
We are seeking a dynamic and strategic Lead / Principal Product Manager to lead our product team. This individual will play a pivotal role in shaping the vision, strategy, and execution of ACRA product portfolio. What you will be doing

  • Strategic Vision and Leadership: Develop and articulate a compelling product vision and strategy aligned with the agency’s overall goals. Lead the product team to achieve this vision.

What we are looking for

  • Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and decision-making abilities.

Product Managers at ACRA ensure our digital services effectively serve the business community while meeting regulatory requirements. At a high level, they shape and drive the product vision for assigned digital services, translating business and user needs into actionable development plans. On a day-to-day basis, they orchestrate product development by balancing user experience, technical feasibility, and business objectives - working closely with designers, developers, and stakeholders to deliver high-quality digital solutions.

The role involves gathering user requirements, prioritizing features, managing product backlogs, and coordinating with cross-functional teams to ensure timely delivery. Product Managers also analyze product metrics, gather user feedback, and continuously iterate on solutions to enhance service delivery. They serve as the bridge between technical teams and business stakeholders, ensuring that our digital services remain responsive to user needs while meeting regulatory standards.

What you will be working on

  • You will be involved in planning and executing the full product lifecycle including the following:
  • Crafting Custom Experiences for Each User Journey
  • Engage the agency Product Owner to establish a clear vision for what the product should be and what is needed to get there
  • Collaborate with business partners to define the product vision and mission and obtain key stakeholder buy-in
  • Work out the product outreach and promotion strategies with business partners
  • Define the product go-to-market strategy and work with relevant partners to execute the plan
  • Designing, Testing, and Iterating Through Experiments
  • Design, test, and iterate through experiments to improve the user journey
  • Manage the product budget through expenditure monitoring, cashflow management, and securing funding
  • Develop the workplan with the product team
  • Provide sufficient resources for product development, including procurement of software, hardware, and ICT professional services
  • Monitoring, Capturing, and Studying User Behaviour
  • Conduct user studies to understand their needs and problems
  • Analyse data to uncover usage patterns that can inform product decisions
  • Research the industry to understand complimentary and competing solutions
  • Collecting, Analyzing, and Presenting User Interest
  • Translate the product strategy into technical, detailed, actionable plans for business and vendor teams
  • Understand the developers’ needs and if necessary support them in exploring emerging tools and techniques that can meet their needs
  • Analysing and Studying User Segments
  • Formulate strong proposals by evaluating the pros and cons of different solutions and provide compelling justifications for them
  • Consider how the services could be delivered for different market segments and prioritize feature development
  • Creating Data-Driven Analytics and Insights
  • Create data-driven analytics and insights for action and to make appropriate trade-offs
  • Coordinate efforts across multiple functions including marketing, legal, and finance
  • Co-create with the Product Owner to draft the budget papers to seek resources for the product's development
  • Driving User Growth
  • Drive user adoption and growth through increased engagement, expanding types of features, and higher usage in a financially sustainable manner
  • Maintain a wide network with key user agencies and central government product teams to seek opportunities for product collaborations
  • Participate in public-speaking engagements to raise awareness of the product and gain more users
  • Keeping Things Organized
  • Run efficient meetings to keep stakeholders and product team members updated
  • Prioritize, plan, and track what needs to be done
  • Document key information and decisions about the product
  • Be responsible for securing funding and monitoring expenses
  • Compliance and Governance
  • Ensure that Product meets security and performance requirements (SSAT, VAPT, Performance Testing)
  • Adhere to compliance requirements and audits (IM, AGO, QMS, etc.)

What we are looking for

  • Diploma/Degree in Computer Science, Infocomm Technology, Engineering or related subject area with minimum 5 years of relevant experience. Candidates with more years of experience will be considered for more senior roles
  • Prior working experience in product management, IT business analysis, application solutioning design and testing
  • Experience in modern product development/management technologies and practices such as Agile, Cloud, Design Thinking, and Product-Centric Development will be strongly preferred.
  • Working directly with engineers, designers and other product managers (ideally at least 3 years).
  • Strong analytical, conceptualisation and problem-solving skills
  • Good communication skills with the ability to pitch ideas and influence
  • A driven and motivated personality with an inquisitive mind
  • Ability to learn and apply
  • Relevant certifications such as CITPM, COMIT, PMP, and Certified Scrum Master.

As the Senior Product Manager in GovTech, you will assist the Project Director and work with the product team to revamp the whole of government (WOG) finance and accounting system and to drive innovative digital product throughout their life cycles, so that government agencies can successfully deliver their digital agenda.

Product Managers make sure that the right things get done so that our digital products meet user needs and continuously remain relevant. At a high level, this means establishing a clear vision for what a product should be and what is needed to get there. On a day-to-day basis, this means handling all the different facets of a product and making sure they are coordinated – across user needs, technical constraints, and design goals.

Success in the role will be proven by your ability to thrive in a dynamic and multifaceted environment, while keeping yourself up to date on new technology developments.

What you will be working on

You are a hands-on Senior Product Manager and will be involved in planning and executing the full product lifecycle including the following:

1. Crafting Customer Experiences for Each User Journey
• Engage the agency Product Owner to establish a clear vision for what the product should be and what is needed to get there
• Collaborate with business partners to define the product vision, strategy, and roadmap, as well as obtain key stakeholder buy-in
• Work out the product outreach and promotion strategies with business partners
• Define the product go-to-market strategy and work with relevant partners to execute the plan

2. Designing, Testing, and Iterating Through Experiments
• Design, test, and iterate through experiments to improve the user journey
• Execute hands-on system configuration when required, including developing proof-of-concept (POC) to validate new features
• Manage the product budget through expenditure monitoring, cashflow management, and securing funding
• Develop the workplan and ensure successful delivery by the product team / external vendors
• Provide sufficient resources for product development, including procurement of software, hardware, and ICT professional services

3. Monitoring, Capturing, and Studying User Behaviour
• Conduct user studies to understand their needs and problems
• Analyse data to uncover usage patterns that can inform product decisions
• Research the industry to understand complimentary and competing solutions

4. Collecting, Analyzing, and Presenting User Interest
• Translate the product strategy into technical, detailed, actionable plans for business and vendor teams
• Understand the developers’ needs and if necessary support them in exploring emerging tools and techniques that can meet their needs

5. Analysing and Studying User Segments
• Formulate strong proposals by evaluating the pros and cons of different solutions and provide compelling justifications for them
• Consider how the services could be delivered for different market segments and prioritize feature development

6. Creating Data-Driven Analytics and Insights
• Create data-driven analytics and insights for action and to make appropriate trade-offs
• Coordinate efforts across multiple functions including marketing, legal, and finance
• Co-create with the Product Owner to draft the budget papers to seek resources for the product's development

7. Driving User Growth
• Drive user adoption and growth through increased engagement, expanding types of features, and higher usage in a financially sustainable manner
• Maintain a wide network with key user agencies and central government product teams to seek opportunities for product collaborations
• Participate in public-speaking engagements to raise awareness of the product and gain more users

8. Keeping Things Organized
• Run efficient meetings to keep stakeholders and product team members updated
• Prioritize, plan, and track what needs to be done
• Document key information and decisions about the product
• Be responsible for securing funding and monitoring expenses

9. Compliance and Governance
• Ensure that Product meets security and performance requirements (SSAT, VAPT, Performance Testing)
• Adhere to compliance requirements and audits (IM, AGO, QMS, etc.)

What we are looking for

  • Degree in Computer Science or Infocomm Technology or Electronics Engineering or related discipline with a minimum 5 years of relevant ICT experience.
  • Experienced in large scale projects involving multiple government agencies / organisational units
  • Candidates with working experience in implementing SaaS ERP or finance and accounting systems with development budget exceeding $5m
  • Prior working experience in product management, IT business analysis, application solutioning design and testing
  • Experience in modern product development/management technologies and practices such as Agile, Cloud, Design Thinking, and Product-Centric Development will be strongly preferred.
  • Working directly with engineers, designers and other product managers (ideally at least 3 years).
  • Strong analytical, conceptualisation and problem-solving skills
  • Good communication skills with the ability to pitch ideas and influence
  • A driven and motivated personality with an inquisitive mind
  • Ability to learn and apply
  • Relevant certifications such as CITPM, COMIT, PMP, and Certified Scrum Master.

Who We Are

The Building and Construction Authority (BCA) is at the forefront of transforming Singapore's built environment. We ensure building safety, champion sustainable construction, and develop industry capabilities through comprehensive regulatory frameworks and innovative programmes.

Our digital transformation initiatives are reshaping the construction sector through:

  • CORENET X - Next-generation building plan submission and approval platform
  • Building Information Modelling (BIM) and Integrated Digital Delivery (IDD)
  • Smart Facilities Management solutions
  • Construction Industry Transformation Map (ITM) digital initiatives

We're seeking a strategic product leader to drive digital transformation in Singapore's construction sector. You'll lead platforms that are the backbone of Singapore's built environment industry - from how construction firms get certified to operate, to how building materials enter the country, to how billions in industry payments flow. You believe in shared success and collaborate openly across functions, domains, and agencies. You take ownership of outcomes and hold the team to a high standard of quality, security, and delivery.

Why Join us

  • Lead digital transformation in a major sector of Singapore's economy
  • Work on platforms used by every construction firm in Singapore

Essential Qualities

  • Ability to balance strategic thinking with hands-on execution
  • Customer-obsessed with strong user empathy
  • Data-driven decision-making approach
  • Comfortable with ambiguity and rapid change

  • Organized, reliable, and proactive with strong attention to detail
  • Open to feedback and growth, and eager to learn from senior team members

Good to Have

The Ministry of Manpower (MOM) drives Singapore’s mission to develop a empowered workforce and thriving workplaces. We shape policies and deliver digital solutions that uphold fair employment, safeguard the well-being of workers, and enable businesses to operate responsibly and productively. As a key enabler of MOM’s digital transformation, our division partners closely with policy, operations, and enforcement teams to modernise regulatory systems, enhance service delivery, and create digital experiences that are intuitive, reliable, and citizen‑centric.

At MOM, you will contribute to meaningful public outcomes that impact millions of workers, employers and businesses. We empower our product teams to solve complex regulatory and operational challenges with technology, data, and design. Join us to build products that strengthen Singapore’s labour market, protect vulnerable groups, and support safe and progressive workplaces for all.

Key Responsibilities

Product Strategy & Development

  • Shape the product vision and strategy for MOM’s digital platforms, ensuring strong alignment with regulatory intent, policy outcomes, and operational (Policy-Ops-Tech) needs across departments.
  • Translate policies, legislative requirements, and service workflows into clear product specifications and user stories that drive meaningful digital transformation.
  • Lead discovery and problem‑framing sessions with policy owners, operations teams, inspectors, enforcement units, and external stakeholders (e.g., employers, employment agencies, workers) to understand ground challenges and surface opportunities for redesign.
  • Evaluate product ideas through evidence‑based approaches, user research, and data insights to ensure solutions are practical, scalable, and citizen‑centric.

Product Management

  • Own and manage the end‑to‑end product lifecycle across multiple MOM platforms (e.g., Policy, Service Delivery, Enforcement, Organisational, etc).
  • Prioritise features across complex stakeholder groups based on regulatory impact, user benefits, feasibility, operational constraints, and cross‑agency dependencies.
  • Partner closely with engineering teams, policy divisions, and operations to ensure timely, high‑quality product delivery.
  • Define and track success metrics that reflect MOM’s policy intent, service quality, compliance outcomes, and user experience.

Technical Collaboration

  • Work with engineering, cybersecurity, architecture, and data teams to design solutions that are scalable, secure, and compliant with government standards.
  • Guide integration approaches with cross‑agency systems (e.g., IRAS, ICA, CPF Board, BCA) and MOM internal platforms.
  • Balance technical debt remediation with new feature development to ensure long‑term product sustainability.
  • Work closely with engineering leads on technical feasibility and architecture decisions
  • Guide integration approaches between regulatory, payment, and enterprise systems
  • Ensure scalable solutions that meet government security and compliance standards
  • Balance new feature development with technical sustainability

User Experience & Design

  • Champion user-centric design for products serving diverse user groups such as employers, migrant workers, employment agencies, inspectors, and internal MOM officers.
  • Simplify complex regulatory processes into intuitive, efficient digital services that reduce friction and enhance service delivery.
  • Use data, behavioural insights, and feedback loops to refine user journeys and continuously improve experience across web, mobile, and officer-facing systems.

Team Leadership

  • Lead and mentor a team of product managers working across multiple product lines.
  • Establish product culture and best practices, ensuring consistent standards in product discovery, experimentation, documentation, and delivery.
  • Drive capability development across product teams, strengthening MOM’s internal digital product competencies.

Stakeholder Management

  • Partner with policy groups, statutory departments, enforcement teams, and external stakeholders to ensure product decisions support MOM’s broader mission of fair, progressive, and safe workplaces.
  • Manage expectations and build consensus among senior leadership, operational managers, and public sector partners.
  • Lead change management activities to drive adoption of new digital processes across MOM and industry stakeholders.
  • Communicate product vision, priorities, and progress clearly to both technical and non‑technical audiences.

Good to Have

  • Government sector experience
  • Digital transformation background
  • Enterprise architecture expertise

What we are looking for:

  • 12+ years of product management leadership, with a track record shipping mission‑critical, enterprise platforms in domains with regulatory, compliance, or high‑stakes operational requirements (public sector or adjacent industries preferred).
  • Policy‑to‑product translation: demonstrated ability to interpret legislative intent, policy objectives, and service workflows, and convert them into clear product strategies, roadmaps, and user stories that deliver measurable outcomes.
  • Multi‑stakeholder leadership across policy divisions, operations, enforcement, service delivery, and technology—able to balance regulatory impact, user benefits, feasibility, and cross‑agency dependencies in prioritisation and trade‑offs.
  • Agile at scale: expertise in discovery and delivery, backlog management, outcome‑oriented OKRs, and evidence‑based decision‑making using product analytics, research, and experimentation.
  • Technical acumen to partner credibly with engineering, architecture, cybersecurity, and data teams—familiar with enterprise integration patterns (APIs/event‑driven), data governance, security and privacy by design, and long‑term product sustainability (tech debt vs. new value).
  • Data‑driven mindset, defining and tracking north‑star and operational metrics that matter to MOM (e.g., service quality, time‑to‑serve, adoption, compliance outcomes, reduction in friction, and productivity uplift).
  • User‑centred design leadership across diverse user groups (employers, migrant workers, employment agencies, inspectors, and internal officers), with a track record simplifying complex regulatory journeys into intuitive digital experiences.
  • Team leadership and coaching: experience building and mentoring product managers and cross‑functional squads; establishing product standards and practices; uplifting product capabilities across a portfolio.
  • Stakeholder influence and communication: able to align senior leadership, guide change management, and communicate product vision and trade‑offs clearly to both technical and non‑technical audiences.
  • Nice to have / domain strengths: familiarity with MOM‑relevant areas such as work pass and regulatory systems, employment standards, workplace safety and health, inspections/case management, and service‑ops transformation; experience with cross‑agency integrations (e.g., ICA, CPF Board, IRAS); exposure to payments or licensing platforms; enterprise architecture background.

[About the Role]

We are seeking a Senior Product Manager to own and lead high‑impact, data‑centric products that support Whole‑of‑Government policy, operational and decision‑making outcomes. The role carries end‑to‑end accountability for product vision, prioritisation and outcomes, working with senior stakeholders across agencies to ensure products deliver sustained value at scale.

As a Senior Product Manager, you will be responsible for the end‑to‑end success of strategic products where data is a primary value driver, such as enterprise data platforms, authoritative datasets and shared analytics products. You will shape the product vision and roadmap, lead problem discovery to ensure the right problems are being solved, and guide delivery through multi‑disciplinary teams.

You will work closely with senior stakeholders across agencies to balance policy intent, operational needs, user outcomes, governance requirements and delivery constraints. This is a Senior Product Manager role, not a separate data‑specialist position, and requires strong product leadership complemented by sufficient data literacy to operate effectively in complex, cross‑agency environments.

[Key Responsibilities]

Product Strategy & Ownership

  • Own and communicate a clear product vision, outcomes and roadmap for data‑centric products.
  • Lead problem discovery to ensure the right user, agency and Whole‑of‑Government problems are being solved before solutioning, rather than digitising existing issues.
  • Translate policy, operational and analytical needs into actionable product strategies and priorities.
  • Define and track product value, focusing on impact such as better decisions, improved timeliness, reuse of data and productivity gains.
  • Ensure product scope remains outcome‑driven, avoiding unnecessary complexity or unsustainable solutions.

Stakeholder Management

  • Act as the single accountable owner for product outcomes across multiple agencies and senior stakeholders.
  • Engage policy owners, data owners, business users, analytics teams, technologists and vendors to align priorities and delivery expectations.
  • Make informed trade‑offs across cost, quality, risk, timeliness and impact.

Delivery Leadership

  • Lead cross‑functional product teams spanning data engineering, analytics, architecture, security and operations.
  • Drive product‑centric delivery practices, including iterative releases, user feedback loops and outcome‑based prioritisation.
  • Oversee vendor‑supported delivery while maintaining strong internal product ownership and capability transfer.

Governance, Risk & Compliance

  • Ensure products meet government requirements for data governance, security, privacy, auditability and operational sustainability.
  • Work closely with governance, security and architecture stakeholders to embed controls by design.
  • Present product plans, costs, risks and progress clearly to senior governance forums.

People & Capability Development

  • Mentor Product Managers and contribute to building strong product capability, particularly for data‑centric products.
  • Role‑model sound product judgement, accountability and outcome ownership.

[Scope of Work]

You will typically work on mission‑critical or cross‑agency products where data is foundational, such as:

  • Enterprise or sector‑wide data platforms
  • Authoritative government datasets
  • Shared analytics or reporting products

The role operates at a broader and more complex scope than a Product Manager, often spanning multiple systems, agencies and user groups.

[What we are looking for]

Core Product Skills

  • Proven experience in product management at a senior level, including product strategy, roadmap ownership and outcome delivery
  • Strong ability to manage senior stakeholders and operate in complex, multi‑party environments
  • Experience articulating product value, risks and trade‑offs to governance or leadership forums

Data & Technical Literacy

  • Good understanding of modern data platforms and architectures (e.g. ingestion, transformation, analytics, data services)
  • Working knowledge of data governance concepts such as data quality, access control and lineage
  • Ability to collaborate effectively with engineers, architects, data and security professionals

Leadership Qualities

  • Clear ownership and accountability for outcomes
  • Comfortable working with ambiguity and evolving policy or organisational needs
  • Balanced perspective on short‑term delivery and long‑term sustainability

[Why Join Us]

  • Lead high‑impact products that shape government‑wide decision‑making and operations
  • Work at scale across agencies on complex, meaningful problems
  • Operate in a senior product role with influence over strategy, investment and outcomes

This role is positioned as a Senior Product Manager with a strong data focus and fits within established product management career pathways.

Product Managers make sure that the right things get done so that our digital products meet user needs and continuously remain relevant. At a high level, this means establishing a clear vision for what a product should be and what is needed to get there. On a day-to-day basis, this means handling all the different facets of a product and making sure they are coordinated – across user needs, technical constraints, and design goals.

Be the leader and driver in executing the implementation of Government to Businesses products in Singapore Customs from the transformation of business processes to product development, testing, delivery and continuous improvements. In particular, a major product you will be working on is the platform that enables trade-related transactions and connections between various stakeholders in the trade ecosystem, including overseas Customs authorities and other trade regulators in Singapore. In addition, you will coach some product managers in the Finance cluster.

What you will be working on

You will lead a team and be involved in planning and executing the full product lifecycle including the following:

Crafting Custom Experiences for Each User Journey

  • Engage the agency Product Owner to establish a clear vision for what the product should be and what is needed to get there
  • Collaborate with business partners to define the product vision and mission and obtain key stakeholder buy-in
  • Work out the product outreach and promotion strategies with business partners
  • Define the product go-to-market strategy and work with relevant partners to execute the plan

Designing, Testing, and Iterating Through Experiments

  • Design, test, and iterate through experiments to improve the user journey
  • Manage the product budget through expenditure monitoring, cashflow management, and securing funding
  • Develop the workplan with the product team
  • Provide sufficient resources for product development, including procurement of software, hardware, and ICT professional services

Monitoring, Capturing, and Studying User Behaviour

  • Conduct user studies to understand their needs and problems
  • Analyse data to uncover usage patterns that can inform product decisions
  • Research the industry to understand complimentary and competing solutions

Collecting, Analyzing, and Presenting User Interest

  • Translate the product strategy into technical, detailed, actionable plans for business and vendor teams
  • Understand the developers' needs and if necessary support them in exploring emerging tools and techniques that can meet their needs

Analysing and Studying User Segments

  • Formulate strong proposals by evaluating the pros and cons of different solutions and provide compelling justifications for them
  • Consider how the services could be delivered for different market segments and prioritize feature development

Creating Data-Driven Analytics and Insights

  • Create data-driven analytics and insights for action and to make appropriate trade-offs
  • Coordinate efforts across multiple functions including marketing, legal, and finance
  • Co-create with the Product Owner to draft the budget papers to seek resources for the product's development

Driving User Growth

  • Drive user adoption and growth through increased engagement, expanding types of features, and higher usage in a financially sustainable manner
  • Maintain a wide network with key user agencies and central government product teams to seek opportunities for product collaborations
  • Participate in public-speaking engagements to raise awareness of the product and gain more users

Keeping Things Organized

  • Run efficient meetings to keep stakeholders and product team members updated
  • Prioritize, plan, and track what needs to be done
  • Document key information and decisions about the product
  • Be responsible for securing funding and monitoring expenses

Compliance and Governance

  • Ensure that Product meets security and performance requirements (SSAT, VAPT, Performance Testing)
  • Adhere to compliance requirements and audits (IM, AGO, QMS, etc.)

What we are looking for

  • Diploma/Degree in Computer Science, Infocomm Technology, Engineering or related subject area with minimum 15 years of relevant experience
  • Experience in managing products targeted at businesses, or large-scale product implementations involving multiple government agencies / organisational branches with development budget exceeding $20m, preferred
  • Prior working experience in product management, IT business analysis, application solutioning design and testing
  • Experience in modern product development/management technologies and practices such as Agile, Cloud, Design Thinking, and Product-Centric Development will be strongly preferred
  • Working directly with engineers, designers and other product managers (ideally at least 3 years)
  • Strong analytical, conceptualisation and problem-solving skills
  • Good communication skills with the ability to pitch ideas and influence
  • A driven and motivated personality with an inquisitive mind
  • Ability to learn and apply
  • Relevant certifications such as CITPM, COMIT, PMP, and Certified Scrum Master

As a Product Manager, you will support the CIO in Singapore Customs and collaborate with fellow team members to deliver digital solutions. To succeed, you must be a pro-active, self-motivated and resourceful team member with good technical/digital skill sets and possesses strong influencing and people handling skills. A good technical knowledge and hands-on skill will be preferred for this post as the PM will need to have good grasp of the technical setup and the analytics tool, as well as product management skill to plan the activities and product roadmap with the product owner.

Key Responsibilities

  • Manage the full product lifecycle — from concept, research, and design through to delivery, maintenance, and enhancement.
  • Drive the product roadmap, ensuring alignment with strategic goals and user needs.
  • Conduct market research and data analysis to identify opportunities, trends, and customer pain points.
  • Gather and define business requirements, write user stories, and manage the product backlog in collaboration with cross-functional teams.
  • Partner with UX designers and stakeholders to craft intuitive and effective user journeys.
  • Work closely with engineering teams to ensure timely and high-quality product releases.
  • Oversee vendor and contract management, ensuring service levels and deliverables are met.
  • Support change management, service request management, and incident/problem management activities.
  • Execute go-to-market, adoption, and usage strategies to maximize product value and impact.
  • Continuously track key metrics to measure product performance and identify opportunities for improvement.

Key Requirements

  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Business, or related field.
  • At least 5 years of experience in digital product management with a solid understanding of the product lifecycle.
  • Proven ability to apply product management disciplines to drive research, conceptualization, development, and adoption of impactful products.
  • Strong technical understanding to effectively communicate with engineering teams and stakeholders.
  • Experience in vendor and contract management, change/service request management, and incident/problem management.
  • Knowledge of Agile methodologies, CI/CD practices, and cloud infrastructure (preferably AWS).
  • Familiarity with user authentication and identity management concepts.
  • Strong stakeholder management and influencing skills, with experience collaborating across organizational boundaries.
  • Data analytics and visualization experience will be an advantage.
  • Excellent analytical, communication, and problem-solving skills with a user-centric mindset.

Customs is seeking a passionate Product Manager or Senior Product Manager to lead the development, delivery, and operation of innovative G2B and G2G digital products and services that enhance the lives of citizens and businesses in Singapore. You will be instrumental in shaping product strategy, driving execution, and ensuring the successful adoption of technology solutions that serve the public good.

As a Product Manager / Senior Product Manager, you will be responsible for

Product Strategy & Vision: Define and articulate the product vision, strategy, and roadmap in alignment with Customs’ mission and user needs. Conduct market research, user interviews, and data analysis to identify opportunities and inform product decisions.
Project Management: strong understanding on managing project integration, scope, time, budget, quality, communication, risks, procurement and stakeholders.
Product Requirements Gathering & Prioritization: Collaborate with stakeholders (business and technical) to conduct market (G2B and G2G) research, as well as gather, analyze, and prioritize product requirements. Translate complex user needs into clear, actionable plans, user stories and specifications.
Product Lifecycle Management: Own all the works involve in the end-to-end product lifecycle, from ideation and conceptualization to launch, iteration, and end-of-life planning.
Cross-Functional Collaboration: Work closely with vendors, engineering, design, data, policy, business process and technical operations teams to ensure seamless product development and delivery. Act as the primary point of contact for product-related matters.
User Centric Focus: Champion a problem-solving user-centric design approach, working with product designers to create intuitive and impactful product experiences.
Performance Monitoring & Optimization: Define key performance indicators (KPIs), OKRs, track product performance, analyze data, and identify areas for improvement and optimization.
Stakeholder Communication: Effectively communicate (written or orally) product updates, progress, and challenges to various stakeholders, including senior management and external partners.
Agile Development: Lead and participate in agile ceremonies (sprint planning, stand-ups, reviews, retrospectives) and foster an agile mindset within the team.
Risk Management: Identify and mitigate potential risks and issues throughout the product development process.
Technical Scope: Strong understanding of software development processes, modern cloud-based web technologies, platforms, testing, onboarding, interfaces and data in complex IT enterprise environments. Ability to engage in technical discussions with engineers.
Promoting Positive Changes: Shape and improve the product development process by identifying gaps, driving best practices, emphasize team works and fostering a culture of learning and continuous improvement.
Be ultimately accountable for delivering meaningful outcomes and public value through the product team’s work.

About the product

Singapore Customs is a regulatory agency under the Ministry of Finance, with the mission of protecting revenue and facilitating trade for a thriving Singapore economy.

Customs eServices serves as Customs' Single Digital Touchpoint for 6,500 Singapore based Companies, offering trade facilitation services including Hand-Carried Export Scheme, Permit Clearance Enquiry Service, Permit Returns, and eGuarantee@Gov. eGuarantee@Gov which started as service meant for Customs, had scaled to support more than 220 school, agencies and financial institutions. This enterprise-wide platform standardizes user experience design, and reusable components (e.g. SPCP integration, SSO, Call-to-Actions, Notifications, Payments, Fileshare) while expanding its compliance service portfolio to enhance revenue protection, such as eLOA and VDP. Customs eServices handled about 2.5 million domestic transactions annually.
Beyond serving the domestic market, Customs eServices enhances Singapore's competitive advantage through digital exchange of customs certificates with trading partners, accelerating cross-border trade facilitation and tariff processing compared to non-digitally connected hubs. Customs eServices handled about 200,000 cross border transactions annually.
To support Customs to meet its 2030 transformation goals, the product teams continue to upgrade existing and rollout new capabilities for trade and compliance. Behind the scenes of some of these eServices are the Customs officers who process the cases or correspond with their respective target market segments. Hence, we are looking for a passionate Product Manager to join the Customs eServices team and work together with the business teams to meet the demands for the benefit of Customs and companies.

[What you will be working on]

As a software engineer, you will:

  • Build prototypes to demonstrate technological opportunities
  • Design system architectures
  • Write production quality code
  • Manage deployments to cloud infrastructure
  • Maintain systems to a high level of reliability

Because our team focuses on pushing new initiatives, you will also have to:

  • Identify potential projects that improve the public good
  • Design novel systems that work around bureaucratic constraints
  • Advocate and explain these technical ideas to other government agencies
  • Establish best practices for developer operations
  • Provide technical leadership across multiple product teams
  • Share your expertise and mentor other engineers

You're not just here to write code, but also to figure out what we should be building and how we should build it. You will work on meaningful projects that solve problems pertinent to our society. The public sector is full of opportunities where even the simplest software can have a big impact on people’s lives. Rather than work on commercial ventures commonly found in the startup scene, we're here to improve how we live as a society through what we can offer as a government.

Your job will be to bring expertise and capability to the public sector. Sometimes this means coding new systems from scratch. Other times this means using the best solutions the development community has to offer. We use cloud services, open source software, and commodity hardware as far as possible. These often include, but are not limited to: GitHub, AWS, React Native, MongoDB, and Stripe amongst others. Knowing what to build and what to reuse lets us avoid wasting time on solved problems and focus on delivering actual value.

[What we are looking for]

  • Have a demonstrated ability to build software - Prior work and personal projects count for much more than specific degrees.
  • Write code to solve abstract programming problems - Clear thinking, the ability to debug, and understanding the limits of an approach matter much more than getting the “correct” answer.
  • Talk and reason about code with other engineers - We work as a team so you need to be able to not just solve problems, but have other people understand your solutions.
  • Take initiative to make things happen - Our job is to push for change in government, so we need to challenge the status quo and not wait for instruction.
  • Want to work for the public good - We are public servants, we serve the public. This sometimes means ignoring political pressures and misguided policies. We need people who will push back if something is not in the public interest.

Overall we look for people who have ability, initiative, good communication, and strong values.

[What you will be working on]

Digital transformation within the public sector is at the heart of what GovTech (Government Technology Agency of Singapore) does. We harness the best info-communications technologies to make a difference to the everyday lives of people in Singapore.

GovTech is seeking a Senior Software Engineer (Full Stack) to work on technically challenging problems in an environment running like a modern product company, solving important underserved problems in the public sector. As an experienced Senior Software Engineer, you are expected to work across frontend and backend concerns, contributing to both delivery and long-term system quality.

As a Senior Software Engineer, you will

  • Partner with stakeholders to prioritise and manage roadmaps for delivering towards product vision and goals
  • Collaborate with Product Managers and Designers to shape the engineering squad’s technical and delivery goals
  • Design and evolve architectures that meet stringent requirements for reliability, resilience, and cybersecurity
  • Balance new feature delivery with technical sustainability, maintainability, and risk mitigation
  • Bring strong technical judgment — helping engineers navigate design and architecture trade-offs in alignment with long-term goals
  • Ensure the team ships high-quality software predictably, and improves over time
  • Monitor delivery health, reliability, and tech debt — and address root causes, not just symptoms
  • Work regularly with engineers to engage in conversations regarding their performance, professional growth, offering constructive feedback, mentoring, and guidance as needed
    Helping with recruitment of engineers for the product team as needed

[What we are looking for]

  • Has experience in modern full-stack web application technologies; experience in modern front-end and backend engineering like Next.Js, React.Js, CSS, Javascript and Node.Js
  • Stay close to the technical details - whether through architecture reviews, design docs, code reviews, or hands-on support when needed
  • Bring clarity - helping teams prioritise, plan, and focus in a complex environment
  • Understand how software systems and engineering practices scale - and can guide improvements across architecture, testing, observability, or infrastructure
  • Believe in shared success - and collaborate openly across functions, domains, and agencies
  • Take ownership of outcomes - and hold the team to a high standard of quality, security, and delivery

We are seeking a Software Engineer to join a full stack data platform team within an internal government agency. This role sits at the intersection of software engineering, data engineering, and applied AI, and involves building scalable applications and services that enable data-driven decision-making across the organization.
You will work in a cross-functional environment alongside Software Engineers (SWE), Data Engineers (DE), and Data Scientists (DS) to design, develop, and deploy production-grade systems.

  • Design, build, and maintain full stack applications (frontend + backend) that interface with internal data platforms
  • Develop APIs and services to support data access, processing, and orchestration
  • Translate business and operational requirements into scalable technical solutions
  • Contribute to system design discussions across SWEs / Data Scientists / Data Engineers
  • Build tools and interfaces that enable efficient data consumption and exploration
  • Partner with data scientists to productionize models and analytics workflows
  • Work closely with data engineers to integrate applications with data pipelines, warehouses, and analytics systems
  • Build and integrate LLM-powered features (e.g., search, summarization, recommendation, or copilots)
  • Work on prompt design, evaluation, and integration of AI services into production systems
  • Collaborate on experimentation and iteration of AI-driven solutions

[What we are looking for]

  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field (or equivalent experience)
  • Strong proficiency in at least one backend language (e.g., Python, Java, Go, Rust)
  • Experience with frontend frameworks (e.g., React, Vue, or similar)
  • Solid understanding of system design, APIs, and distributed systems
  • Experience working with cloud platforms, preferably AWS
  • Familiarity with databases (SQL and/or NoSQL)
  • Team player with good communication skills.
  • Preferred requirements
  • Relevant experience building production systems
  • Experience working in data platform or data-intensive environments
  • Exposure to data engineering concepts (ETL pipelines, data modeling, orchestration)
  • Experience building or integrating LLM-based or AI-powered applications
  • Experience with ML, deep learning, TensorFlow, Python, NLP
  • Familiarity with tools such as vector databases, embeddings, or model APIs
  • Knowledge of DevOps practices (CI/CD, containerization, infrastructure as code)

[What you will be working on]

As a Software Engineer, your scope includes contributing to multiple components or features within your team, with growing independence in driving development.

You are expected to deliver moderately complex tasks, balancing quality, speed, and maintainability, while still collaborating closely with team members for design and planning.

You take more initiative in suggesting improvements, supporting teammates, and building awareness of the broader product and technical ecosystem.

Your impact extends beyond individual tasks to positively affect team processes and product outcomes.

You influence quality of deliverables, and contribute to reliable and maintainable systems.

Craft and Execution

Deliver Features Across Multiple Components Independently

  • Deliver POCs or features that span multiple components/modules, with guidance when needed.
  • Make thoughtful trade-offs between performance, security, and maintainability based on your understanding of context.
  • Write/Review code with clarity and maintainability in mind, applying good practices consistently.
  • Expand test coverage (e.g. unit, integration) to support key workflows and reduce flakiness.

Improve Code Reliability and Maintainability

  • Address small pockets of technical debt within your domain.
  • Suggest small improvements that enhance product performance or team velocity.
  • Contribute to CI/CD processes and deployment pipelines in collaboration with the team.
  • Contribute to resolving moderate incidents with team support; escalate complex issues when needed.

Deepen Product and Platform Technical Knowledge

  • Develop a deeper understanding of your product’s architecture and dependencies.
  • Apply secure coding and compliance principles with guidance, integrating platform standards into your work.

Ownership

Own Moderately Complex Features from Start to Finish

  • Independently plan and complete moderately scoped tasks, from design to delivery.
  • Flag risks early.

Monitor and Validate Impact of Your Work

  • Follow through on the impact of your work after it ships (e.g., metrics, bugs, or feedback).
  • Proactively troubleshoot and fix small production issues.

Actively Seek and Provide Constructive Feedback

  • Seek and apply feedback regularly on both code and working style.
  • Actively participate in team knowledge-sharing activities (e.g., demos, retros, or guilds).

Unblock and Support Team Members When Needed

  • Provide constructive code review feedback to peers and junior engineers.
  • Help others get unblocked or navigate tools, documentation, or context within your team.

Strategic Alignment

Align Work with Team and Product Priorities

  • Align your tasks with team goals and priorities; suggest improvements where you see opportunities.
  • Ask clarifying questions when requirements are unclear.

Balance Short-Term Delivery with Code Health

  • Demonstrate the ability to balance short-term delivery with long-term code health.
  • Participate in discussions about solution design and trade-offs, contributing your perspective.

Apply Platform and Compliance Standards Effectively

  • Use relevant organisational stack, platforms, or standards effectively in your work.
  • Understand compliance, cost, and performance considerations that affect your domain.

Culture and Organisational Influence

Model Good Engineering Practices for Peers

  • Reinforce the organisation's engineering values through your day-to-day work and interactions.
  • Help newer engineers navigate team workflows or practices.

Actively Shape and Reinforce Team Culture

  • Participate in engineering-wide activities like retros, internal talks, or improvement initiatives.
  • Engage respectfully with cross-functional collaborators (e.g., Product, UX) in shaping good outcomes.

Share Knowledge and Mentor Junior Engineers

  • Share technical insights or lessons learned in informal settings (e.g., demos, brown bags).
  • Support newer engineers in small ways, such as explaining concepts, debugging strategies, or team practices.

[What we are looking for]

Technical Depth

  • Has designed, built, or operated systems; credible when discussing code or architecture.

Product Mindset

  • Frames problems clearly, focuses on user and agency outcomes, validates assumptions early, and guides teams toward evidence-based decisions.

Lifecycle Awareness

  • Knows how to move from discovery → POC → POV → scale; understands how practices, architecture, and operational rigour should evolve through each stage.

Pragmatic Leadership

  • Balances ideal designs with delivery realities.

Collaborative Energy

  • Works easily with Product and Design; values diverse perspectives.

Curiosity and Growth

  • Keeps learning and helps others adapt to new technologies and practices.
  • Keeps up with modern practices like AI-assisted development and platform automation.

The Ministry of Finance is responsible for the Government Procurement (GP) policies, which govern how government agencies conduct their procurement. With evolving needs of our public officers and changing procurement landscape, MOF is embarking on the Whole of Government Procurement initiative.

GeBIZ X is the MOF product that would transform the procurement and business experience for WOG public officers and external suppliers.

Who we are looking for

We are seeking a dynamic and strategic Engineering Manager that drives technical excellence and team growth by setting strategic direction, building high-performing engineering teams, and ensuring sustainable delivery. This individual balances features delivery with technical sustainability, makes data-driven decisions, and fosters an inclusive learning culture. Key responsibilities include technical leadership, people development through coaching and feedback, quality assurance, and continuous improvement of development processes and team practices.

The Engineering Manager is required to work on technically challenging problems in an environment running like a modern product company, solving important underserved problems in the public sector. He/She will be responsible for technical direction and processes, additionally, driving the product centric design, implementation & delivery.

Our Team
We Our team is made up of passionate individuals committed to solving challenges and driving meaningful change. We embrace diversity of thought, encourage experimentation, and foster a culture of continuous learning. As part of this team, you will lead design direction and collaborate with UX designers, finance officers, and solution providers — forward-thinking professionals equally invested in your growth and in transforming MOF’s corporate finance services.

What you will be working on

  • Aim: Set Direction
  • Defining your team’s engineering vision, strategy, and directions that align with the organization’s overall vision and goals.
  • Collaborate with Product and UX partners to shape the squad’s technical and delivery goals
  • Balance new feature delivery with technical sustainability, maintainability, and risk mitigation
  • Bring strong technical judgment — helping engineers navigate design and architecture trade-offs in alignment with long-term goals
  • Use metrics (e.g., delivery velocity, incident trends, performance) to guide decisions and improve flow
  • Assemble: Build and Support the Team
  • Build a high-performing, lean team with the right mix of skills, experience, and growth potential

Coach and develop your engineers through 1:1s, feedback, and career growth planning and engage in conversations regarding their performance, professional growth, offering constructive feedback, mentoring, and guidance as needed.

Build a high-trust, inclusive team culture where feedback is valued and learning is continuous

Drive performance management with fairness, transparency, and

context

Achieve: Deliver Outcomes Sustainably
Proactively identifying and initiating worthwhile engineering initiatives to maximize our impact on the public good.

Establishing practices and tools to encourage a strong engineering culture and maximize engineering effectiveness and impact.

Ensure the team ships high-quality software predictably, and improves over time

Support technical decision-making and architecture reviews, with support from Staff+ ICs

Monitor delivery health, reliability, and tech debt — and address root causes, not just symptoms

Improve Developer Experience (DevX) and reduce friction through better tools, processes, or team rituals

Partnership for Success
Partnering with stakeholders and agencies to prioritize and manage roadmaps for delivering towards product vision incrementally and efficiently.

Creating a long-term technical plan and roadmap for the assigned team, and for other teams as needed. Update the roadmap regularly to meet future product/business needs and infrastructure requirements.

Working with cross-functional partners to develop quality, reliability, and security strategies within your team.

Required qualifications, skills and mindset

Master’s or Bachelor’s degree in relevant qualifications in any of the following: Computer Science, Computer or Electronics Engineering, Information Technology or related technical discipline

Minimum 10 years of relevant professional work experience relating to software engineering, solution engineering or system engineering with at least 6 years of hands-on experience

Minimal 3 years business analyst experience in software development projects and Agile methodologies e.g. SCRUM

Minimal 3 years’ experience working in an Agile environment and Cloud platform.

Experience with Government Commercial Cloud (GCC), SHIP-HATS and SG Tech Stack (SGTS)

8+ years of experience working in modern full-stack application technologies including SaaS, Bespoke and COTS solutions, in modern integration services, front-end and backend engineering, including at least 2 years in a leadership role

Proven track record of implementing large-scale, high-availability Salesforce solutions, preferably in the public sector

Knowledge of data protection regulations and privacy considerations in the public sector

Proven track record of people management (working with software engineers, DevOps engineers or infra/integration engineers) and being able to develop individuals’ careers and motivation to achieve team’s outcomes

Experience working on reasonably large, high-availability software systems that have operated at scale.

Experience in modern full-stack web application technologies including SaaS and Bespoke products in modern front-end and backend engineering.

Have a strong sense of what it means to be a technical leader, particularly as it relates to the suite of soft skills beyond just your technical chops.

Passion towards engineering excellence; you deeply understand that software engineering is a team sport, and you take pride in the craftsmanship aspect of software engineering.

Passion towards working for public good with interest.

The ability to guide a team to achieve important goals together.

2+ years of experience coaching developers; helping them enhance their performance and grow their careers.

Process good communication skills, ability to articulate technology concepts, participate in discussions on requirements with stakeholders

Please note that this will be a 2-year fixed term contract.

At National Environment Agency (NEA), we are always on the lookout for high performing individuals who share our aspiration of making Singapore one of the world’s best living environments. We are looking for a Senior Full Stack Engineer to take ownership of engineering across NEA’s waste management products, including a operations system and other modern applications.

This is a high-impact role for someone who can both build and assess. This is a hands-on role in a lean team, you will be expected to actively code and deliver. You should be comfortable working across the stack, while evaluating existing systems and shaping how they should evolve.

Your responsibilities will include:

1. Full Stack Development & Delivery

Build and maintain backend services and frontend interfaces. Own features end-to-end:

  • from backend logic and APIs to frontend implementation and user experience

  • Develop new capabilities from scratch (0–1) where required

2. System Assessment & Technical Direction

Rapidly understand existing systems (architecture, codebase, integrations) Evaluate trade-offs across delivery speed, complexity, and maintainability Provide clear, actionable recommendations on system evolution

3. Cross-System Integration & Data Enablement

Build and maintain integration layers between:

  • Waste Management system
  • modern applications data platform (in collaboration with Data Engineers)
  • Design and implement APIs and services to support interoperability and downstream analytics

4. DevOps & Production Ownership

Own features from development through deployment and monitoring Microsoft Azure Government Commercial Cloud (GCC), including governance and security considerations. Improve CI/CD, observability, and deployment processes

5. Product & Stakeholder Collaboration

Work closely with PMs and designers to deliver user-facing features Translate requirements into scalable technical solutions Flag risks early and drive pragmatic trade-offs

[What we are looking for]

  • 3–6+ years of software engineering experience (flexible for strong candidates)
  • Strong full stack capability: Backend (NodeJS/Golang or similar, APIs, integrations, data modelling) / Frontend (React or similar frameworks)
  • Proven ability to build and own systems end-to-end (0–1 and beyond)
  • Strong coding fundamentals, able to write and reason about production-quality code independently without AI
  • Hands-on mindset, willing to code, not just design or delegate
  • Comfortable working with and improving legacy systems
  • Ability to quickly understand complex systems and navigate ambiguity
  • Familiar with cloud environments and modern DevOps practices

Nice to Have

  • Experience modernising legacy or monolithic systems
  • Experience working across multiple products or systems
  • Exposure to government or regulated environments (e.g., GCC – Government commercial cloud)
  • Familiarity with data integration patterns
  • Experience using AI-assisted development tools effectively

job_title: Senior Enterprise Software Specialist, Human Capital Management (PSD)
job_description:
GovTech is the lead agency driving Singapore’s Smart Nation initiatives and public sector digital transformation. As the Centre of Excellence for Infocomm Technology and Smart Systems (ICT & SS), GovTech develops the Singapore Government’s capabilities in Data Science & Artificial Intelligence, Application Development, Smart City Technology, Digital Infrastructure, and Cybersecurity.

At GovTech, we offer you a purposeful career to make lives better where we empower our people to master their craft through robust learning and development opportunities all year round.

Play a part in Singapore’s vision to build a Smart Nation and embark on your meaningful journey to build tech for public good. Join us to advance our mission and shape your future with us today!

Learn more about GovTech at tech.gov.sg.

[What you will be working on]

GovTech is seeking a highly skilled and experienced Senior Enterprise Software Specialist to lead the implementation, customization, and ongoing support of Human Capital Management (HCM) enterprise software solutions. This role is critical in enhancing our HR operational efficiency and delivering seamless digital experiences for public service officers.

  • Lead the full lifecycle implementation of complex HCM enterprise software solutions, including requirements gathering, system design, configuration, testing, deployment, and post-implementation support.
  • Act as a subject matter expert for HCM modules (e.g., Core HR, Payroll, Time & Attendance, Talent Management, Benefits) within the chosen enterprise software platform.
  • Collaborate closely with HR stakeholders, business users, and technical teams to translate business requirements into effective and scalable system solutions.
  • Develop and execute comprehensive test plans, manage user acceptance testing (UAT), and ensure the quality and integrity of system functionalities.
  • Provide advanced technical support, troubleshooting, and resolution for complex HCM system issues, often acting as an escalation point.
  • Manage system upgrades, patches, and enhancements, ensuring minimal disruption and optimal performance.
  • Develop and maintain detailed system documentation, user manuals, and training materials.
  • Conduct training sessions and provide ongoing guidance to end-users and junior support staff.
  • Analyze system performance, identify areas for improvement, and recommend innovative solutions to optimize HCM processes.
  • Stay abreast of industry best practices, emerging technologies, and new functionalities within the HCM enterprise software landscape.

[What we are looking for]

The successful candidate will serve as both a technical contributor and strategic leader, conducting comprehensive business process analysis across Core HR, Talent Management, and Workforce Planning functions whilst directly executing complex configurations, customisations, and system integrations. Key responsibilities include leading HCM implementations, building proof-of-concepts, developing advanced workforce analytics, and ensuring seamless integration with critical government systems including payroll and learning management platforms.

This position requires a trusted technical specialist who can operate as an in-house expert and technical lead to vendor teams, demonstrating strong ownership of moderately complex to advanced project scopes. The role demands deep platform mastery validated through practical delivery, with impact extending beyond individual outcomes to improve team-level quality through enhanced configuration patterns, knowledge transfer, and strengthened system reliability.

The ideal candidate will possess proven hands-on implementation experience with enterprise HCM platforms, strong understanding of HR best practices and employment law, and excellent business process analysis skills. Technical proficiency in talent management modules, payroll integration, data migration, and troubleshooting is essential, alongside the ability to translate business requirements into working technical solutions whilst maintaining oversight and governance over complex government technology ecosystems.

About Work Pass Integrated System (WINS)

WINS is a large-scale ops-tech transformation initiative by Ministry of Manpower's Work Pass Division (WPD). It is a multi-year effort to replace legacy systems and consolidate work pass transactions into an integrated platform that supports policy agility and enables adaptable, future-ready services.

Design in WINS plays a strategic role in shaping coherent end-to-end experiences across multiple systems, business stakeholders, and operational workflows.

[What you will be working on]

As a Principal / Lead Designer for WINS, you will operate at the system and programme level, shaping experience strategy and driving high-quality design outcomes at scale.

This role leads through influence, standards, and alignment. You will work closely with design managers, product leaders, operations, policy, and engineering partners to ensure design contributes to WINS’ strategic outcomes and MOM’s broader service vision.

  • Own the end-to-end experience vision, ensuring key user journeys remain coherent, usable, inclusive, and consistent across products, systems, and touchpoints.
  • Identify systemic user and operational pain points, help teams reframe complex policy, technical, and workflow challenges into clear experience vision, principles, and actionable design direction.
  • Shape and steward common capabilities (e.g. shared journey patterns, service-level foundations, cross-product UX consistency, and emerging technology opportunities such as automation and AI-assisted workflows), enabling teams to scale solutions fast, accelerate learning through test & iteration, and guide programme delivery priorities.
  • Establish and evolve design standards, frameworks, and quality bars across the programme, defining what “success” looks like from discovery and concept development through delivery, post-launch measurement, and iteration.
  • Strengthen data and measurement framework to track experience quality, user outcomes, and operational efficiency. Demonstrate design impact on policy objectives and operational performance.
  • Partner with cross-functional leaders (product, engineering, operations, and policy leaders) to align design decisions across tracks, communicate trade-offs clearly, and ensure experiences meet user, operational, and policy objectives across the lifecycle.
  • Shape a strong, inclusive team culture across functions in WINS and WPD stakeholders by introducing rituals and practices that foster collaboration, shared learning, and psychological safety. Lead through influence by mentoring designers, facilitating critique and shared learning, strengthening design maturity, and supporting capability-building.
  • Drive incremental validation of new design approaches and AI tools to keep designer at the forefront of public service design.
  • Support hiring and onboarding efforts in Govtech, actively contributing to a diverse, inclusive design practice where all voices are valued.

[What we are looking for]

  • 15+ years of experience in system design, service design, product design, or design strategy, with demonstrated impact on service delivery outcomes and operational performance. Experience in regulated environments (government, healthcare, financial services) or compliance-driven contexts strongly preferred.
  • Proven experience leading design across complex, multi-stakeholder, multi-product environments.
  • Strong systems and service design thinking, with the ability to connect user needs, operations, policy, and technology.
  • Experience building design or innovation capabilities from scratch - establishing labs, frameworks, or practices that scale across organizations, generates tangible value through cost reduction, productivity improvement, or operational efficiency.
  • Track record of working across regulated or high-stakes environments (e.g., public sector, healthcare, financial services), demonstrating fluency with compliance requirements, policy constraints, and multi-layered stakeholder management.
  • Experience designing for both digital and physical service touchpoints, understanding how government services operate across channels.
  • Deep understanding of design methods and frameworks, applied pragmatically.
  • A collaborative leader who builds trust, alignment, and momentum across teams.
  • Excellent stakeholder management, facilitation, communication, and storytelling skills.

You’ll thrive here if you:

  • Enjoy making sense of complexity and ambiguity.
  • Care about outcomes and long-term impact, not just deliverables.
  • Influence through clarity, empathy, and credibility.
  • Are motivated by improving public services at scale.
  • Think like a business partner, not just a design advocate. Understanding trade-offs between user needs, operational constraints, and organizational objectives.
  • Have built something from nothing. Whether that's a new capability, a team, a framework, or a service.
  • See design as a means to measurable impact, not just better experiences.

About the Role

As part of the Internal Approving Authority - Information Technology (IAA-IT) team, you will be responsible for analysing, evaluating, and reviewing information and communications technology (ICT) budget proposals and expenditures within the Ministry. This role ensures that ICT investments align with strategic objectives, deliver value for money, and comply with government ICT policies and procurement guidelines whilst supporting the Ministry's digital transformation initiatives.

The position requires a deep understanding of both public sector financial management and the rapidly evolving technology landscape that underpins modern government operations. The candidate should possess analytical capabilities to evaluate complex technology proposals, assess emerging digital trends, and provide strategic financial guidance that enables the Ministry to leverage technology effectively whilst managing costs and risks prudently.

Key Responsibilities

ICT Budget Analysis and Evaluation
Review and analyse ICT budget submissions from various divisions and departments within the Ministry, assessing technical requirements, cost justifications, and alignment with digital strategy. Evaluate the financial viability of proposed ICT projects, including hardware, software, cloud services, and digital infrastructure investments. Review cost-benefit analysis for major ICT initiatives and technology refresh cycles.

Technology Investment Assessment
Assess the strategic value and business case for ICT investments, ensuring alignment with the Ministry's digital roadmap and whole-of-government ICT policies. Review vendor quotations and pricing models used for ICT procurements, including software licensing, cloud subscriptions, and managed services. Evaluate the total cost of ownership for ICT assets and services over their lifecycle.

Cost Norms Development and Benchmarking
Establish and maintain cost standards and benchmarks for technology purchases across the Ministry. Research market prices for ICT services, hardware, and software to create reference guides. Regularly update these benchmarks by tracking industry pricing trends, including developing standard cost estimates for common technology purchases. Validate these cost standards by reviewing actual project costs after completion, adjusting the benchmarks as needed to ensure future budget planning reflects realistic market conditions.

Compliance and Risk Management
Ensure ICT budget proposals comply with government procurement regulations, cybersecurity requirements, and data governance policies. Ensure leverage of WOG or Ministry contracts and service agreements for ICT vendors and service providers for ICT procurements. Assess financial risks associated with technology investments, including vendor dependency and obsolescence risks.

Strategic Planning Support
Contribute to the development of the Ministry's ICT budget strategy and multi-year technology investment plans. Provide financial analysis to support ICT governance committees and technology steering groups. Collaborate with ICT teams to understand technical requirements and translate them into budget implications.

Stakeholder Collaboration
Work closely with the product teams, finance teams, and business units to understand technology needs and budget constraints. Liaise with central agencies such as GovTech and Ministry of Finance on ICT budget matters and policy compliance.

Job Qualifications

  • At least 5 years of experience in ICT operations, technology project management, or IT procurement within government or large organisations, with proven success in technology implementation and vendor management.
  • Deep understanding of modern technology architectures, cloud computing, cybersecurity, and emerging technologies combined with developing financial analysis capabilities and proficiency in analytical tools and government procurement processes.
  • Strong communication skills, both verbal and written, to present technical information to diverse audiences including finance teams, with strong project management abilities to coordinate multi-departmental technology analysis projects under tight deadlines.

  • CulturePass: Democratising access to arts and culture for all Singaporeans.

  • CrowdTaskSG: A crowdsourcing platform that empowers citizens to contribute to national policy and projects.

  • Wireless Alert Alarm System: A critical safety net deployed in seniors' homes to provide immediate aid.

[What you will be working on]

You will be the design voice in a discovery squad, leading the human-centred work that shapes what gets built — and why.

Lead Design Research

Plan and execute mixed-method research (interviews, contextual inquiry, diary studies, surveys) to surface user needs, pain points and mental models. You synthesise findings into clear insights that the whole team — including non-designers — can act on.

Map Services End-to-End

Go beyond screens. Use service design tools — service blueprints, journey maps, ecosystem maps, stakeholder maps — to understand and articulate the full service experience across channels, touchpoints and backstage operations.

Frame Problems, Define Opportunities

Collaborate with Product Managers and policy teams to translate ambiguous briefs into clear problem statements and opportunity spaces. You bring the user's voice into strategy conversations and help teams avoid solving the wrong problem.

Co-Create and Prototype

Run ideation workshops and co-design sessions with stakeholders and users. Rapidly prototype concepts — from rough sketches to high-fidelity interactive mockups — to test assumptions early and validate direction before any code is written.

Produce High-Quality Design Artefacts

When the direction is clear, produce polished UI designs, interaction flows and design specifications that are ready for handover. Your production work sets the quality bar for what delivery teams will build.

Influence and Communicate

Present your work compellingly to senior government stakeholders and C-suite audiences. You translate complex user insights and service concepts into narratives that earn alignment and build confidence in the proposed direction.

Deliver Clarity Through Design

Your final output in discovery is a validated, well-articulated design direction — service concepts, UX principles, interaction patterns and a clear design vision — ready for seamless handover to a delivery team.

[What we are looking for]

We are looking for a Senior UX Designer with a generalist's range: someone equally at home facilitating a research session, mapping a complex service ecosystem, and crafting a pixel-perfect prototype. You are not a specialist researcher, and you are not a visual designer alone — you are someone who can do it all, and knows when to use each tool.

In this role, you will be embedded in discovery squads alongside Product Managers, Engineers and Policy teams. You will lead the design-side of the "0-to-0.5" phase — helping us ask better questions, see the full picture and define service concepts that are human-centred, viable and future-ready.

Note: This role is upstream of delivery. Once a direction is validated and handed over to implementation teams, you will move on to the next discovery challenge.

  • Experience: 5+ years of UX design experience, with a portfolio that demonstrates work across research, service design and UI.
  • Design Research: Proven ability to lead design research — planning studies, conducting fieldwork, synthesising insights and communicating findings to diverse audiences.
  • Service Design: Hands-on experience with service design tools: service blueprints, journey maps, ecosystem mapping and stakeholder alignment workshops.
  • Production Craft: Strong visual design and prototyping skills — you can go from whiteboard sketch to high-fidelity, interactive prototype with confidence.
  • Strategic Thinking: Ability to navigate ambiguity, hold the user perspective in complex stakeholder environments, and translate policy intent into human-centred concepts.
  • Facilitation & Communication: Excellent communication and facilitation skills — comfortable running workshops, presenting to senior leaders, and building shared understanding across disciplines.
  • Generalist Approach: A collaborative, generalist mindset — you adapt to what the project needs rather than defaulting to a fixed toolkit.

Bonus Points For

  • Experience working in or with the public sector, government agencies or civic tech.
  • Familiarity with digital service standards, accessibility guidelines or inclusive design practice.
  • Exposure to legacy system modernisation or large-scale service transformation projects.
  • Experience contributing to design systems or cross-team design operations.

Why Join Us

  • Shape National Services: Work at the point where the most important decisions are made — upstream, in discovery, where design has the greatest leverage.
  • Pure Discovery Work: Focus on the full arc of design work: research, synthesis, service design, concept generation and prototyping — without getting absorbed into production delivery cycles.
  • A World-Class Team: Join a squad where Design, Product, Engineering and Policy work as genuine partners from day one.
  • Tangible Impact: See your work become the design foundation for public services used by millions of Singaporeans.
