job_description:
GovTech is the lead agency driving Singapore’s Smart Nation initiatives and public sector digital transformation. As the Centre of Excellence for Infocomm Technology and Smart Systems (ICT & SS), GovTech develops the Singapore Government’s capabilities in Data Science & Artificial Intelligence, Application Development, Smart City Technology, Digital Infrastructure, and Cybersecurity.

At GovTech, we offer you a purposeful career to make lives better where we empower our people to master their craft through robust learning and development opportunities all year round.

Play a part in Singapore’s vision to build a Smart Nation and embark on your meaningful journey to build tech for public good. Join us to advance our mission and shape your future with us today!

Learn more about GovTech at tech.gov.sg.

We are Digital Society Products (DSP). We build tech for public good. Anchored in the Ministry of Culture, Community & Youth (MCCY) serving a Whole-of-Government mandate, we create solutions that solve real citizen problems and modernise public services.

We turn policy goals into working software. With a strong heritage in GovTech, our team has a long history of shipping national-scale digital products.

Our portfolio includes:

  • CulturePass: Democratising access to arts and culture for all Singaporeans.
  • CrowdTaskSG: A crowdsourcing platform that empowers citizens to contribute to national policy and projects.
  • Wireless Alert Alarm System: A critical safety net deployed in seniors' homes to provide immediate aid.

Who We Are Looking For

We believe the most critical phase of product development happens before a single line of code is written.

We are looking for a Discovery Product Manager to lead this upstream work for high-impact government projects. You will partner with policy teams to navigate ambiguity, de-risk investments and define the "What" and "Why" of the next generation of public services.

This is a strategic role that requires strong collaboration, a deep understanding of user and stakeholder needs and the ability to navigate public sector constraints while pushing for innovation and modernisation.

Your goal is to ensure we solve the right problems in the right way. By leading multi-disciplinary teams through problem framing, opportunity mapping and service envisioning, you will translate user needs into co-created concepts that are aligned, actionable and future-ready.

Note: This role focuses purely on achieving clarity. Once a direction is validated and actionable, you will hand it over to implementation teams for delivery.

What you will be working on:

  • This is a strategic "0-to-0.5" role. You will lead a squad with Design, Engineering to validate hypotheses and define the future state of government services.
  • Orchestrate the Vision: Lead inception workshops and envisioning sessions with policy owners and C-suite stakeholders to crystallise vague problem statements into clear product opportunities.
  • De-Risk the Future: Conduct rigorous fit-gap analysis, market sizing and feasibility assessments. You will influence if a problem requires a digital solution, a policy change or a process redesign.
  • Shape the User Experience: Apply design thinking to translate user research into actionable service concepts, opportunity maps and prototypes. You will partner with Design and Engineering to co-create services that are visionary, viable and inclusive.
  • Bridge Policy & Tech: Act as the translator between high-level government policy goals and on-the-ground technical constraints, ensuring solutions are both visionary and viable.
  • Modernise Legacy: Tackle complex legacy systems by proposing future-ready, interoperable architectures that pave the way for digital transformation.
  • Stakeholder Management: Engage and align C-suite and senior government stakeholders on the product vision, strategy and delivery roadmap.
  • Deliver Clarity: Your final "product" in this role is clarity. You will synthesize insights into a validated product direction and roadmap, facilitating a seamless handover to delivery teams.

We are looking for:

  • A strategic thinker who thrives in ambiguity and can tell a compelling story.
  • Experience: 5+ years of product management experience, with a proven track record of leading complex, high-impact initiatives.
  • Discovery Expertise: Strong experience in shaping early product direction, MVP scoping or working on pre-delivery discovery (0-to-1 work).
  • Strategic Translation: Ability to translate user needs, policy requirements, and technical constraints into viable product strategies.
  • Design Thinking: Solid understanding of design thinking principles and how to apply user research insights to product and service design.
  • Stakeholder Management: Excellent communication skills with the ability to navigate bureaucracy and align senior leadership/C-suite.
  • Collaboration: Able to facilitate cross-agency collaboration and navigate ambiguity to drive alignment.
  • Technical Fluency: Familiarity with technology and system design principles and agile ways of working.

Bonus Points For

  • Familiarity with government processes, digital service standards, procurement frameworks or data governance.
  • Experience in public sector digital transformation or modernising large-scale legacy systems.

Why Join Us?

  • Shape the National Agenda: Work upstream where the biggest decisions are made. The directions you define will become the roadmap for national services used by millions.
  • Pure Product Thinking: Focus on the "Why" and "What" without getting bogged down in the daily grind of delivery and ticket management.
  • A World-Class Team: Join a discovery squad with Design, Engineering and Policy working as one team.
  • Tangible Impact: Continue the legacy of products like CulturePass and CrowdTaskSG, influencing how government services are imagined, not just implemented.

About the Role

We are seeking an experienced Product Manager to lead the onboarding of licences onto the GoBusiness platform and develop innovative licensing features that streamline business operations in Singapore. This role sits at the intersection of government services and digital transformation, requiring someone who can navigate complex regulatory requirements while delivering user-centric solutions.

Key Responsibilities

The successful candidate will drive the strategic development of licensing capabilities on GoBusiness, working closely with government agencies to digitise and integrate various business licences onto a unified platform. You will be responsible for understanding the end-to-end licensing journey from a business perspective, identifying pain points, and designing solutions that reduce regulatory burden while maintaining compliance standards.

Your role involves conducting extensive stakeholder engagement with both government agencies and businesses to understand licensing requirements, processes, and user needs. You will translate these insights into product requirements, working with engineering teams to build scalable licensing features that serve thousands of Singapore businesses.

You will manage the product roadmap for licensing features, prioritising development based on business impact, user feedback, and government policy objectives. This includes overseeing the technical integration of various agency systems, ensuring seamless data flow and user experience across different licensing workflows.

Candidates with experience in government digital services, business licensing processes, or regulatory technology will be particularly well-suited for this role. You will work across CI/CD Tools (GitLab), Cloud (AWS), Orchestration Tools (Kubernetes EKS), and Infra-As-Code (Terraform) to create a secure, scalable, and developer-friendly platform.

This role requires strong software engineering fundamentals, systems thinking, and a passion for automation and platform excellence.

Platform Design, Build & Operations

  • Architect, implement, and operate platform components across CI/CD Tools (GitLab), Cloud (AWS), Orchestration Tools (Kubernetes EKS), and Infra-As-Code (Terraform).
  • Build reliable, scalable, and secure services that power the Runtime platforms (CStack, Airbase).
  • Continuously improve platform performance, cost efficiency, and operational robustness.

Developer Experience & Productivity

  • Design and build workflows, tooling, and paved paths that enable tenants to deploy and operate applications quickly, safely, and consistently.
  • Champion automation and self-service capabilities to minimise friction for application teams.

Automation & Toil Elimination

  • Identify operational bottlenecks and repetitive tasks; build automation via CI/CD, Infrastructure-as-Code, and controllers/operators.
  • Partner with other engineers in the team to enable highly-automated, low-touch operations.

Observability & System Health

  • Implement and maintain comprehensive observability (logs, metrics, traces, alerts) aligned with the Four Golden Signals.
  • Build automation for proactive health checks, anomaly detection, and self-healing where practical.

Production Support & Incident Management

  • Participate in on-call rotations; respond to incidents to minimise MTTR.
  • Lead or contribute to post-incident reviews, drive actionable follow-ups, and improve platform resilience.

Security & Compliance

  • Embed security into every layer of the platform through secure defaults, policy enforcement, vulnerability scanning, and AWS/K8s best practices.
  • Collaborate with security teams to meet regulatory and compliance requirements.

Performance, Optimisation & Reliability

  • Diagnose and resolve performance bottlenecks across the stack (AWS, Kubernetes, workloads, networking).
  • Define, measure, and improve KPIs such as MTTR, error rates, SLO compliance, and cost efficiency.

Strategic Tenant Engagement

  • Act as a technical advisor for tenants on containerisation, CI/CD, and cloud-native deployment best practices.
  • Participate in architecture reviews and guide tenants toward stable, secure, and scalable patterns.

Knowledge Sharing & Documentation

  • Maintain high-quality documentation, runbooks, playbooks, and architecture diagrams.
  • Ensure operational and onboarding processes are well-understood across the team.

Continuous Learning & Innovation

  • Stay current with the latest in cloud-native, Kubernetes, GitLab, and AWS ecosystems.
  • Propose new approaches, technologies, and improvements that enhance platform capabilities.

[What we are looking for]

Core Technical Skills

  • Degree or diploma in Computer Science, Engineering, or relevant experience.
  • Proven experience as a DevOps / Platform / SRE engineer working in cloud-native environments.
  • Strong understanding of AWS, Kubernetes (especially EKS), container orchestration, and cloud operations.
  • Proficiency with IaC tools (Terraform preferred) and cloud integrations (e.g., AWS Load Balancers, Secrets Manager).
  • Ability to troubleshoot complex issues across distributed systems, containers, and underlying infrastructure.
  • Hands-on experience with Kubernetes tooling: Helm, Kustomize, kubectl, ecosystem APIs.
  • Experience managing databases (e.g., PostgreSQL, or cloud-managed equivalents), including backup/restore processes, snapshot automation, recovery testing, and ensuring data durability.
  • Good software engineering fundamentals with ability to write production-grade code in Go, TypeScript, or Python.
  • Understanding of how web applications work (Go, Python, React/NextJS).
  • Familiarity with automated testing (unit, integration, end-to-end) for tools and automation you build.
  • Experience with GitOps/CI/CD tools (GitLab CI/CD, ArgoCD).
  • Experience with observability systems (Prometheus, Grafana, ELK/Elastic Stack) and SLO design.
  • Strong understanding of networking, security, and storage in both AWS and Kubernetes contexts.

Preferred Skills

  • Certified Kubernetes Administrator (CKA) or CKAD.
  • Experience building Kubernetes operators/controllers in Go.
  • Experience with service mesh technologies.
  • Experience with chaos engineering or large-scale reliability testing.

Soft Skills

  • Proactive in identifying problems and proposing practical, strategic solutions.
  • Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills.
  • Clear and concise communicator who can work with developers, security teams, and tenant engineering groups.
  • Calm and effective under pressure, especially during incidents.
  • Adaptable, curious, and continuously learning.
  • Customer-focused and capable of providing actionable, empathetic technical guidance.
  • Leadership or mentoring experience is a plus.

job_title: Assistant Director / Senior Manager, AI Strategy and Plans
job_description:
GovTech is the lead agency driving Singapore’s Smart Nation initiatives and public sector digital transformation. As the Centre of Excellence for Infocomm Technology and Smart Systems (ICT & SS), GovTech develops the Singapore Government’s capabilities in Data Science & Artificial Intelligence, Application Development, Smart City Technology, Digital Infrastructure, and Cybersecurity.

At GovTech, we offer you a purposeful career to make lives better where we empower our people to master their craft through robust learning and development opportunities all year round.

Play a part in Singapore’s vision to build a Smart Nation and embark on your meaningful journey to build tech for public good. Join us to advance our mission and shape your future with us today!

Learn more about GovTech at tech.gov.sg.

About the Role

GovTech is leading the transformation toward an AI-native government - one where AI multiplies the impact of every public officer, agency, and service. The AI Strategy Office (AISO) charts the strategic frame, orchestrates delivery across programmes, and ensures the build and adoption of AI capabilities that are safe, trusted, and transformative.

As a Strategy & Planning Manager, you will operate at the nexus of policy, technology, and execution. You will translate strategic intent into actionable roadmaps, design frameworks to bring clarity to ambiguity, and ensure disciplined follow-through across a broad portfolio of AI initiatives.

We are looking for a strong strategic operator - someone equally comfortable shaping long-term strategy and driving operational excellence, simultaneously conceptualising the big picture, and operating in the weeds to keep complex workstreams aligned and moving.

If you enjoy crafting measurable strategy and driving programme success at scale, we’d love to work with you.

[What you will be working on]

Strategy Development

  • Lead annual and multi-year strategic planning for AISO, including objective-setting, programme prioritisation, and executing interventions.
  • Conduct research and horizon-scanning on global AI trends, frontier models, compute strategy, emerging risks, talent implications, and governance approaches.
  • Develop strategy papers, issue analyses, and position pieces to drive WOG AI adoption, capability development, and investment decisions.
  • Translate complex technical, policy, and organisational considerations into clear, actionable narratives for leadership and stakeholders.
  • Facilitate strategic discussions with internal teams, ministries, statutory boards, industry partners, and academia to surface opportunities, risks, and alignment needs.
  • Prepare and deliver presentations to senior leadership; craft concise briefing materials, decision papers, and narratives that build understanding and buy-in.

Planning & Portfolio Management

  • Shape and execute the AISO operating model, running the rhythm of business - planning cycles, leadership meetings, review mechanisms, retreats, comms cadence, and ensuring strong documentation and decision traceability.
  • Maintain the AISO strategic roadmap and ensure coherence across workstreams – driving alignment across programmes, governance initiatives, capability development, and sectoral adoption workstreams.
  • Evaluate plans using structured frameworks (value, readiness, risk, cost/benefit, interdependencies) to support evidence-based prioritisation.
  • Establish OKRs and operate a robust reporting, steering, and review cadence to track progress, impact, and risks.
  • Develop dashboards and reporting mechanisms to give leadership clear visibility into progress, bottlenecks, and dependencies.
  • Ensure programme governance, performance reviews, and escalation pathways are well-run and disciplined.

[What we are looking for]

Experience & Skills

  • 8+ years of experience in strategy, planning, management consulting, programme management, public policy, or tech/AI product strategy.
  • Strong clarity of thought, able to craft crisp narratives, explain complex concepts, and influence stakeholders.
  • Directive communicator with excellent written and verbal communication skills. You must be able to influence without direct authority and negotiate with diverse stakeholders.
  • Skilled in PMO/portfolio management and experienced in OKRs or similar frameworks.
  • Strong stakeholder engagement and cross-functional coordination experience.
  • Agile and comfortable working in a fast-paced, ambiguous environment where priorities may shift.
  • Bias for clarity, structure, and disciplined execution.

Nice-to-have

  • Background or knowledge in AI, digital government, technology policy, or public-sector transformation.
  • Experience working in or strong knowledge of Whole-of-Government or multi-agency environments.

[About the role]

Government Digital Transformation (GDT) enables government agencies’ success in digital transformation by bringing the best of GovTech—through forward‑deployed CIO teams and shared capabilities—to deliver impactful digital services for citizens, businesses, and public officers. Strategy & Plans Office (SPO) in GDT seeks a dynamic and experienced leader to serve as the Assistant/Deputy Director. This role is critical in driving the strategic direction, development, and implementation of strategy and plans that align with the GDT’s goals and objectives.
[Key Responsibilities]
Strategic Architect & Change Catalyst
Transform ambiguous executive visions into strategies that shape Singapore's digital government landscape. You'll be the strategic translator who takes high-level directives from senior leadership and GDT Exco and crafts them into compelling, actionable roadmaps that drive nationwide digital transformation.
Programme Orchestrator for Nation-Scale Initiatives
Lead the strategic orchestration of Singapore's key digital transformation programmes, including the groundbreaking CIO Teams of the Future and Ministry Family Digitalisation Plan. You'll be at the helm of initiatives that reshape how government leverage technology, with direct visibility into their impact across the entire public sector ecosystem.
Cross-Government Collaboration Architect
Design and execute the strategic frameworks that unite Singapore's government agencies in their digital journey. You'll orchestrate cross-divisional planning sessions, craft compelling strategic narratives, and identify breakthrough synergies that amplify transformation impact across the whole-of-government landscape.
Digital Transformation Strategist & Advisor
Leverage your digital transformation expertise to shape the future of government operations. You'll define meaningful success metrics through OKRs, architect workforce transformation strategies, and chart realistic yet ambitious pathways that push the boundaries of what's possible in digital government.
Leadership Development Champion
Cultivate the next generation of digital transformation leaders by mentoring high-potential Managers and Senior Managers. You'll guide them in strategic thinking, stakeholder mastery, and initiative execution whilst building SPO's capability pipeline for sustained impact.
Senior Stakeholder Influencer & Alliance Builder
Engage directly with Singapore's most senior digital leaders—Cluster Directors and CIOs—to co-create strategies that drive cross-agency transformation. You'll be the strategic bridge-builder who secures buy-in for ambitious initiatives that span the entire government ecosystem.

[Required Competencies]

  • Strategic Thinking: Ability to approach challenges and opportunities with a high-level, analytical mindset—synthesising information, anticipating implications, and developing effective ways of organising work, clarifying accountabilities, and guiding both planned strategies and ad hoc initiatives.
  • Stakeholder Management: Proven ability to identify, engage, and build strong relationships with key stakeholders—both internal and external—while effectively facilitating discussions to foster alignment, trust, and collaboration across all levels.
  • Effective Communication: Good written and verbal communication skills, including the ability to clearly articulate complex ideas, facilitate productive conversations, and deliver impactful presentations to diverse audiences.
  • Collaboration: Strong aptitude for working across divisions and multidisciplinary teams, driving unified efforts and achieving consensus on shared organisational objectives.
  • Leadership & Programme Oversight: Skilled at managing a programme office, coordinating workstreams, and coaching staff
  • Digital Transformation Experience: Understanding of digital transformation challenges, including technology adoption, operational challenges and workforce capability development.
  • Experience: At least 4 years in strategy, planning and/or digital transformation, ideally with exposure to public-sector transformation or enterprise change.

[Learning Opportunities]

  • Develop advanced strategic thinking skills by tackling both planned strategies and ad hoc initiatives, learning how to organise team efforts, clarify roles, and address complex organisational challenges.
  • Enhance stakeholder management abilities by engaging with senior leaders and diverse teams, building proficiency in facilitating discussions and navigating different perspectives to achieve alignment.
  • Refine communication skills through opportunities to lead meetings, deliver presentations, and prepare communication materials for a variety of audiences.
  • Strengthen collaborative skills by participating in and facilitating cross-functional initiatives, fostering synergy and cooperation among multiple stakeholders.

Who We Are & Our Vision

At GovTech, we don’t just build apps and technology; we build the digital infrastructure of an entire nation. Our mission is to transform the delivery of public services through technology that is seamless, secure, and—above all—human-centric.

The Product Marketing Team is the bridge between world-class engineering and millions of users. We don’t just "launch" products; we drive adoption, build trust, and ensure that every digital touchpoint improves a life. Our end goal is that through our work, Singapore becomes a model of how governments can use technology to improve the public good, and that GovTech products are loved and have a positive impact across Singapore.

The Portfolio: Impact at Scale

You will oversee a diverse ecosystem of products that have redefined the Singaporean digital experience, examples include:

  • Singpass: The trusted digital identity of every Singaporean.
  • GovWallet: Revolutionizing how government payouts and credits reach citizens.
  • BookingSG: Making bookings seamless for public officers and citizens.
  • Oobee: Making digital accessibility a reality for all.
  • MySkillsFuture 2.0: Powering the nation's lifelong learning and career ambitions.
  • SG Culture Pass: Singapore’s pass to the arts and culture, designed to turn every citizen into a cultural insider.

Technical Requirements & Qualifications

Experience

  • Seniority: Minimum 6–8 years of experience in Product Marketing or Growth roles.
  • Seasoned Marketer: Have a demonstrated ability to plan and execute marketing or product marketing strategies. Prior work and personal projects count for much more than specific degrees.
  • SaaS Expertise: Minimum 2 years in Enterprise SaaS. You must demonstrate experience managing complex B2B or B2B2C product lifecycles, API-led ecosystems, and platform adoption.
  • People Management: Proven experience working in teams and influencing across multi-levels of stakeholders. Having managed a small team or reports will be a plus.

Technical Skills

  • Go-to-market Fluency: Expert-level knowledge of Go-To-Market (GTM) strategy and a proven track record of executing on plans proficiently and managing budgets efficiently. An ability to work and influence across different product teams to scale adoption and grow users, plus improve retention rates of GovTech products.
    • Some key activities include:
      • Drive user adoption and acquisition by identifying the product discovery channels and user touch points.
      • Being an expert on our users, who they are, how they behave and their key criteria for taking action.
      • Manage and execute product marketing campaigns
      • Analyzing product funnels and engagement data to identify friction points and directing the team on intervention strategies.
      • Managing a team of PMMs, providing technical guidance on lifecycle marketing and stakeholder management.
  • Data & Analytics: Have exceptional ROI-tracking skills, able to prove what is, or isn’t, working. You must be able to perform independent data deep-dives to validate marketing decisions and create feedback loops. A proficiency in a GA4, Mixpanel, or Amplitude is ideal.
  • The SaaS "Lens": You bring a "Product-Led Growth" mindset. You view B2B2C and B2B products like GovWallet or BookingSG through the lens of user friction, activation, and retention while managing them as an integrated ecosystem rather than a collection of features.
  • Narrative Excellence: You are a world-class writer and presenter. You can take a 50-slide technical deck and distill it into a 3-slide "Vision Pitch" that inspires action, as well as crafting a message that resonates with every end user.
  • Stakeholder Diplomacy: The ability to navigate the "Gov" in GovTech. You can translate complex technical roadmaps into value-based narratives for non-technical policy makers and technical product team members alike.

Why join us?

Because in most companies, "Product-Market Fit" means a better quarter. At GovTech, it means a better country. With us, you’ll have the opportunity to shape and improve the lives of everyone in Singapore.

At DXD (Digital Excellence & Products Division), Ministry of Education (MOE), we believe that meaningful digital transformation starts by improving the everyday experiences of our users — including the thousands of school and HQ staff who keep our education system running smoothly.

We are seeking a Senior / Lead UX Designer to lead the user experience and service design for MOE’s HR Systems. This role will focus on simplifying complex HR workflows, improving usability, and enabling smarter, more intuitive digital tools that directly support HR functions from recruitment to delivering positive employee experiences.

In the first 6–12 months, the role will focus on the problem discovery in the hiring processes and identify opportunities to for tech to improve the recruitment outcomes.   • Map end-to-end user journeys and service blueprints to identify opportunities for process improvement

UX Design, Prototyping, and Iteration

Skills, Behaviours, and Attributes

  • Strong foundation in service design, UX research, and journey mapping
  • Proficient in prototyping and visual design tools
  • Skilled in conducting and facilitating workshops to align diverse stakeholders
  • Clear communicator able to translate user insights into actionable designs
  • Passionate about simplifying complex systems to improve user experience

  • At least 8 years of experience in UX design, service design, or digital transformation roles
  • Experience designing for HR, enterprise or administrative systems is highly valued
  • Familiarity with public sector environments or large-scale system transformations is a plus

job_title: Principal / Lead Software Engineer (Cybersecurity Specialist) — TradeNet CII
job_description:
About GovTech

The Government Technology Agency (GovTech) transforms the delivery of Government digital services by taking an "outside-in" view, putting citizens and businesses at the heart of everything we do. We build the Smart Nation infrastructure and the platforms the public sector runs on, and we partner agencies to engineer trustworthy, resilient digital services at national scale.

What is this role?

TradeNet is Singapore's national single-window trade platform and a legislated Critical Information Infrastructure (CII). Its availability and integrity underpin national trade continuity; disruption is an economic event, not a system outage.

We are seeking a Principal / Lead level Software Engineer (Cybersecurity Specialist) to be the technical design authority for the modernised TradeNet CII rebuild, focusing on architecture security and resilience.

This is an architect-and-build mandate, not an assurance or operations role. As a first-line engineering role, you will

  1. Design and embed security and resilience controls into the platform starting from the architecture and down to the implementation details.
  2. Decide what is inherited from the GovTech security tech stack versus built and owned by the product team.
  3. Design the defensive terrain jointly with the Singapore Customs Agency CISO (ACISO) so that the agency can defensibly discharge its CII regulatory accountability to Cyber Security Agency (CSA).

You will be measured on the delivery of TradeNet CII that is secure and resilient by design and construction — not on the volume of findings triaged or evidence produced after the fact.

Impact and outcomes

You will be successful when:

  • TradeNet's security architecture is conformant with CSA CCoP v2 and WOG IM8 by design, not by downstream remediation, and the conformance basis is clearly documented and defensible to CSA.
  • The Customs ACISO can stand behind a coherent, evidenced security and resilience architecture for the CII without reconstructing it from operational artefacts.
  • The platform has an engineered national trade-continuity posture — recoverability, degraded-mode operation, and containment of blast radius are designed properties, not aspirations.
  • The boundary between controls inherited from the GovTech security tech stack and controls owned by the TradeNet product is explicit, maintained, and used to scope CII audit and attestation.
  • Security control intent is expressed as code and continuously monitored, reducing reliance on point-in-time attestation.
  • There is a strong culture of security in the organization through your leadership, mentorship and coaching.

What you will be working on

Secure and resilient architecture (core mandate)

  • Own the reference security architecture for the TradeNet CII: trust boundaries, identity architecture, segmentation and trust-zone strategy, east-west controls, encryption and key management, and blast-radius containment for the CII boundary.
  • Embed secure-by-design controls by working inside product and engineering teams through the delivery lifecycle — at design, solutioning and implementation time, not as post-build validation.
  • Maintain a living threat model per system and trust boundary, referenced to MITRE ATT&CK and to relevant adversary classes (including supply-chain and nation-state), and use it to drive architecture and segmentation decisions — not merely to prioritise remediation.

Resilience and national trade continuity

  • Engineer the platform's recoverability and graceful degradation: degraded-mode operation, recovery objectives appropriate to a national single-window, and containment design that preserves trade continuity under attack.
  • Map and treat dependency and concentration risk across the TradeNet ecosystem (traders, brokers, ports, and partner agencies), including third-party and supply-chain integration risk.
  • Design for, and participate in, CSA-mandated cyber resilience exercising for the CII, feeding findings back into the architecture.

Defensive terrain (with the Customs ACISO)

  • Partner the Customs ACISO as the technical design authority enabling the agency to discharge CII accountability to CSA: design the Customs digital terrain and CII boundary with multi-layered defence, and translate regulatory obligation into architecture rather than into compliance tasks.
  • Co-develop the defensible architecture and resilience narrative the ACISO relies on for CSA engagement, audits, and CII regulatory submissions.

Platform leverage and control inheritance

  • Determine and document what the CII inherits from the GovTech security tech stack (e.g. GCC, central SOC/monitoring, ShipHats pipeline guardrails, IM8 baseline controls) versus what the TradeNet product team must build and own.
  • Maintain the shared-responsibility delineation as an architectural artefact that also scopes CII audit boundaries, so inherited controls are not re-attested.

Continuous assurance and secure delivery

  • Express security control intent as code: pipeline guardrails, policy-as-code, and continuous control monitoring designed into the platform so conformance is observable continuously rather than reconstructed for audits.
  • Champion secure SDLC and agile security practice within the delivery cadence, embedded in engineering teams rather than gating them.

Transitional / Day-2 scope (explicitly secondary)

The following are transitional and not the primary remit. Operational validation of the CLE's own designs sits with an independent assurance line to preserve control independence; the CLE provides engineering guidance, not self-validation:

  • Advisory and incident-response engineering support in coordination with SIRO, ACISO, and GCSOC.
  • Scoping and engineering guidance for external VAPT and vulnerability assessment, with closure validation performed independently.

What we are looking for

  • 10+ years in Cybersecurity as a Software Engineer and not just in the capacity of auditing, pen-testing, operational triage, etc….
  • We need someone that is hands on where the ideal candidate is someone that can design systems to be secure and can sit down to write code if they need to, someone that can coach our engineering teams on how to write better and more secure code, and someone that understands the cybersecurity tooling landscape to help us choose the right tools, we are not looking for a high level theorist / academic.
  • Demonstrated experience with designing security architecture into regulated / critical systems and platforms at the national or whole enterprise level, not just providing testing and assurance but actually designing and implementing.
  • Working command of Singapore regulatory frameworks for critical systems: CSA CCoP v2 for CII and WOG IM8 (Reform), with the ability to translate obligation into architecture and to delineate control inheritance for audit scoping.
  • Strong systems thinking: able to reason about a national platform as an interdependent whole — boundaries, failure modes, recoverability, and concentration risk — not as a checklist of controls.
  • Depth across cloud and platform security architecture (IaaS/PaaS/SaaS), identity, encryption and key management, segmentation and zero-trust patterns, and secure SDLC / policy-as-code.
  • Fluency with architectural and adversary frameworks (MITRE ATT&CK, NIST, ISO 27001, CIS benchmarks) used to drive design decisions.
  • Ability to operate as a technical design authority alongside a CISO, clearly within the first line, and to produce architecture that withstands regulatory and audit scrutiny.
  • Proficiency in at least one scripting/automation language (e.g. Python, TypeScript, Shell/Bash, etc…) for policy-as-code and tooling.
  • Preferably proficient in both Kotlin/JVM stack and TypeScript.
  • Architecture-leaning certifications are advantageous (e.g. CISSP / CISSP-ISSAP, SABSA, cloud security architecture). Incident-response and offensive certifications are useful but not the primary signal for this role.
  • Subject to personnel security clearance / CII vetting appropriate to access to a national CII.
  • Subject to the nature of the role, onsite presence during fixed hours may be required.

How this role sits in our assurance model

This is a first-line engineering role. The Customs ACISO holds CII risk accountability; an independent assurance function validates and attests. The CLE provides the engineering and architectural substance both rely on, and does not validate or attest to the controls it designs. This separation is deliberate and protects the integrity of the CII assurance chain.

---

  • Drive user advocacy by designing exceptional user experiences across the entire examination lifecycle and executing robust go-to-market strategies for exam administrators, question authors, markers, and educational institutions.
  • Define and track appropriate metrics to measure platform success, user satisfaction, operational efficiency, and security compliance whilst supporting data-driven product decisions.
  • Balance development priorities between new examination capabilities, migration of reusable assessment components, and maintenance of existing systems, ensuring stakeholder alignment and uninterrupted examination operations.
  • Actively uncover opportunities to leverage AI and emerging technologies to enhance examination security, streamline administrative processes, improve marking efficiency, and support innovative assessment methods within a controlled, auditable environment.
  • Ensure platform sovereignty and security by maintaining SEAB's ownership and governance of critical examination infrastructure whilst enabling composable architecture for continuous improvement.

What we are looking for:

  • Product management experience with proven success driving transformation of complex digital platforms (education technology, assessment systems, or mission-critical enterprise systems preferred).
  • Excellent stakeholder management skills to influence senior government leaders, educational stakeholders, and collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams in regulated environments.
  • Comfort in ambiguity and complexity to discern the highest leverage problems in examination administration whilst navigating security requirements, compliance standards, and diverse user needs.
  • Validated leadership ability to lead product teams in rapidly iterating and solving problems whilst maintaining rigorous security and quality standards essential for examination integrity.
  • Deep technical capabilities to work effectively with design and engineering teams on secure, scalable platforms and understand the implications of composable architecture and API design.
  • Keen product intuition to identify high-potential opportunities for innovation in assessment technology whilst balancing reliability requirements and regulatory compliance.
  • Strong analytical skills to synthesise examination metrics, user feedback, and operational data into actionable insights that improve both user experience and system performance.
  • Understanding of security and compliance requirements, particularly in high-stakes assessment environments where trust, fairness, and integrity are paramount.

What Success Looks Like

  • A unified examination administration and digital assessment platform that seamlessly serves all user groups with high satisfaction and adoption rates.
  • Successful modernisation of SEAB's examination processes whilst maintaining operational continuity and security standards.
  • Strong stakeholder confidence in the platform's ability to uphold examination integrity whilst enabling innovation.
  • Measurable improvements in operational efficiency, user experience, and assessment capabilities.
  • Establishment of SEAB as a leader in secure, innovative assessment technology within the education sector.

[What you will be working on]

We build Singpass, Singapore’s national digital identity system, one of the most critical and widely used digital platforms in the country, and a leader in the national identity space. Singpass enables authentication, authorisation, digital signatures, corporate identity (Corppass), government verified data sharing (Myinfo), and secure transactions across both government and private-sector services.


Working on Singpass means working on infrastructure where security, reliability, and trust are non-negotiable. The system must remain robust against constant security threats, operate seamlessly at scale, and support millions of transactions daily. Changes must be carefully
evaluated, well-coordinated, and safely executed in a live environment that the entire nation depends on.


We continue to innovate and expand Singpass, improving ease of use, security, and value for our users and partners. We keep up to date with new global developments to inform our strategy. This is your chance to contribute directly to incorporating these next-generation capabilities into Singpass

As a software engineer, you will:

  • Build prototypes to assess new capabilities with security and feasibility in mind

  • Design and evolve architectures that meet stringent requirements for reliability, resilience, and cybersecurity

  • Write secure, high-quality, production-ready code

  • Manage cloud deployments with a focus on operational safety and system integrity

  • Conduct maintenance and monitoring to uphold high uptime and service stability

Because our team drives new initiatives while operating critical infrastructure, you will also

  • Identify opportunities to strengthen Singpass services and security posture

  • Design solutions that account for regulatory requirements, operational constraints, and risk management

  • Work closely with product managers, policy teams, UX designers, cybersecurity specialists, partner agencies, and external providers

  • Explain technical proposals, including security implications, to diverse stakeholders
    across government

In addition to the above, more experienced software engineers will be expected to:

  • Establish engineering, reliability, and secure-by-design best practices

  • Drive technical leadership across multiple teams and products

  • Mentor engineers while elevating our security and reliability culture

  • Support technical hiring and capability development

  • Lead cross-agency discussions on architecture, cybersecurity, and risk mitigation

This role requires balancing innovation with the responsibility of safeguarding a national digital identity system. You’ll enhance existing infrastructure while ensuring that new features are secure, dependable, and resilient.

What it is like working here:

  • Security-First Mindset - Handling national identity infrastructure demands strong engineering discipline, attention to detail, and a proactive approach to risk management.

  • Rapid Prototyping - We test ideas quickly to uncover issues early, especially those affecting reliability or security.

  • Ownership - Engineers help shape product direction, advocate for secure and robust technical choices, and influence how national systems evolve.

  • Polygot Environment - While our current infrastructure is based on Java and TypeScript, we are moving to standardise on Go for new projects. You'll work on optimising established services and delivering new capabilities using modern Go architecture.

  • Continuous Learning - You'll explore new technologies, architectures, and security practices while working with diverse teams across government.

[What we are looking for]

We value strong engineering fundamentals over specific programming languages. Our systems use a variety of technologies, and we select tools based on technical and security requirements.

We’re looking for people who:
  • Demonstrate the ability to build high-quality, secure software
  • Think clearly about abstract problems, with strong debugging and reasoning skills
  • Communicate and collaborate effectively with engineers, policy officers, UX teams, cybersecurity specialists, and partner agencie
  • Take initiative to drive improvements in reliability and security
  • Care deeply about public good, and understand the responsibility of working on critical national infrastructure

[About the Role]

The Government Digital Products (GDP) Team aims to spearhead the digital transformation of government. Our team aims to design and develop software applications that help government agencies to better serve the needs of the people of Singapore.

At the Engagement and Case Management Programme, our products are the primary touchpoints through which residents interact with the Singapore Government. We design and develop a suite of software applications including FileSG, Signify, PersonaliseSG, CrowdTask SG, and BookingSG.

As an Engineering Manager, you will lead a cross-functional agile team to deliver high-quality software while providing strong technical leadership. You will shape the team’s architecture and engineering practices, guide technical decision-making, and foster a culture of ownership, collaboration, and continuous improvement.

You will work closely with Product, Design, and Engineering partners to solve complex problems, deliver high-impact features, and continuously improve how the team builds and operates software

[What you will be working on]

Lead and Grow an Engineering Team

  • Lead, mentor, and develop a cross-functional team to deliver reliable, high-quality software.
  • Foster a culture of ownership, collaboration, and continuous improvement.
  • Provide hands-on technical guidance and support engineers in solving complex problems.
  • Help engineers grow through coaching, technical feedback, and strong engineering practices.

Drive Technical Strategy and Architecture

  • Define and evolve the technical vision and architecture for the team’s systems.
  • Guide design and architecture decisions, balancing speed, scalability, maintainability, and long-term platform goals.
  • Partner with other engineering leaders to align on architecture, platform direction, and cross-team technical initiatives.
  • Identify and mitigate technical risks while maintaining a healthy balance between feature delivery and technical sustainability.

Deliver High-Impact Software

  • Collaborate with Product Managers and Designers to shape solutions and define delivery goals.
  • Lead the team in planning and executing engineering work while maintaining strong delivery outcomes.
  • Use engineering and operational metrics (e.g., delivery flow, reliability, performance) to continuously improve team effectiveness.

Raise the Engineering Bar

  • Champion best practices in system design, code quality, testing, and operational excellence.
  • Promote DevOps practices including CI/CD, automation, and strong operational ownership.
  • Establish scalable engineering processes such as design reviews, architecture discussions, and effective code reviews

[What we are looking for]

Experience

  • At least 5 years of experience building and delivering production web applications.
  • Experience leading or mentoring engineers within a development team.
  • Strong experience designing and building scalable backend systems.

Technical Skills

  • Strong experience with Node.js and TypeScript.
  • Experience building modern web applications and APIs.
  • Familiarity with modern front-end frameworks such as React or Next.js.
  • Experience working with cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud.
  • Experience with automated testing and modern development practices.
  • Experience with container technologies (e.g., Docker).
  • Experience implementing or operating CI/CD pipelines.
  • Exposure to GenAI technologies or AI-powered product capabilities.

Leadership & Mindset

  • Strong technical judgment and ability to navigate architectural trade-offs.
  • Passion for mentoring engineers and building strong teams.
  • Clear communicator who collaborates effectively across engineering, product, and design.
  • Curious, proactive, and motivated to continuously learn and improve systems and processes.

job_title: Senior Software Engineer , Singapore Land Transport Authority
job_description:
GovTech is the lead agency driving Singapore’s Smart Nation initiatives and public sector digital transformation. As the Centre of Excellence for Infocomm Technology and Smart Systems (ICT & SS), GovTech develops the Singapore Government’s capabilities in Data Science & Artificial Intelligence, Application Development, Smart City Technology, Digital Infrastructure, and Cybersecurity.

At GovTech, we offer you a purposeful career to make lives better where we empower our people to master their craft through robust learning and development opportunities all year round.

Play a part in Singapore’s vision to build a Smart Nation and embark on your meaningful journey to build tech for public good. Join us to advance our mission and shape your future with us today!

Learn more about GovTech at tech.gov.sg.

[Overview]

We are looking for a hands-on Senior Software Engineer to join a team modernising a complex business-critical system within the public sector. This is a role for an engineer who enjoys solving hard problems in legacy environments, where business workflows are intricate, data models are dense, and correctness matters as much as delivery speed.

You will work on systems that are operationally important, rich in domain logic, and tightly coupled to real-world processes. Success in this role requires more than strong coding ability. We are looking for someone who can understand business workflows, reason through legacy system behaviour, shape maintainable modern solutions, and build software that is reliable, testable, and safe to evolve.

We value engineers who are comfortable contributing beyond implementation, including helping to shape technical direction and uplift team capability over time.

[What you will be working on]

Digital transformation within the public sector is at the heart of what GovTech does. We harness technology to solve meaningful and underserved problems across multiple domains. In this role, you will help modernise a legacy system with complex workflows, high data integrity requirements, and significant downstream operational impact.

As a Senior Software Engineer, you will be responsible for designing, implementing, and evolving core application logic and services, while helping the team navigate legacy constraints and modern engineering practices.

Your responsibilities will include:

  • Designing and implementing backend services, APIs, and application logic for complex business workflows
  • Analysing legacy systems, schemas, and behaviours to uncover business rules, data relationships, and hidden operational constraints
  • Translating legacy data structures and process flows into cleaner domain models and maintainable modern service designs
  • Designing and evolving data models that preserve integrity while supporting future extensibility
  • Building validations, state transitions, orchestration flows, and exception handling for transaction-heavy processes
  • Working closely with QA engineers and fellow developers to ensure the system is testable, observable, and production-ready
  • Writing high-quality, maintainable code with strong automated test coverage
  • Participating in technical design discussions, code reviews, defect analysis, and release readiness activities
  • Supporting migration planning, data reconciliation, and coexistence between legacy and modern components where required
  • Documenting key technical decisions, business-rule assumptions, and implementation trade-offs clearly
  • Contributing to engineering standards, development practices, and delivery discipline across the team
  • Providing technical guidance to peers and helping to uplift engineering quality through collaboration, mentoring, and knowledge sharing

[What we are looking for]

Technical Requirements

  • Strong hands-on experience in Java
  • Strong experience building backend services using frameworks such as Spring Boot
  • Strong experience with TDD and test automation tool, such as Junit, JMeter, etc.
  • Experience with modern frontend development using NodeJS and TypeScript / JavaScript is a plus.
  • Strong experience with REST APIs, backend workflow implementation, and enterprise integration patterns
  • Strong experience with relational databases such as PostgreSQL, Oracle, or equivalent.
  • Experience working on legacy enterprise systems or large business-critical systems
  • Experience in AWS ecosystem (EC2, RDS, EKS) is a plus.

Additional technical expectations

  • Strong software engineering fundamentals in backend and service development
  • Strong understanding of data modelling, transactional integrity, and stateful workflow design
  • Experience writing unit, integration, and service-level automated tests
  • Experience with CI/CD, source control workflows, and release-safe engineering practices
  • Ability to troubleshoot across application, data, and integration layers
  • Familiarity with observability, production support, and operational debugging
  • Good judgment in balancing code quality, delivery pace, and legacy system realities

What we offer you:

GovTech is an equal opportunity employer committed to fostering an inclusive workplace that values diverse voices and perspectives, as we believe that diversity is the foundation to innovation.   

Our employee benefits are based on a total rewards approach, offering a holistic and market-competitive suite of perks. These include leave benefits to meet your work-life needs and employee wellness programs. 

We champion flexible work arrangements (subject to your job role) and trust that you will manage your own time to deliver your best, wherever you are, and whatever works best for you.

Stay connected with us on social media at go.gov.sg/ConnectWithGovTech

The MOM Enforcement platforms help in streamlining migrant worker and employer interactions, process licenses, support intelligence gathering and enforcement activities, manage prosecution workflows, oversee operational functions, maintain health records, and serve as centralised hubs for address and training data across Ministry of Manpower operations

Senior Product Managers are expected to lead our products or initiatives, demonstrating a high degree of ownership and strategic thinking.

In addition to product execution, Senior PMs mentor junior team members, fostering growth within the team. They serve as a key liaison between business leadership and product teams, advocating for product needs and user insights.

  • Maintain a wide network with key user agencies and central government product teams to seek opportunities for product collaborations
  • Participate in public-speaking engagements to raise awareness of the product and gain more users

[About the Role]

We are looking for a Forward-Deployed Product Lead (Senior Product Manager/Lead Product Manager) to be embedded within the Land Transport Authority (LTA) to drive the modernisation of enforcement systems under the Enforcement 2.0 (ENF2.0) track.

This is a hands-on leadership role where you will work at the intersection of policy, operations, and technology, leading cross-functional teams to reimagine and rebuild mission-critical enforcement workflows into modern, scalable digital products.

What is Enforcement 2.0 (ENF2.0)

ENF2.0 is LTA’s next-generation enforcement transformation initiative.

It aims to:

  • Modernise legacy enforcement systems and workflows
  • Enable end-to-end digital enforcement journeys
  • Improve operational efficiency and decision-making for enforcement officers
  • Integrate data across systems to support smarter, more proactive enforcement
  • Build modular, scalable platforms that can evolve with policy and operational needs

This includes areas such as:

  • Case management for enforcement officers
  • Workflow automation and rule engines
  • Data-driven insights and analytics for enforcement actions

The programme adopts a product-led, agile, and discovery-first approach, moving away from traditional system delivery.

[What you will be working on]

Strategic Leadership

  • Define and drive the product vision, strategy, and roadmap for ENF2.0
  • Align product direction with LTA’s operational priorities and Whole-of-Government (WOG) digital transformation goals
  • Translate policy and operational needs into clear product outcomes and measurable impact
  • Drive prioritisation based on user value, impact, and feasibility

Product Development Leadership

  • Lead and be accountable for end-to-end product delivery across discovery and development
  • Build and lead multi-disciplinary product squads (PM, engineers, designers, data)
  • Make strategic decisions on build vs buy, architecture, and resource allocation
  • Ensure strong execution across iterations, with rapid validation through pilots and beta releases

Capability Building

  • Build and uplift product management capabilities within LTA’s Traffic Road Operations (hire, coach, mentor)
  • Develop product culture and ways of working within agency teams
  • Deliver training, coaching, and on-the-job mentorship aligned with GovTech Product Management Practice
  • Establish best practices for:
    • Product discovery
    • Prioritisation and roadmap planning
    • Iterative delivery and outcome tracking

[What we are looking for]

  • Strong experience in product leadership (preferably in complex, multi-stakeholder environments)

Our mandate is to shape policies and drive initiatives that not only enhance the quality of education but also integrate digital advancements and global best practices—ensuring that our educators and students are well-prepared to navigate and excel in an ever-evolving world.

Joining MOE DXD’s Tf(x) team will place you at the forefront of transforming education through technology. As part of our innovative unit, you will create bespoke tools tailored specifically for educators, empowering them to streamline processes, enrich classroom experiences, and inspire the next generation of learners. In this role, your technical expertise will intersect with visionary educational goals. This is an opportunity to drive meaningful change in a dynamic environment where creativity, collaboration, and cutting-edge technology converge to shape the future of education.

Expect yourself to collaborate with experienced educators, product/ops managers, software engineers and designers to rapidly prototype innovative solutions that tackle key challenges in the education sector. You will be working in an environment that encourages continuous learning through hands-on guidance and mentorship. Be prepared to utilise a continuous integration engineering pipeline and an iterative design process to streamline both development and testing.

[What you will be working on]

You will work in Tf(x), a team of product/ops managers, software engineers and designers (formerly Transform): “We're a dynamic team, equally driven by our passion for making a positive impact and our dedication to our craft. Whether we're developing robust software or designing user-centric experiences, we ensure that everything we do is delivered at our best.”

Your job scope with our team is as follows:

  • Develop bespoke digital tools to help schools and teachers address their operational and administrative pain-points. You will join a team of software engineers, product managers and designers to establish a digital product team with the broad objective of building bespoke products for MOE officers and educators. This includes data analytics tools for analysing student results, profiling tools to identify at-risk students, and even an ops-readiness digital product to ready schools for emergencies such as COVID-19. Much of the work will be synthesised from hackathons, user discovery sessions, workshops and rapid prototyping.
  • Modernise the School Cockpit IT system and reorganise the engineering team structure. The School Cockpit is a critical system used by schools and educators for school administrative tasks and aggregating student information across Singapore. The vision is for MOE to re-conceptualise this long-standing system into a collection of applications with potential reuseability across the entire MOE ecosystem. Aside from engineering work, there will be an opportunity to work with MOE’s engineering partners in the private sector, and transform the way public-private engineering partnerships have traditionally worked in MOE.
  • Facilitate the transfer of central engineering knowledge from GovTech to MOE. You will be working with a product team who has previously built central products (e.g. SafeEntry, TraceTogether and OurSG Grants Portal) and Artificial Intelligence products that look into the extraction, synthesis and usage of data in government agencies. [What you will be working on]

Digital transformation within the public sector is at the heart of what GovTech (Government Technology Agency of Singapore) does. We harness the best info-communications technologies to make a difference to the everyday lives of people in Singapore.

GovTech is seeking an engineering manager to work on technically challenging problems in an environment running like a modern product company, solving important underserved problems in the public sector. Some of the problems are related to different programs including healthcare, manpower transformation, trade facilitation, national development, HR and Finance products.

As an Engineering Manager, you will be responsible for technical direction and processes in one (or more) teams. Additionally, you will drive the design, implementation & practices across Engineering teams in MSF. You would be accountable for the Enterprise Architecture for MSF.

Your responsibilities will include:

  • Defining your team’s engineering vision, strategy, and directions that align with the organization’s overall vision and goals.
  • Partnering with stakeholders and agencies to prioritize and manage roadmaps for delivering product vision incrementally and efficiently.

  • Creating a long-term technical plan and roadmap for the assigned team, and for other teams as needed. Updating the roadmap regularly to meet future product/business needs and infrastructure requirements.

  • Regularly engage with engineers in conversations about their performance, professional growth, offering constructive feedback, mentoring, and guidance as needed.

  • Working with cross-functional partners to develop quality, reliability, and security strategies within your team.

  • Proactively identifying and initiating worthwhile engineering initiatives to maximize our impact on the public good through developing and evolving the enterprise architecture.

  • Establishing practices and tools to encourage a strong engineering culture and maximize engineering effectiveness and impact.

  • Establish and drive the MSF Engineering Landscape — building engineering visibility and standards from the ground up across a portfolio of 30+ systems and multiple vendors.   • Helping with recruitment, particularly for more senior hires.

  • Experience working on reasonably large, high-availability software systems that have operated at scale.

  • Experience in modern full-stack web application technologies; experience in modern front-end engineering like Next.js/React.js and Java/TypeScript on the backend is preferred.

  • Have a strong sense of what it means to be a technical leader, particularly as it relates to the suite of soft skills beyond just your technical chops.

  • Passion for engineering excellence; you deeply understand that software engineering is a team sport, and you take pride in the craftsmanship aspect of software engineering.

  • Passion for working in the public interest.

  • The ability to guide a team to achieve important goals together.

  • 2+ years of experience coaching developers, helping them enhance their performance and grow their careers.

About Ministry of Social and Family Development (MSF)

The Ministry of Social and Family Development (MSF) is Singapore's lead ministry for social and family policies, working to build a caring and inclusive society where every individual and family can thrive.

We deliver essential services through our network of Family Service Centres, Social Service Offices, and industry partners, supporting vulnerable populations across all life stages. Our work encompasses family services, early childhood, social assistance, disability support, mental health initiatives, and community development programmes.

We are actively expanding our team to drive impact for our key initiatives including:

  • Case Central for integrated case management for Social Service Sector
  • ComLink+ for enhanced community support programme
  • Social Service Coordination Centre for Whole-of-Govt insights
  • Early Childhood programmes that give our youngest citizens the best start in life
  • Sectors Futures Technology to look ahead on sectoral digitalisation and transformation efforts

These innovative platforms represent our commitment to leveraging technology and coordinated approaches to better serve Singaporeans.

Join our dedicated team of professionals and help make a meaningful difference in the lives of Singaporeans as we work together to nurture strong families and build resilient communities.

[What you will be working on]

GovTech is seeking a motivated Product Manager to lead the development and enhancement of a critical government platforms. This role involves defining product vision, strategy, and roadmap to deliver impactful digital services to citizens.

As a Product Manager, you will hold significant ownership over your product domain, driving it from concept to reality while aligning with the broader programme goals. Your core responsibilities include:

  • Define and articulate the product vision, strategy, and roadmap, aligning with GovTech's strategic objectives and citizen needs.

  • Conduct thorough user research, market analysis, and competitive landscape assessment to identify opportunities and inform product decisions.

  • Translate user needs and business requirements into detailed product specifications, user stories, and acceptance criteria.

  • Prioritize features and initiatives based on impact, feasibility, and strategic alignment, managing the product backlog effectively.

  • Collaborate closely with engineering, design, and other cross-functional teams throughout the product lifecycle, from ideation to launch and post-launch optimization.

  • Communicate product updates, progress, and challenges to stakeholders at various levels.

  • Monitor product performance using key metrics, analyze data, and iterate on features to continuously improve the user experience and achieve desired outcomes.

  • Manage stakeholder expectations and foster strong relationships with government agencies and external partners.

  • Stay abreast of emerging technologies and industry best practices to identify innovative solutions

[What we are looking for]

  • Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Business, or a related field.

  • Minimum of 5 years of experience in product management, preferably within a government or public sector context.

  • Proven track record of successfully launching and managing digital products.

  • Strong understanding of agile methodologies and product development frameworks.

  • Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and decision-making skills.

  • Exceptional communication, presentation, and interpersonal skills, with the ability to influence and engage diverse stakeholders.

  • Experience with user research, A/B testing, and data-driven product development.

  • Familiarity with government digital services and the Singapore public service landscape is a plus.

  • Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced environment.

What we offer you:

GovTech is an equal opportunity employer committed to fostering an inclusive workplace that values diverse voices and perspectives, as we believe that diversity is the foundation to innovation.   

Our employee benefits are based on a total rewards approach, offering a holistic and market-competitive suite of perks. These include leave benefits to meet your work-life needs and employee wellness programs. 

We champion flexible work arrangements (subject to your job role) and trust that you will manage your own time to deliver your best, wherever you are, and whatever works best for you. 

Please note that this will be a 2-year fixed term contract.

[What you will be working on]

As a Data Engineer, you will be responsible for designing, developing, and maintaining robust data infrastructure that enables analytics and insights across the organisation. You will work closely with business stakeholders, data analysts, and other technical teams to deliver scalable data solutions that support data-driven decision making. Your job scope includes (but not limited to) the following:
Requirements Analysis and Solution Design: Collaborate with business users, data analysts, and stakeholders to elicit and document requirements for analytics use cases. Translate business requirements into technical specifications and design optimal data architecture solutions that align with organisational data strategy and governance frameworks.
Data Pipeline Architecture and Design: Design end-to-end data pipelines from various source systems to different layers within the data analytics platform, including bronze (raw), silver (technical), and gold (business) layers. Ensure pipeline designs follow best practices for scalability, reliability, and maintainability whilst adhering to data governance and security requirements.
Data Pipeline Development and Implementation: Develop, test, and deploy data pipelines using modern data engineering tools and frameworks. Implement data transformation logic, quality checks, and error handling mechanisms to ensure reliable data processing. Build automated workflows that can handle both batch and real-time data processing requirements.
Data Infrastructure Maintenance and Optimisation: Monitor and maintain existing data pipelines to ensure optimal performance, reliability, and cost-effectiveness. Troubleshoot data quality issues, resolve pipeline failures, and implement performance improvements. Conduct regular reviews of data infrastructure to identify opportunities for enhancement and modernisation.
Data Modelling and Architecture: Design and implement appropriate data models including star schema, snowflake schema, and wide table structures based on analytical requirements. Implement Slowly Changing Dimension (SCD) strategies to handle historical data changes effectively whilst maintaining data integrity and analytical accuracy.
Collaboration and Knowledge Sharing: Work collaboratively with cross-functional teams including data analysts, data scientists, and business stakeholders to ensure data solutions meet business needs. Contribute to the development of data engineering best practices and standards within the organisation.

[What we are looking for]

Experience:

  • Minimum 3-5 years of experience in data engineering, data analytics, or related technical field, with demonstrated experience in designing and implementing data pipelines and analytics solutions

Skills:

  • Data Architecture and Modelling: Strong knowledge of data modelling concepts including star schema, snowflake schema, and wide table designs. Proficient in implementing Slowly Changing Dimension (SCD) patterns and understanding their appropriate use cases for maintaining historical data accuracy.
  • Data Platform Technologies: Comprehensive understanding of modern data architecture patterns including Data Lake, Data Warehouse, Data Lakehouse, and Data Mesh concepts. Ability to evaluate and recommend appropriate architectural approaches based on business requirements and technical constraints.
  • Technical Programming Skills: Proficient in Python programming language with experience in data manipulation libraries (pandas, numpy), data pipeline frameworks, and API development. Strong SQL skills for data querying, transformation, and database management across various database platforms.
  • Data Engineering Practices: Solid foundation in data engineering principles including data ingestion, transformation, quality assurance, and pipeline orchestration. Knowledge of data analysis techniques and data management practices including data governance, lineage tracking, and metadata management.
  • Cloud and Platform Experience: Knowledge of Databricks and AWS cloud services will be an advantage, including experience with cloud-based data processing, storage solutions, and managed analytics services. Familiarity with infrastructure-as-code and DevOps practices for data platforms.
  • Problem-Solving and Communication: Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to troubleshoot complex data issues and optimise system performance. Excellent communication skills to effectively collaborate with both technical and non-technical stakeholders, and ability to translate business requirements into technical solutions.

[About the Team]

The Government Technology Agency (GovTech) aims to transform the delivery of Government digital services by taking an “outside-in” view, putting citizens and businesses at the heart of everything we do. We also develop the Smart Nation infrastructure and applications and facilitate collaboration between citizens and businesses to co-develop technologies.

Join us as we support Singapore’s vision of building a Smart Nation - a nation of possibilities empowered through infocommunication technology and related engineering.

Experimental Systems and Technology Lab (ESTL) of GovTech is an engineering team within the Ministry of Education (MOE). We are made up of engineers, user experience designers and education officers. Our team aims to design and develop software applications that help MOE to transform their systems and services through digitalisation and innovation. You can find out more about us at https://www.estl.edu.sg/.

[What you will be working on]

As a Senior Software Engineer at ESTL, you will architect the digital foundations that enable the Ministry of Education (MOE) to realize its core mission: Moulding the future of our nation.

Imagine transforming the way hundreds of thousands of users access the suite of digital systems in MOE. This is your chance to join a team building a next-generation platform that delivers seamless, secure, and passwordless digital experiences at national scale.

You’ll also work on innovative authentication solutions, including modern technologies like facial recognition, making access simpler and safer for students, teachers, and staff.

This is more than software—it’s creating real impact that thousands experience every day.

Your responsibilities include:

  • Feature Leadership & Delivery: Lead a squad of 2–4 SWEs to design, develop, and maintain IAM/IGA capabilities such as authentication, authorization, identity lifecycle management (joiner–mover–leaver), access provisioning, and certification workflows, ensuring secure and scalable implementations.
  • Technical Excellence & Standards: Write clean, maintainable, and well-tested code, and contribute to improving engineering practices within the team. Provide mentorship and technical guidance to junior and mid-level engineers within the team.
  • Cross-Functional Leadership: Work closely with Product Managers and UX Designers to understand requirements and deliver effective solutions with a strong user experience.
  • Architecture & System Design: Contribute to architectural decisions and system design discussions, ensuring solutions are scalable, resilient, and aligned with long-term platform strategy.
  • Code Quality: Participate in code reviews and uphold engineering standards within the team. Promote maintainable design, test coverage, and secure coding practices.
  • DevOps, Scalability & Reliability: Support CI/CD pipelines and cloud deployment processes for services owned by the team. Contribute to operational stability through monitoring, incident support, and reliability improvements for assigned services.
  • Agile Leadership & Delivery Excellence: Actively participate in agile ceremonies including sprint planning, estimation, and retrospectives. Contribute to predictable delivery of features and continuous improvement of team execution practices.

[What we are looking for]

  • Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related field.
  • 8+ years of software engineering experience, including 3–5 years in technical leadership roles.
  • Proven ability to deliver large-scale, high-impact systems or platforms.
  • Strong technical foundation with experience in modern architectures (e.g. cloud-native, microservices, CI/CD, DevOps).
  • Strong analytical mindset with the ability to troubleshoot complex system issues and optimize performance.
  • Excellent communication skills and a desire to work in a collaborative, cross-functional environment.
  • A passion for learning new technologies and a commitment to fostering a culture of technical excellence and inclusion.

Overall we look for people who have ability, initiative, good communication, and strong values.
