Join us in modernising the technology that supports Singapore's democratic processes! As a Senior Software Engineer at Elections Department (ELD), you will play the role of a technical lead in one of our squads and work on products that directly support Singaporeans to participate in elections. You'll be modernising and enhancing critical electoral systems that ensure secure, efficient, and transparent elections for our nation.

This is a unique opportunity to:

  • Build technology solutions that serve millions of Singaporeans during elections
  • Work on high-impact projects that strengthen our electoral system
  • Collaborate with a dedicated team modernising Singapore's electoral infrastructure
  • Contribute to systems that must meet the highest standards of security, reliability, and public trust

Your responsibilities will include:

  • Taking an active role in team delivery of product and engineering goals.
  • Delivering high-quality, maintainable, and reusable software systems.
  • Making technical contributions, and mentoring and guiding junior software engineers by coaching and mentoring through engineering best practices.
  • Collaborating with Product Manager towards achievement of high-priority initiatives (to maximize our impact on the public good)
  • Establishing practices and tools to encourage a strong engineering culture and maximize engineering effectiveness and impact.
  • Strengthening how the team builds software through solution design works, constructive code/design reviews and enhancing quality, reliability, security and DevOps practices
  • Proactively identify and manage cross-team technical dependencies, coordinating with other engineering teams and stakeholders toto accelerate product outcomes.
  • Proactively refactor/reduce technical debt for long term maintainability.
  • Contributing to create a long-term technical plan and roadmap for the assigned squads, and for other squads as needed. Updating the roadmap regularly to meet future product/business needs and infrastructure requirements.

[What we are looking for]

  • Experience working on reasonably large, high-availability software systems that have operated at scale.
  • Experience in modern full-stack web application technologies; experience in modern front-end engineering on Next.Js/React.js/Angular/TypeScript and backend development with .Net or Node.js/TypeScript is preferred.
  • Experience with cloud infrastructure and DevOps practices; familiarity with AWS deployment, Terraform, Docker, CI/CD pipelines, cloud-native architecture, or container orchestration would be advantageous.
  • Have a strong sense of what it means to be a technical leader, particularly in leading technical decision-making, conducting code reviews, and driving architectural discussions
  • Passion towards engineering excellence; you deeply understand that software engineering is a team sport, and you take pride in the craftsmanship aspect of software engineering.

The Ministry of Finance (MOF) is responsible for the Government Procurement (GP) policies, which govern how government agencies conduct their procurement. With evolving needs of our public officers and changing procurement landscape, MOF is embarking on the Whole of Government Procurement initiative.

GeBIZ X is the MOF product that would transform the procurement and business experience for WOG public officers and external suppliers.

Our team is made up of passionate individuals committed to solving challenges and driving meaningful change. We embrace diversity of thought, encourage experimentation, and foster a culture of continuous learning. As part of this team, you will lead design direction and collaborate with UX designers, finance officers, and solution providers — forward-thinking professionals equally invested in your growth and in transforming MOF’s corporate finance services.

We are seeking a dynamic and strategic Lead Designer to lead our product team. This individual will play a pivotal role in shaping the vision, strategy, and execution of GeBIZ X product portfolio. The Lead Designer will collaborate with cross-functional teams to drive product innovation, ensure market fit, and deliver exceptional value to our customers.

The Lead Designer leads strategic design initiatives while ensuring alignment with organizational goals. He/She coordinates and plans discovery research, including user interviews, workshops, surveys, usability testing, and gathering insights to understand user needs and pain points; evaluates the usability of new and existing products and makes constructive suggestions for change. Key responsibilities include translating discovery research into actionable insights, creating wireframes and prototypes in Figma, conducting user testing, iterating designs based on feedback, and collaborating closely with product manager, software engineer to deliver user-centric solutions within technical constraints.

Define and Drive Procurement Design Strategy, Roadmap, and Outcomes

  • Develop and own the design vision, strategy, and multi-year roadmap for end-to-end procurement systems, aligned with MOF's digitalisation priorities.

  • Establish, track, and deliver on design OKRs and success metrics that drive user adoption, operational impact, and continuous improvement.

  • Prioritise design initiatives that maximise value creation and usability for procurement stakeholders, from requisition through contract management and vendor relations.

Enhance Procurement Operations through Human-Centred Design and Systems Thinking

  • Strengthen procurement workflows by embedding user research and data-driven design decisions to support strategic sourcing, vendor evaluation, and contract lifecycle management.

  • Redesign procurement interfaces and experiences through systematic design standards that enable intuitive, multi-stakeholder collaboration across complex technology solutions (eg. SaaS/ Bespoke).

  • Implement design systems and methodologies that proactively identify user friction points and reduce operational risk through detection of usability barriers in procurement processes.

Coach and Develop the Procurement Design Practice

  • Mentor and develop designers and design researchers to build internal capabilities and foster a high-performance, user-focused design culture.

  • Promote cross-functional collaboration between design, product, and engineering teams whilst evangelising design-led problem-solving across the finance and digital teams.

  • Lead the development of design standards, frameworks, and measurement methodologies that demonstrate design impact beyond traditional product metrics in complex government procurement contexts.

Required qualifications, skills and mindset:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Industrial design, Interaction design, Human Computer Interaction (HCI), Visual Arts or similar; Master’s degree preferred.

  • Minimum of 8 years of experience in design across multiple industries, with demonstrated success in launching and scaling complex digital products.

  • Proven track record of leading end-to-end design initiatives from discovery through delivery, with measurable impact on user adoption and business outcomes.

  • Strategic design thinking with strong analytical and synthetic capabilities, including the ability to navigate ambiguity and drive design initiatives that align with organisational objectives.

  • Experience with agile methodologies and full product lifecycle management, particularly in complex enterprise software environments.

  • Demonstrated ability to influence senior stakeholders and cross-functional teams, translating user needs into strategic design decisions that balance business goals with user outcomes.

  • Expert-level proficiency in design methodology with exceptional attention to detail and deep understanding of how users engage with complex digital systems.

  • Advanced skills in design and research tools including Figma, Miro, and project management platforms such as Jira.

  • Strong foundation in design principles, typography, and accessibility standards (WCAG), with experience applying these in government or regulated environments.

  • Passion for public good is a strong plus.

Key Competencies :

  • Product Leadership: Ability to define strategy, own outcomes, and lead multi-year product execution.

  • Data-Driven Decision-Making: Able to translate data into actionable insights and financial intelligence.

  • User-Centric Thinking: Designs with users in mind, balancing operational workflows with intuitive UX.

  • Systems Thinking: Comfortable working across a complex organisation with diverse stakeholders and processes.

  • Coaching & Team Growth: Skilled in mentoring others and building high-performing product teams.

job_title: Senior Software Engineer, AI Infrastructure & Operations (AI Practice)
job_description:
GovTech is the lead agency driving Singapore’s Smart Nation initiatives and public sector digital transformation. As the Centre of Excellence for Infocomm Technology and Smart Systems (ICT & SS), GovTech develops the Singapore Government’s capabilities in Data Science & Artificial Intelligence, Application Development, Smart City Technology, Digital Infrastructure, and Cybersecurity.

Within the AI Practice, the AI Infrastructure & Operations (AI Infra & Ops) team defines the reference architecture and best practices for running AI at scale. Rather than owning a single product, we work across multiple agencies and use cases — helping teams stand up production-grade AI infrastructure, establishing governance standards, and building reusable platform components that accelerate adoption government-wide.

As a Senior AI Infra & Ops Engineer, you will be a technical leader and trusted advisor — shaping architectural standards, uplifting engineering capability across agencies, and ensuring that AI workloads across government are production-ready, cost-efficient, and secure. You will operate at the intersection of deep technical expertise and stakeholder engagement, working with a diverse set of agency teams at varying levels of AI maturity.

At GovTech, we offer you a purposeful career to make lives better where we empower our people to master their craft through robust learning and development opportunities all year round.

Play a part in Singapore’s vision to build a Smart Nation and embark on your meaningful journey to build tech for public good. Join us to advance our mission and shape your future with us today!

Learn more about GovTech at tech.gov.sg.

[What you will be working on]

You will work across multiple agencies and projects simultaneously, combining hands-on engineering with advisory and enablement work. You will be responsible for:

You will bridge the gap between experimental data science and production-grade engineering, working across multiple agencies and use cases rather than a single product. You will be responsible for:

  • MLOps & Lifecycle Management: Design, build, and maintain automated CI/CD pipelines for ML models. Establish whole-of-government standards for model versioning, automated testing, canary deployments, and rollback strategies. Build reusable pipeline components and templates that agencies can adopt to move toward self-service model delivery.

  • Model Optimization & Quantization: Apply and benchmark advanced techniques (e.g., quantization, pruning, distillation, speculative decoding) to optimize LLMs and other deep learning models for inference. Evaluate inference engines (vLLM, TensorRT-LLM, Triton Inference Server) and publish recommendations balancing performance, cost, and resilience. Provide hands-on support to agency teams applying these to their workloads.

  • Infrastructure & Platform Architecture: Architect scalable, multi-tenant AI infrastructure (on-prem and cloud) to support training and inference workloads, with a focus on GPU orchestration (e.g., Kubernetes, Volcano, Run:ai, NVIDIA GPU Operator). Define reference architectures, capacity planning guidance, and FinOps practices that agencies can adopt.

  • Observability & Monitoring: Design and champion observability standards for AI systems — covering model health, data/concept drift, system latency, GPU telemetry, and cost attribution. Define SLO/SLI frameworks to ensure AI services are transparent, debuggable, and maintainable at scale.

  • Security & Governance: Collaborate with Responsible AI and cybersecurity stakeholders to embed security guardrails and compliance checks into standardized deployment pipelines, ensuring AI systems meet government security standards (e.g., IM8, CSA guidelines). Drive governance frameworks covering model lineage, reproducibility, and approval workflows.

  • Technical Consulting & Enablement: Act as a senior subject matter expert for government agencies, conducting architecture reviews, maturity assessments, and providing actionable roadmaps to scale AI from POC to production. Develop reusable playbooks, reference implementations, and training materials. Mentor engineers within the AI Practice and across partner agencies — the goal is to build self-sufficient teams.

[What we are looking for]

  • Education: Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, or a related technical field. Postgraduate study or research in distributed systems, ML systems, or high-performance computing is a plus.

  • Experience: At least 6–8 years of progressive experience in DevOps, MLOps, Cloud Infrastructure, or Platform Engineering, with a significant portion focused on AI/ML workloads in production environments. Experience in a consultancy, platform team, or CoE setting — where you've had to advise and enable multiple teams rather than just build for one — is highly valued.

  • Core Technical Skills:

    • Strong proficiency in Python and shell scripting; working knowledge of at least one systems language (Go, Rust, etc.) is a plus.

    • Deep, hands-on expertise with containerization and orchestration (Docker, Kubernetes) in production settings, including experience operating multi-tenant clusters.

    • Proven experience designing MLOps platforms and patterns using frameworks such as Kubeflow, MLflow, BentoML, or equivalent tooling.

    • Advanced Infrastructure-as-Code (IaC) skills with Terraform or Ansible — including module design, state management, and CI/CD integration for infrastructure.

    • Experience with GitOps workflows (ArgoCD) and platform engineering patterns (internal developer platforms, self-service portals, golden paths).

  • Domain Expertise:

    • Strong understanding of GPU hardware and performance tuning for distributed training and inference.

    • Hands-on experience with LLM serving stacks (vLLM, TensorRT-LLM, Triton Inference Server, KServe) and model optimization techniques (quantization, distillation, pruning).

    • Expertise in observability stacks (Prometheus, Grafana, OpenTelemetry, ELK/Loki) and ML-specific monitoring tools (Arize, Langfuse, etc.).

    • Solid understanding of networking fundamentals and service mesh architectures.

  • Consulting & Leadership Skills:

    • Demonstrated ability to engage with multiple stakeholder teams at varying levels of technical maturity and tailor advice accordingly. You can be equally effective whiteboarding with senior engineers and presenting infrastructure roadmaps to agency leadership.

    • Experience conducting architecture reviews, writing technical guidance documents, and developing reusable standards that others adopt.

    • Track record of mentoring engineers and building capability in others — the measure of success is teams that become self-sufficient, not teams that stay dependent on you.

    • Strong written and verbal communication skills. You can produce clear, concise technical documentation and present confidently to both technical and non-technical audiences.

    • A proactive, systems-thinking mindset — you anticipate failure modes across diverse environments and can triage the highest-impact recommendations when agency teams have limited bandwidth.

    • Passion for building public good through scalable, well-governed technology.

  • Preferred / Bonus:

    • Experience managing large-scale GPU clusters or cloud AI services in production.

    • Prior experience in a consulting, professional services, or internal platform/CoE role serving multiple product teams.

    • Contributions to open-source projects in the MLOps, Kubernetes, or AI infrastructure space.

    • Certifications in Cloud Architecture or Kubernetes .

    • Experience with cost optimization at scale — FinOps practices, spot/preemptible instance strategies, and resource right-sizing for GPU workloads.

    • Familiarity with emerging paradigms: multi-modal model serving, agentic AI infrastructure, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) pipelines, and edge inference.

About the Consulting Practice:

The Consulting Practice within the Government Technology Office of GovTech helps agencies resolve complex IT challenges, modernize systems and develop innovative solutions with confidence. We bring together deep engineering, product, and design expertise to assess legacy environments, align stakeholders, and shape multi-year solutions that are human-centric, scalable, and delivered by GovTech and industry partners.

Each Consulting team member is a leader in their domain and works collaboratively with GovTech teams and agencies to address the government’s most critical technology challenges. As a founding member, you’ll also shape this growing practice while working on diverse and impactful problems in the public sector.

[What you will be working on]

As a Principal / Lead Designer in the Innovation team, you will lead high-impact innovation engagements end-to-end, spanning research, problem definition, ideation & prototyping, solution design, visioning and running of proof of concepts (POCs). This role is ideal for someone with extensive experience in accelerating the development of new digital products or services, who is excited to operate at the intersection of public sector, emerging technologies and designing user experiences for the era of AI.

You’ll apply strong research, experience design, prototyping, storytelling with consulting acumen to shape solutions that are not just desirable, but also feasible and truly impactful.

  • Manage design x innovation engagements end-to-end, from discovery to development of working prototypes and proof of concepts (POCs)

  • Drive innovation activities from research, strategic framing, to hands-on prototype building

  • Provide thought leadership, strategies and hands on support to agency product teams to level up their innovation capabilities

  • Facilitate co-creation between agency, GovTech and industry partners to bring emerging technologies to public sector use cases

  • Build the innovation playbook, combining methodologies, tools, templates and case studies that enable GovTech teams to deliver better innovation outcomes

  • Act as a coach and advocate of user-centred, innovation practices

You’ll thrive here if you:

  • Embody a culture of experimentation and learning

  • Comfortable navigating uncertainty, quick to adapt when things change

  • Can shift fluidly between research, design, prototyping and storytelling

  • Are energised by working in ambiguous spaces at the edge of what is possible

  • Focus on outcomes and long-term value, not just deliverables

  • Influence through building trust, clarity, and collaboration

What we are looking for

  • 10+ years in experience design, innovation consultancy, UX or adjacent fields such as UX engineering or creative technology

  • Proven ability to lead projects in complex, multi-stakeholder environments, working with and managing team members across disciplines (design, tech, ops and policy) to bring innovative solutions to life

  • Use a variety of research, design methods and prototyping tools flexibility to validate ideas

  • Strong communicator and storyteller who builds trust quickly and brings people together

  • Experienced at navigating ambiguity and balancing user, technical, and operational needs

  • Inspire through articulating a bold vision of what’s possible with technology while also clearly communicating risks and practical considerations

  • Passionate mentor who fosters curiosity, learning, and systems thinking

[About the role]

The Product Policy function sits at the critical intersection of government policy and digital product delivery. It enables tech teams to succeed in a complex public sector environment by:

  • Guiding product teams through regulatory and policy considerations / constraints; and
  • Supporting public agencies to understand how technology can improve public service delivery

As a Product Policy Specialist (Individual Contributor), you will play a key role in shaping policy approaches that enable good technology outcomes. You will work directly with internal product teams and public agencies to identify blockers, develop pragmatic policy strategies, and help align digital products with regulatory, operational, and public interest needs.

Join us if you are excited about working in the nascent tech policy space and to strategically influence how tech, policy and ops are effectively deployed in the public service.

[What you will be working on]

Policy-Product Integration

  • Translate policy constraints / intent into actionable guidance for product teams
  • Help agencies understand how product design choices intersect with public policy goals
  • Advise on trade-offs between user experience, compliance, and government outcomes

Strategic Problem Solving

  • Identify systemic barriers to effective digital delivery (e.g. outdated policies, fragmented approvals)
  • Co-develop solutions with internal and external stakeholders that balance compliance with innovation
  • Use systems thinking and backcasting to assess long-term implications of policy-product decisions

Stakeholder Engagement

  • Work closely with product managers, designers, engineers, legal, and policy teams
  • Collaborate with public agencies to align on project objectives and delivery conditions
  • Communicate clearly across technical and non-technical audiences

Writing & Communication

  • Craft memos, policy notes, and communications that frame product issues in clear, policy-aligned terms
  • Present recommendations to internal leads and external partners with clarity and persuasion

[What we are looking for]

  • Government Acumen: Understands how regulations, governance processes, and public accountability shape digital delivery. Familiarity with Singapore’s public sector or similar operating contexts is a plus.
  • Product Orientation: Comfortable working with digital product teams, understanding product life cycles, and prioritising user needs. Able to assess how tech decisions can drive or hinder policy outcomes.
  • Systems Thinker: Able to anticipate downstream impacts, navigate ambiguity, and identify root causes in complex systems.
  • Initiative & Ownership: Self-starter who can independently scope and drive initiatives. Comfortable making progress in unclear or fast-evolving contexts.
  • Strong Communicator: Excellent written and verbal communication. Able to synthesise complexity and convey compelling recommendations.
  • 6–10 years of experience in public policy, product strategy, regulatory affairs, or related fields

[What you will be working on]

AI Practice is the AI R&D and capability development arm of GovTech's Government Technology Office (GTO). As a Data Scientist (Multimodal AI) on the Multimodal AI Squad, your work will span two broad areas:

Frontier exploration and evaluation. Systematically scanning, experimenting with, and interpreting the latest AI developments across vision, video, audio, and document modalities. This includes producing fast-response technical explorations when meaningful new capabilities emerge, as well as deeper assessments of maturing areas. The emphasis is on getting your hands dirty. This is empirical work, not editorial.

Agency engagement and transformation. Translating frontier AI capabilities into real outcomes for government agencies. The squad builds AI capabilities through rapid experimentation and works with agencies to identify and test promising capabilities against their own data and use cases, with viable solutions graduating onward for productisation. You will support the journey from discovery through experimentation and prototyping.

Day to day, you can expect to:

  • Experiment with state-of-the-art multimodal models (VLMs, MLLMs, diffusion models, speech and audio models) and evaluate their applicability to government use cases and strategy
  • Design, build, and run evaluations and benchmarks for AI models on real-world Singapore government tasks
  • Build functional prototypes and proof-of-concept solutions for agency engagements spanning smart city video analytics, document intelligence, generative media, and content authentication
  • Stay current with the latest developments in applied AI and assess what translates from research settings to practical government applications
  • Support the development of our multimodal AI showcase and prototyping platforms
  • Produce technical write-ups and contribute to knowledge artifacts such as handbooks, reports, and evaluation findings

[What we are looking for]

We are looking for a data scientist who sits at the intersection of exploration and applied AI. Someone who can pick up a new model or technique, understand how it works, and turn it into something users can actually try.

Technical Foundation

  • Degree in Computer Science, Data Science, AI/ML, Engineering, or a related field
  • Proficiency in Python and familiarity with deep learning frameworks (PyTorch preferred)
  • Solid understanding of machine learning fundamentals, including model training, evaluation methodology, and experiment design
  • Foundational knowledge of computer vision concepts and/or experience working with image, video, or audio data
  • Interest in or exposure to multimodal AI systems, vision-language models, or generative AI
  • Comfortable reading technical documentation and staying current with new developments in applied AI

AI-Augmented Proficiency

  • Comfortable using AI-assisted development tools (e.g., Claude Code, Cursor, OpenAI Codex) to accelerate your work
  • Able to critically evaluate AI-generated outputs rather than accepting them blindly
  • Treats AI as a collaborator, using it to learn new libraries, debug, and move faster

Mindset

  • Curious and outcome-oriented. You enjoy going deep on a problem but ultimately care about whether it works in practice
  • Intellectually driven. You try new models and stay current because you genuinely enjoy it
  • Comfortable with ambiguity. Government AI work means scoping problems that aren't always well-defined
  • Strong communicator. You can explain what you built and why it matters to both technical peers and non-technical agency stakeholder

job_title: Director / Deputy Director, Engineering Management (Product Development Office)
job_description:
GovTech is the lead agency driving Singapore’s Smart Nation initiatives and public sector digital transformation. As the Centre of Excellence for Infocomm Technology and Smart Systems (ICT & SS), GovTech develops the Singapore Government’s capabilities in Data Science & Artificial Intelligence, Application Development, Smart City Technology, Digital Infrastructure, and Cybersecurity.

At GovTech, we offer you a purposeful career to make lives better where we empower our people to master their craft through robust learning and development opportunities all year round.

Play a part in Singapore’s vision to build a Smart Nation and embark on your meaningful journey to build tech for public good. Join us to advance our mission and shape your future with us today!

[Background]

Product Development Offices (PDOs) is a new construct within the GDT CTOF to bring together Product, Design, and Engineering leadership to help Ministries and their agencies deliver digital products faster, more securely, and with better user outcomes.

Each PDO partners closely with a Ministry CIO/CD, supports multiple agency product teams, and connects those teams back to GovTech’s shared platforms and practices.

The PDO Engineering Leader represents modern *product engineering* - building teams that can move confidently from problem discovery and early experiments to scalable, reliable, user-centred digital services. They operate as the "Head of Engineering" for the Ministry, shaping outcome-focused delivery and platform-aligned technical decisions across agencies.

Beyond guiding day-to-day delivery, PDO Engineering Leaders shape the Ministry’s technical direction through a product mindset — ensuring coherence across systems, promoting reuse and shared value, avoiding unnecessary complexity, and lifting engineering capability across both internal and vendor teams. They steer teams toward clear problem definitions, measurable outcomes, and evidence-based decisions.

The Engineering Lead in a Product Development Office (PDO) is a modern technical leader - the "Head of Engineering" for a Ministry - someone who’s built, designed, or operated real systems, understands how technology decisions play out in practice, build capability, and can guide teams through the full journey from POC to scale.

[What you will be working on]

As Director/Deputy Director, you extend your leadership across multiple squads executing across multiple delivery threads (as manager of managers or across workstreams through tech leads) or a broader product (or infra) area, ensuring alignment, consistency, and impact across teams.

You oversee multiple squads (including internal staff and/or vendor teams) by setting a unified technical vision and enforcing multi-squad standards that drive measurable impact.

Your responsibilities include coaching EMs and Tech Leads, fostering cross-squad collaboration, and ensuring that your leadership structure remains lean and focused.

You are accountable for delivering consistent outcomes and high-quality solutions by balancing innovative initiatives with essential BAU improvements, making strategic decisions that always benefit the organisation's long-term objectives

Role and Responsibilities

  • Establish a unified architectural vision and technical standards that meets organisational requirements.

  • Set rigorous expectations for security, scalability, and advanced DevOps/SRE practices across squads.

  • Define and champion platform-wide architecture, tools, and practices that improve org-wide scalability, security, and velocity.

  • Ensure systems are sustainable, cost-effective, and adaptable to future needs.

  • Oversee design reviews and drive large-scale initiatives (e.g., major refactoring, system migrations) that yield quantifiable improvements.

  • Ensure that both innovative features and BAU work (tech debt reduction, bug fixes) contribute to efficiency gains and system reliability.

  • Oversee modernisation efforts or major platform migrations within your area of responsibility.

  • Balance innovation with operational excellence, reducing tech debt and delivery friction.

Ownership and Accountabilities

  • Own multi-squad outcomes by setting clear, measurable targets that support the organisation's long-term strategy.

  • Foster a culture of strong accountability where every deliverable meaningfully contributes to the organisation’s goals.

  • Drive engineering outcomes at scale — through delegation, system design, and strategic decisions.

  • Establish a culture of strong ownership where every decision balance organisational benefit, delivery needs, and long-term sustainability.

  • Shape org structures in collaboration with your leads to align talent with opportunity and enable sustainable delivery.

  • Swiftly remove blockers and refine processes to enable sustained delivery and measurable impact across squads.

  • Make strategic decisions that yield sustainable, quantifiable benefits for the organisation.

  • Deliver high-impact outcomes across divisions by swiftly removing blockers and strengthening systems.

  • Exercise decision-making authority with transparency and empathy, ensuring choices reflect the organisation’s values and serve long-term outcomes.

Strategic Alignment

Cultural and Organisational Influence

  • Technically credible — has coded, built, or designed production systems and can discuss implementation trade-offs with confidence.

  • Outcome-oriented — focuses teams on the real-world impact of the work, ensuring decisions link to user value, organisational goals, and public good.

  • Ecosystem thinker — balances the needs of individual product teams with Ministry-wide alignment, reusability, and capability growth.

  • Secure and reliable by design — incorporates these principles early, not after delivery.

job_description:
GovTech is the lead agency driving Singapore’s Smart Nation initiatives and public sector digital transformation. As the Centre of Excellence for Infocomm Technology and Smart Systems (ICT & SS), GovTech develops the Singapore Government’s capabilities in Data Science & Artificial Intelligence, Application Development, Smart City Technology, Digital Infrastructure, and Cybersecurity.

At GovTech, we offer you a purposeful career to make lives better where we empower our people to master their craft through robust learning and development opportunities all year round.

Learn more about GovTech at tech.gov.sg.

[What you will be working on]

We are seeking a Senior Product Designer to spearhead the design of Singapore Examination and Digital Assessment Board's (SEAB) reimagined digital platform for examination administration and digital assessment. This role will shape the user experience of a unified, sovereign system that seamlessly connects exam administration, authoring, delivery, and marking processes. The designer will create intuitive interfaces that uphold trust and fairness whilst enabling continuous innovation across SEAB's examination administration and digital assessment capabilities.

Key Responsibilities:

Design Unified Examination Administration and Assessment Experiences (Primary)

  • Lead the design of a cohesive user journey across examination registration, content authoring, exam delivery, marking, and results management — creating a productivity suite experience where different tools work seamlessly together.

  • Design intuitive interfaces for exam administrators, question authors, markers, and candidates that maintain consistency whilst serving distinct user needs and workflows.

  • Create clear visual representations of complex examination data, marking workflows, and administrative processes for both technical and non-technical users.

  • Translate SEAB's requirements for exam integrity, security, and fairness into user-centred design solutions that maintain trust whilst improving usability.

Establish Design Patterns for Examination Administration and Assessment (Secondary)

  • Develop comprehensive design systems, components, and interaction models that ensure consistency across all modules of the examination administration and digital assessment platform.

  • Design how users interact with assessment content, marking interfaces, administrative dashboards, and reporting tools in intuitive and meaningful ways.

  • Create design standards and guidelines that ensure accessibility, security compliance, and user-friendliness across the entire examination administration system.

  • Work closely with product and engineering teams to ensure design feasibility within the composable architecture framework.

Drive Service Design and User Research (Secondary)

  • Conduct extensive user research with exam administrators, question authors, markers, school leaders, and candidates to understand pain points and decision-making processes.

  • Facilitate workshops and ideation sessions with SEAB stakeholders to co-create solutions that address real-world examination administration and assessment challenges.

  • Apply service design methodologies to map user journeys across the entire examination lifecycle and identify opportunities for improvement.

  • Test and iterate designs through concept testing, user feedback sessions, and usability testing to ensure alignment with user needs and expectations.

What Success Looks Like

  • A unified examination administration and digital assessment platform where users can seamlessly navigate between exam administration, authoring, delivery, and marking with consistent, intuitive experiences.

  • Strong user adoption and satisfaction across all user groups, from exam administrators to candidates.

  • A robust design system that enables rapid development of new features whilst maintaining platform consistency and integrity.

  • Examination administration processes that are more efficient, transparent, and user-friendly whilst upholding SEAB's standards for trust and fairness.

  • Successful integration of innovative features (including AI tools) within a controlled, auditable environment that maintains exam security.

[Who we are looking for]

  • Extensive experience designing complex digital platforms, particularly in education technology, examination administration systems, or enterprise software suites.

  • Strong service design expertise with proficiency in user research, journey mapping, ideation, prototyping, and user testing methodologies.

  • Experience designing for multiple user types with varying technical capabilities and distinct workflow requirements.

  • Familiarity with security-conscious design principles and compliance requirements in regulated environments.

  • Excellent visual design skills with expertise in creating comprehensive design systems, information architecture, and interaction design.

  • Proficiency in industry-standard UX design tools such as Figma, Sketch, or Adobe XD.

  • Strong facilitation and communication skills, with ability to conduct workshops and clearly articulate design decisions to diverse stakeholders.

  • Experience working with product managers, engineers, and domain experts in complex, mission-critical systems.

  • Design thinking mindset with ability to balance innovation with reliability and security requirements.

We are seeking an experienced end-to-end Designer to join our Customs eServices (CES) team in designing end-to-end digital services, and developing a comprehensive design system for implementation by the development teams. Your role will involve collaborating with team members from Customs business branches, ITD and Government appointed contractors to ensure consistency and efficiency in design across various digital services. This is a unique opportunity to shape the future of Customs digital services and create meaningful impact for Singapore's trading community.

The Designer will be an integral part of the existing CES ITD team comprising Project Managers, Product Managers, Digital Business Analysts, and Software Engineers. The Designer reports to the Digital Service Manager for day-to-day work on-site. The key responsibilities are:

  • Design & Design System Leadership: Develop and maintain the Customs Design System, inclusive of UI/UX, in compliance with Government Digital Service Standards, ensuring consistency across all CES.

  • Research & Strategy: Conduct market research, validate hypotheses, and lead user journey mapping workshops to drive innovation in government-to-business and government-to-government services.

  • User Research & Discovery: Plan and execute comprehensive user research including interviews, surveys, usability testing, and workshops to understand user needs and inform design decisions.

  • Optimisation: Work with the eService owners and/or government appointed contractors to optimise eServices and business processes

  • Design & Prototyping: Create user flows, wireframes, and prototypes to validate requirements with business teams and test concepts with users, iterating based on feedback from stakeholders and technical teams.

  • Product Development & Operation: Work together with the technical teams on the design, development, continual enhancements, and CES operation support

  • Standards & Documentation: Create and maintain design standards, guidelines, templates, components, and comprehensive documentation. Publish design packages on developer portals for team circulation.

  • Collaboration & Implementation: Work closely with CES ITD team members to define problem statements and ideate solutions. Collaborate with developers and contractors to ensure design implementation meets specifications and supports Customs transformation initiatives.

  • Performance & Quality: Develop and monitor tracking metrics, participate in design reviews, provide constructive feedback, and stay current with digital design trends and best practices.

[What we are looking for]

Strong Design Skills, Decision-Making and Problem-Solving

  • Independently solve problems from initial brief to validated solution through research, testing, and iteration

  • Create accessible, well-designed solutions that work within technical and operational constraints

  • Make informed design decisions in complex situations and clearly explain your reasoning with evidence

  • Define problems as well as solve them, using user-centred approaches to guide project and product direction

  • Deliver high-quality designs for complex user journeys while balancing user needs, accessibility, and technical feasibility

  • Elevate product team performance through design critique, mentoring, and sound judgment in uncertain situations

Strong Sense of Ownership and Proactiveness

  • Take full responsibility for complex, ambiguous assignments from start to finish

  • Independently manage assigned work and deliver results with minimal supervision

  • Willing to make smart decisions, learn from outcomes, and adapt approach based on results

  • Mentor junior team members by sharing knowledge, breaking down complex problems, and providing helpful feedback

  • Spot issues early and address them proactively, knowing when to seek help from others

  • Identify improvement opportunities and recommend actionable next steps

Planning Contribution, Alignment and Prioritisation

  • Align your work with team goals and focus on high-impact tasks that deliver business value

  • Adapt quickly to changing priorities and work independently to meet objectives

  • Break down team goals into actionable plans and help shape platform and product strategy

  • Identify opportunities, analyse data, and provide clear recommendations that support organisational goals

  • Anticipate risks and challenges, taking steps to address them proactively

Contribute to Building Trust and Learning Culture

  • Guide cross-functional collaboration and collaborate effectively with team members and stakeholders to deliver results

  • Resolve disagreements constructively and facilitate alignment between different stakeholders

  • Respectfully challenge existing processes to drive improvements and lead change initiatives within the team

  • Share experiences and provide recommendations to enhance team performance and learning

Comfortable with People Management for Senior Designer:

  • Support team coordination by communicating simple project goals and priorities to team members and how individual tasks connect to broader objectives

  • Conduct check-ins with junior team members, focusing on immediate work progress and basic feedback delivery

  • Help team members with day-to-day blockers and provide technical mentoring

[What you will be working on]

GovTech Consulting embeds expert teams directly with government agencies to modernise services and build lasting in-house capability. We work at the intersection of design, engineering, product, and policy — and AI is changing how all of that fits together.

This role exists because we're at an inflection point. The way design teams work alongside product, engineering, data, and business is shifting fast — and we need someone to lead how we evolve. Not just adopt new tools, but continuously rethink workflows, run structured experiments across projects, carry learnings forward, and build the collaboration models that let every stream work better together.

What you'll do

  • Lead design across a portfolio of agency engagements — owning outcomes, supporting project teams, and driving modernisation across different domains and contexts
  • Shape how the design team's workflows and processes evolve with AI — running experiments across projects, carrying learnings forward, and turning what works into repeatable process
  • Build the collaboration model between design and adjacent streams: product, engineering, data, and business — using AI as a lever to improve how teams work together
  • Identify where new tools need to be built or adopted, from AI-assisted research and synthesis to new ways of prototyping and communicating decisions
  • Hire, mentor, and grow designers — creating the conditions for the team to do their best work across diverse and demanding engagements

You'll thrive here if

  • You treat workflow and process as real design problems — worth the same rigour as anything you'd ship to a user
  • You run experiments to get to outcomes, not just to learn — you close the loop and drive change based on what you find
  • You're as comfortable in the weeds of a project as you are setting direction across a portfolio
  • You get energy from other people's growth, and see building a strong team as some of the most important work you do

[What we are looking for]

  • 12+ years in design, with meaningful time at senior or lead level running teams and cross-functional programmes
  • A track record of shaping how design teams operate — not just delivering design work, but improving the workflows and processes around it
  • Genuine curiosity about AI and its implications for design workflows — you don't need to have all the answers, but you need to be actively working through the questions
  • Over the last one to two years, you've been actively experimenting: new workflows, new team shapes, new ways of collaborating — not just following the conversation, but running your own tests and forming your own views
  • Strong cross-functional credibility — someone who can work as a peer with product, engineering, and data leads, not just an advocate for design
  • Experience hiring, developing, and retaining designers across different disciplines and levels

What you can expect

  • A mandate to shape something genuinely new — this isn't a maintenance role, it's a build role
  • Real influence at the team level, with the seniority and space to make calls that matter
  • A team that takes design seriously and a leadership group that wants to understand what AI means for how we work — not just what it means for what we build

About Government Productivity Engineering (GPE) At GovTech
The Government Productivity Engineering (GPE) Group combines user-centric productivity tools with dependable, high-performance technology to create comprehensive solutions. The Group’s mission is to enhance national public services by improving infrastructure and equipping Public Officers with resources that support digital transformation. Through these efforts, GPE aims to raise productivity, flexibility and transparency for operations across all government agencies.

How we work:

The Customer Success Programme Manager is a role for a leader who will serve as the key liaison between GovTech and Agencies’ senior leadership to identify strategic opportunities, drive GovTech’s initiatives at Agencies on a longer-term, and be accountable to realise outcomes using GovTech’s products that would advance the ministry cluster’s digitalisation programme. This role combines strategic stakeholder management, customer success advocacy, and operational oversight to ensure effective deployment and utilisation of GovTech’s products.

He/She will partner with senior leadership to define strategies, and ensuring its alignment to organisational needs. He/She should be portrayed as a trusted thought partner with key stakeholders. The Ministry of Finance is responsible for the Government Procurement (GP) policies, which govern how government agencies conduct their procurement. With evolving needs of our public officers and changing procurement landscape, MOF is embarking on the Whole of Government Procurement initiative.

GeBIZ X is the MOF product that would transform the procurement and business experience for WOG public officers and external suppliers.

[Who we are looking for]

The Ministry of Finance is transforming government procurement through the Whole of Government Procurement initiative. GeBIZ X will revolutionise the procurement experience for WOG public officers and external suppliers.

We are seeking a Staff Platform Infrastructure Engineer to oversee the Enterprise Infrastructure of the GeBIZ X Product. You will demonstrate technical leadership in infrastructure engineering, designing, building, and operating the infrastructure ecosystem that comprises Ivalua SaaS as the Base Solution, seamlessly integrated with Extended Solutions including COTS and SaaS applications, connecting to government agency systems and the Singapore Government Tech Stack (SGTS). This orchestration layer, hosted on AWS Government Commercial Cloud (GCC) using API Gateway and SFTP protocols, serves as the critical integration hub.

You will be expected to navigate technical complexity across GovTech, Government agencies and technology partners, ensuring the interoperability of GeBIZ X and the wider network of systems whilst maintaining resilience, security, scalability, and compliance with government standards.

Core Values

Decision-making

  • When making important decisions, we identify overarching goals, weigh up our options, identify impact, and clearly communicate and manage the risks and trade-offs of our decision.

Ownership

  • In addition to technical responsibilities, this means having opinions on what is being done and having ideas on what should be done next. Building something that you believe in is the best way to build something good.

Continuous Learning

  • Working on new ideas often means not fully understanding what you are working on. Taking time to learn new architectures, frameworks, technologies, and even languages is not just encouraged but essential.

[What you will be working on]

Key Responsibilities

  • Full-Stack Infrastructure Engineering (AWS/GCC)
    Lead hands-on design and implementation of the complete platform ecosystem in AWS/GCC, including compute, storage, and specialised networking. Architect and manage API Gateway as central orchestrator governing traffic between external SaaS and internal government services.
  • COTS Implementation & SaaS Integration
    Lead installation, configuration, and maintenance of COTS products (e.g., e-Signature solutions like Sign with Singpass) within AWS/GCC. Engineer secure, high-performance bridges to external SaaS environments and establish monitoring standards across all layers.
  • DevSecOps & SHIP-HATS Integration
    Champion DevSecOps practices by driving SHIP-HATS ecosystem adoption (GitLab, SonarQube, etc.) to automate infrastructure delivery. Ensure strict adherence to IM8 policies and GovTech's "Security-by-Design" principles whilst building automation tools to reduce manual operations.
  • Strategic Alignment & Technical Leadership
    Develop roadmaps for complex infrastructure initiatives, coordinate across teams and external partners, manage technical risks proactively, and invest in team growth through mentorship and knowledge sharing.
  • Core Competencies
    Systems thinking for scalable ecosystem design spanning multiple environments, analytical leadership translating business problems to infrastructure solutions, effective stakeholder management across project teams and vendors, and self-driven ownership committed to leveraging technology for public good.

Technical Leadership Expectations

  • Engineer Resilient Platform Infrastructure at Scale
    Engineer resilient systems through deep understanding of infrastructure engineering and user behaviour. Tackle cross-cutting technical issues elegantly, establish high monitoring standards, and engineer systems with observability in mind to evaluate behaviour at scale.
  • Lead Complex Platform Infrastructure Transformations
    Apply systems thinking to improve efficiency, repeatability, and scale. Evolve infrastructure practices based on feedback and failure mode observations. Lead design, implementation, and launch of platform infrastructure services across multiple teams, navigating technical and organisational complexity with sound judgement.
  • Technical Leadership with Agency or WOG Impact
    Coordinate delivery across divisions ensuring alignment and quality. Proactively address potential failure points, collaborate with PMs/EMs at scale, and help unify organisational technical standards across teams.

Leadership Responsibilities

  • Strategic Execution
    Support divisional strategic agenda and drive key projects. Independently identify projects supporting strategic objectives, provide direction with strong outcome measurement ability, and develop roadmaps for complex initiatives.
  • Team Leadership
    Align team goals with departmental objectives, contribute to hiring standards, conduct regular 1:1s, provide growth opportunities, and create productive environments by removing barriers and motivating teams.
  • Cross-Team Collaboration
    Lead cross-functional collaboration across multiple teams, resolve stakeholder conflicts, facilitate solutions to achieve strategic outcomes, and champion continuous improvement whilst building capability in areas of expertise.
  • Mentorship
    Actively invest in peer and junior member growth through mentorship, sponsorship, and knowledge sharing. Mentor L3 and below officers beyond immediate team, measurably improving colleague capabilities.

[What we are looking for]

Experience & Education

  • Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, or related technical discipline. 8–10 years in Infrastructure Engineering, DevOps, or SRE with proven AWS/GCC and enterprise COTS experience.

Technical Skills

  • Infrastructure Orchestration: AWS API Gateway mastery including secure endpoints, rate limiting, and traffic routing
  • COTS Management: Commercial software installation and operationalisation in cloud environments
  • Infrastructure as Code: Expert Terraform proficiency for GCC-side ecosystem
  • Cloud & GCC: Deep AWS services (EKS, Lambda, RDS, IAM) and GCC landing zone expertise
  • Hybrid Integration: Secure connectivity patterns (VPNs, PrivateLink) and API-first architectures

Please note that this will be a 2-year fixed term contract.

About Ministry of Manpower (MOM) Enforcement

MOM's Enforcement Division ensures regulatory compliance across work pass licensing, migrant worker address management, and enforcement operations. The systems supporting these functions are critical to MOM's regulatory mandate, balancing policy objectives with operational efficiency and user needs across employers, enforcement officers, and migrant workers.

Design in Enforcement plays a strategic role in transforming fragmented legacy systems into coherent, efficient digital services that support complex workflows while maintaining regulatory rigor.

[What you will be working on]

As a Senior Designer for MOM Enforcement Division, you will shape end-to-end experiences across critical regulatory systems including license management, migrant worker address management, and enforcement workflows.

You will work closely with product managers, operations teams, policy officers, and engineering to translate complex regulatory requirements into usable, scalable digital experiences. This role requires balancing compliance constraints with user-centered design, operational efficiency, and long-term system coherence.

Your portfolio will include:

  • Licence management systems for work pass compliance
  • Migrant worker address management workflows
  • Enforcement operations and case management tool

Lead end-to-end design delivery across systems

  • Own product-level design from discovery through post-launch optimization, ensuring that enforcement systems are usable, coherent, and operationally effective while meeting policy and compliance requirements.

  • Conduct discovery using appropriate methods - user interviews, stakeholder workshops, operational shadowing, journey mapping, and data analysis to deeply understand enforcement workflows, pain points, and opportunities for improvement.

  • Map service ecosystems and end-to-end journeys across multiple systems and touchpoints, identifying systemic gaps, friction points, and opportunities to improve both user experience and operational efficiency.

  • Bridge business, operations, policy, and technology by facilitating alignment across cross-functional teams. Translate complex regulatory, operational, and technical constraints into clear design direction and actionable solutions.

  • Drive design direction by analyzing research findings, synthesizing insights, and defining design principles that guide product decisions while balancing user needs with regulatory and operational realities.

  • Rapidly prototype and iterate using modern tools and AI-assisted workflows (e.g., Figma, Claude for design assistance, AI prototyping tools) to accelerate concept validation, stakeholder feedback, and learning cycles.

  • Establish and maintain design standards for enforcement products, ensuring consistency, accessibility, and quality across all touchpoints while contributing to GovTech's broader design system.

  • Define and track success metrics for design solutions, measuring impact on user outcomes, operational efficiency, and policy objectives. Use data to drive continuous improvement.

Shape systems-level thinking and design excellence

  • Maintain strategic perspective at the programme level, ensuring individual product decisions align with broader enforcement division goals and contribute to MOM's service transformation vision.

  • Connect design work across multiple products and touchpoints, identifying opportunities for shared patterns, reusable components, and common capabilities that reduce duplication and increase efficiency.

  • Strengthen design quality through critique, review, and knowledge sharing, raising the overall standard of work and fostering a culture of design excellence across the enforcement portfolio.

Lead through collaboration and influence

  • Partner with cross-functional teams (product, engineering, operations, policy) to ensure design is deeply integrated into product strategy, planning, and delivery — not an afterthought.

  • Communicate design decisions, trade-offs, and recommendations clearly to stakeholders at all levels, ensuring alignment between user needs, operational constraints, and regulatory objectives.

  • Influence without authority by building trust, demonstrating value, and articulating how design contributes to measurable outcomes - not just better-looking interfaces.

  • Mentor junior designers and share knowledge across the organization, strengthening design capability and fostering a culture of learning and continuous improvement.

Contribute to GovTech's design community

  • Drive efficiency and innovation by piloting new tools, methods, and AI-assisted workflows in your work, demonstrating how design can operate faster and smarter without sacrificing quality.

  • Actively participate in design community initiatives - sharing learnings, contributing to design standards, and supporting efforts that benefit all GovTech designers.

  • Support hiring and team-building efforts where relevant, helping build a strong, diverse, inclusive design practice.

What success looks like

Enforcement systems feel coherent and connected across critical journeys from licensing to address management to enforcement operations with measurable improvements in usability and operational efficiency.

Cross-functional teams trust design to frame problems, guide decisions, and deliver solutions that meet both user needs and regulatory requirements.

Design work accelerates through effective use of modern tools and AI-assisted workflows, enabling faster iteration, higher output, and greater strategic focus.

Enforcement division stakeholders recognize design as a strategic capability that contributes to measurable outcomes not just a service function

[What we are looking for]

Experience and domain understanding:

  • 10+ years of experience in UX/UI design, product design, service design, or related fields
  • Proven experience leading design across complex, multi-stakeholder products or services
  • Experience in regulated, compliance-driven, or high-stakes environments (government, healthcare, financial services, legal/regulatory systems) strongly preferred
  • Demonstrated experience working on at least 2–3 different products, showing breadth and adaptability

You'll thrive here if you

  • Enjoy tackling complexity and ambiguity - breaking down messy regulatory and operational problems into clear, actionable design solutions.
  • See design as a means to measurable impact - cost reduction, productivity improvement, better policy outcomes not just better experiences.
  • Embrace efficiency and innovation - actively seeking ways to work smarter through modern tools, AI assistance, and streamlined methods.
  • Influence through clarity and credibility - earning trust by demonstrating value, not asserting authority.
  • Care about public service - motivated by the opportunity to improve critical government systems that affect thousands of employers, workers, and officers.
  • Are pragmatic and adaptable - able to balance ideal design solutions with real-world constraints, timelines, and trade-offs.
  • Want to build something meaningful - contributing to the transformation of legacy enforcement systems into modern, user-centered services.

[About Us]

Digital Excellence & Products Division (DXD) is a GovTech team within the Ministry of Education (MOE). DXD sits at the intersection of technology, design, and education. It’s a place where you can solve diverse, complex challenges for the education sector alongside thoughtful, intelligent individuals who lead with curiosity, empathy, and optimism. We develop meaningful products, platforms, and solutions for public good.

The Geospatial Team within DXD is focused on building MOE’s geospatial and spatial modelling capabilities. The team develops the models, tools, and geospatial foundation that help MOE anticipate future demand, optimise school locations, and make evidence-based planning decisions.

[What you will be working on]

As a Software Engineer on the Geospatial Team, you will be responsible for developing the systems and platforms that power MOE's long-term infrastructure and space planning.

Your responsibilities will be to:

  • Develop the MOE Spatial Modelling Engine that forecasts future education demand based on housing growth, demographics, migration patterns, land-use plans, and accessibility
  • Design and develop internal APIs and standards to ensure consistent and accessible geospatial data layers, such as school locations and travel-time layers
  • Embed mapping features and location intelligence into existing MOE tools, such as School Cockpit+, to improve decision-making workflows
  • Engage with product managers, planners, and policy officers to translate complex planning needs into technical requirements and robust software solutions
  • (For senior hires): Provide technical leadership for the geospatial engineering practice, establish best practices, and mentor junior team members

[What we are looking for]

  • Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Engineering, or a related discipline
  • Minimum 3 years of experience in developing and deploying real-world software applications at scale
  • Experience with GIS platforms (e.g., ArcGIS), geospatial APIs, routing engines, or 2D/3D mapping technologies
  • Experience with cloud technologies (AWS, GCP, or Azure) and building modern data or software architecture
  • Ability to work with complex datasets, including demographic, land-use, and transport data from various government agencies
  • A product-focused mindset with a passion for working for the public good and a specific interest in the education domain

GovTech is the lead agency driving Singapore's Smart Nation initiative and public sector digital transformation. We harness the power of technology to make lives better for citizens, businesses, international audiences and the public service sector.

Through the GreenGov initiative, the government has committed to the public its aim to achieve net-zero emissions by approximately 2045. Furthermore, it has established clear and measurable milestones to facilitate progress across all agencies. Since the year 2021, agencies have utilised the eGreenGov portal, which is housed within the National Environment Agency’s Waste and Resource Management System (WRMS), to report various environmental data of their respective premises.

The Public Sector Sustainability Office (PSSO) reviews the progress of agencies, provides updates to the Cabinet, and produces the annual GreenGov report championing the GreenGov initiative. They intend to help agencies identify opportunities to improve resource efficiency and climate resilience, and partner them to streamline the process of implementing these improvements.

job_title: UX Designer, Family Life Group (FLG) - Ministry of Social & Family Development
job_description:
GovTech is the lead agency driving Singapore’s Smart Nation initiatives and public sector digital transformation. As the Centre of Excellence for Infocomm Technology and Smart Systems (ICT & SS), GovTech develops the Singapore Government’s capabilities in Data Science & Artificial Intelligence, Application Development, Smart City Technology, Digital Infrastructure, and Cybersecurity.

At GovTech, we offer you a purposeful career to make lives better where we empower our people to master their craft through robust learning and development opportunities all year round.

Play a part in Singapore’s vision to build a Smart Nation and embark on your meaningful journey to build tech for public good. Join us to advance our mission and shape your future with us today!

Learn more about GovTech at tech.gov.sg.

[What you will be working on]

As an UX Designer supporting Family Life Group’s (FLG) product portfolio, you will drive end-to-end user experience work across two digital services:

(1) a digital marriage registration portal, and

(2) a family counselling portal.

You will work upstream to understand user needs through research and discovery, and translate insights into clear, testable design solutions in partnership with Product Managers and engineering teams.

1) Discovery & User Research

  • Together with the Product team, plan and run discovery activities (e.g., interviews, workshops, surveys) to understand user needs and pain points across the digital marriage registration and family counselling portals.

  • Conduct user research and concept validation (moderated and unmoderated) and capture evidence-based findings.

  • Synthesise research outputs into clear insights, problem statements, and opportunity areas that inform product direction and prioritisation.

2) Interaction Design & Prototyping

  • Translate insights into user journeys, user flows, information architecture, and interaction patterns.

  • Create wireframes and prototypes (low- to high-fidelity) to communicate design intent and support delivery.

  • Work with product managers, business owners and engineering partners implement designs.

3) Iteration, Quality & Outcomes

  • Iterate designs based on analytics and reported user feedback and track UX issues observed during delivery and post-release for continuous improvement.

  • Drive design quality through clear specifications, consistency, accessibility considerations, and pragmatic handover artefacts.

  • Contribute to strategic design initiatives that advance FLG portfolio outcomes (e.g., clearer service journeys, reduced user effort, improved completion).

What we expect from you

You are a key contributing member of the product team. You are expected to work with autonomy, consistently deliver high-quality outcomes, and collaborate effectively with Product Managers, engineers, and business stakeholders.

Scope & direction

  • Lead key design workstreams within a project, making day-to-day decisions that align with user needs and product goals.

  • Work independently from defined problem to solution options, engaging the right partners when decisions affect scope, risk, or cross-team dependencies.

  • Support team capability by coaching junior designers through constructive feedback and shared practices.

Craft & execution

  • Apply best practices in problem-solving and solution development, using evidence, iteration, and testing to validate outcomes.

  • Produce accessible and coherent designs within product, service, and operational constraints.

  • Make sound trade-offs in moderately complex situations and explain decisions using evidence; use AI-assisted tools with critical judgement.

Ownership

  • Own assigned tasks and projects end-to-end, including quality of outputs and clarity of handover.

  • Identify and address issues proactively with minimal guidance, and know when to escalate or ask for help.

  • Identify opportunities and recommend next steps to improve processes or outcomes within your work area.

Strategic alignment

  • Align your work with team goals, product OKRs, and agreed priorities; focus on tasks that deliver the highest expected value.

  • Adapt to changing priorities independently, while keeping the team informed of impact to scope, timelines, or risks.

Culture & organisational influence

  • Collaborate constructively with peers and stakeholders to ensure work is executed effectively end-to-end.

  • Address minor conflicts respectfully and early, focusing on shared outcomes.

  • Share knowledge, resources, and learnings (including successes and failures) to strengthen team capability and improve how we work

Who you’ll be working with:

  • You’ll report to the lead product manager

  • On a day to day, you’ll be embedded in product teams working with business, product manager and engineering to ensure designs solve business problems and progress product OKR’s.

[What we are looking for]

UX Craft (Core Requirements)

  • End-to-end UX capability: discovery → synthesis → design → testing → iteration.

  • Interaction design strength: produce user journeys/flows, wireframes, and prototypes at the right fidelity for the stage.

  • Expertise in tools of the trade – Figma, Maze etc

  • Ability to plan and conduct interviews/workshops/surveys/usability tests; translate findings into actionable insights.

Strong plus

  • Demonstrated experience in UX “vibe coding” / rapid prototyping approaches (e.g., AI-assisted or low/no-code tools), as there will be upcoming initiatives in this area.

Context fit

  • Comfort supporting multiple products within the FLG portfolio as priorities shift.

  • A curious, collaborative and respectful working attitude.

job_title: Senior / UX Designer, Culture, Community and Youth Cluster (CCYC)
job_description:
GovTech is the lead agency driving Singapore’s Smart Nation initiatives and public sector digital transformation. As the Centre of Excellence for Infocomm Technology and Smart Systems (ICT & SS), GovTech develops the Singapore Government’s capabilities in Data Science & Artificial Intelligence, Application Development, Smart City Technology, Digital Infrastructure, and Cybersecurity.

At GovTech, we offer you a purposeful career to make lives better where we empower our people to master their craft through robust learning and development opportunities all year round.

Play a part in Singapore’s vision to build a Smart Nation and embark on your meaningful journey to build tech for public good. Join us to advance our mission and shape your future with us today!

Learn more about GovTech at tech.gov.sg.

About CCYC
CCYC (Culture, Community and Youth Cluster) is a part of GovTech's Government Digital Transformation team where we support the Ministry of Culture, Community and Youth (MCCY) and its statutory boards — such as the National Arts Council (NAC), People’s Association (PA), Sport Singapore (SportSG), and MUIS—by delivering digital solutions that promote social cohesion, cultural vibrancy, and youth engagement.

Our growing suite of products include the following:

  • Community Engagement Platforms

Digital products that enable citizens to participate in grassroots and national events, including Volunteer Management, Community Event Registration, Feedback and Ideation products for civic engagement.

  • Cultural Participation and Arts Enablement

Event Discovery and Ticketing, Grant Application for Arts Funding, Digital Storytelling and Archival products.

  • Youth Development and Empowerment

Platforms for Youth Leadership and Volunteering Opportunities, Gamified Learning Tools for civic education, Co-creation Platforms for Youth Policy Input etc.

  • Sports and Active Lifestyle

Facility booking, Fitness tracking and Rewards, Data Platforms for Sports Policy and Performance Tracking

[What you will be working on]

You will own the design of CCYC’s community products across the full product lifecycle — from discovery through launch, optimisation and growth.

Own the Full Design Arc

Lead design from 0 to 1 to 100. This means shaping the initial product concept, designing and shipping the first version, and then continuously improving the experience based on data, feedback and evolving user needs. You do not hand off and move on — you stay accountable to the outcome.

Drive Product Thinking Through Design

Bring a product mindset to every design decision. You think about retention, activation, and adoption — not just usability. You use design as a lever for growth: improving onboarding flows, reducing friction in high-drop-off moments, and creating experiences that bring users back.

Conduct Lightweight, Continuous Research

Run user interviews, usability tests and contextual checks as a regular part of your workflow. You do not wait for a dedicated research phase — you embed research into the cadence of product development to stay grounded in real user behaviour, especially across MCCY's diverse community demographics.

Design Across the Full Stack of UX

From user flows and information architecture to interaction design, visual design and motion — you handle it all. You produce work to a high standard of craft and detail, with particular attention to microinteractions, accessibility and the nuances that separate a polished product from a functional one.

Maintain and Evolve the Design System

Take ownership of the CCYC product design system. Keep components consistent, well-documented and scalable as the product grows. You balance system rigour with the pragmatism needed to move fast in a live product environment.

Manage Stakeholders and Communicate Design Decisions

Work directly with MCCY stakeholders — community managers, grassroots leaders, operations teams — as well as internal product and engineering partners. You present your work clearly, advocate for the user, and navigate competing priorities without losing design integrity.

Contribute to Design Culture

As a senior IC, you set the quality bar. You give constructive feedback, contribute to shared design practices within DSP, and help elevate the standard of product design across the team.

[Who we are looking for]

We are looking for a Senior UX Designer to own the end-to-end product design. This is a full product design role — you will work from 0 to 1 to 100, taking products from initial concept through to a polished, growing, live experience used by hundreds of thousands of Singaporeans.

You are a generalist with strong product instincts. You conduct research, shape the product direction, design the interface, sweat the microinteractions, and maintain the design system — all while staying close to the metrics that tell you whether your work is actually making a difference. You are equally comfortable in a whiteboard session with a product manager and in Figma refining a component for the fourth time.

This is an individual contributor (IC) role. You will operate with significant autonomy, manage stakeholders, and be the design voice your squad relies on.

We are looking for a designer who combines sharp product instincts with strong craft — someone who can operate independently across the full range of design work.

  • Experience: 5+ years of product design experience, with a portfolio demonstrating end-to-end ownership of a shipped, live product — from early concept through to post-launch iterations.
  • Product Thinking: A clear product mindset: you think about metrics, growth and adoption, and can articulate how your design decisions connect to product outcomes.
  • UX Research & Design: Strong UX fundamentals — user research, journey mapping, information architecture and usability — applied pragmatically within a fast-moving product team.
  • UI & Interaction Design: High-craft visual and interaction design skills, including a strong eye for typography, layout, visual hierarchy and microinteractions.
  • Design Systems: Hands-on experience owning or contributing significantly to a component-based design system.
  • Communication: Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills — you can present design rationale confidently, handle pushback constructively, and build trust with non-designers.
  • Autonomy: Self-directed and comfortable with ambiguity — you can manage your own workload, set priorities and deliver independently without close supervision.

Bonus Points For:

  • Experience designing for mass-market consumer apps with diverse or non-digital-native user bases.
  • Familiarity with growth design principles: onboarding optimisation, engagement loops, notification design or A/B testing.
  • Experience working in or alongside the Singapore public sector or community organisations.
  • Knowledge of accessibility standards (WCAG) and inclusive design for diverse demographics.

Why Join Us:

  • Opportunity to shape national youth and community initiatives
  • Complex, meaningful problems to solve
  • Collaborative, innovation-focused environment
  • Professional development opportunities
  • Chance to make a lasting impact on Singapore's social fabric
