job_title: Senior Software Engineer (Reimagine Engineering), Software Engineering Practice
job_description:
GovTech is the lead agency driving Singapore’s Smart Nation initiatives and public sector digital transformation. As the Centre of Excellence for Infocomm Technology and Smart Systems (ICT & SS), GovTech develops the Singapore Government’s capabilities in Data Science & Artificial Intelligence, Application Development, Smart City Technology, Digital Infrastructure, and Cybersecurity.

At GovTech, we offer you a purposeful career to make lives better where we empower our people to master their craft through robust learning and development opportunities all year round.

Play a part in Singapore’s vision to build a Smart Nation and embark on your meaningful journey to build tech for public good. Join us to advance our mission and shape your future with us today!

Learn more about GovTech at tech.gov.sg.

[What you will be working on]

We are the GTO – Software Engineering Practice within GovTech, dedicated to uplifting engineering standards, tools, and practices across the organization. Our mission is to reimagine how software is built, tested, shipped, and maintained - by empowering engineering teams with the tools, libraries, and guidance they need to succeed.

We build and steward key initiatives that shape how GovTech engineers work every day such as:

  • The GovTech Developer CLI, a unified command-line experience for onboarding and day-to-day tasks

  • Starter Kits and Software Libraries to accelerate secure, consistent development

  • The GovTech Developer Handbook, a central source of truth for engineering practices and guidelines

We work across the full software development lifecycle - from local dev environments to production delivery - to drive engineering excellence, consistency, and velocity across public digital services. We also serve as practice enablers, helping to propagate best practices, gather community feedback, and champion a culture of engineering excellence across GovTech.

Our north star:

  • Reduce the time it takes for an engineer to deliver value to production to under one hour.

  • We aim to first raise the bar within GovTech, and ultimately scale this impact across Whole-of-Government (WOG) engineering teams through common tooling, standards, and shared communities of practice.

As a Senior Software Engineer in the Reimagine Software Engineering team, you will:

Shape the Developer Experience

Design, build, and maintain tools like the GovTech Developer CLI, starter kits, and common libraries that improve how engineers onboard, develop, and ship software.

Build Foundational Libraries and Kits

Develop reusable Node.js and Golang libraries, templates, and patterns that enable teams to adopt secure-by-default and production-ready practices.

Lead CI/CD and Automation Initiatives

Collaborate with platform and product teams to define best practices for continuous integration, deployment pipelines, and local development environments (e.g., Dev Containers).

Champion Software Engineering Standards

Help define and codify best practices and standards in the GovTech Developer Handbook, and partner with teams to support adoption and onboarding.

Drive Infrastructure and DevOps Patterns

Support initiatives around containerization, runtime hardening, developer environments, and observability tooling - making it easy for teams to do the right thing.

Enable and Uplift the Developer Community

  • Continuously engage developers across GovTech to understand pain points and bottlenecks

  • Facilitate feedback loops, conversations, and forums to uncover developer pain points

  • Propagate and socialize good practices, gather insights, and drive solutions that reflect real-world developer needs

Collaborate and Mentor

Work closely with cross-functional teams to understand their needs, guide them in adopting shared tooling, and mentor engineers on engineering best practices.

Contribute to Strategic Engineering Initiatives

Help shape the technical direction and roadmap of how GovTech engineers build software - from first commit to production operations.

[What we are looking for]

You may be a great fit if:

  • You have strong backend software engineering experience in Node.js and/or Golang
  • You’re passionate about improving developer workflows and building tools, libraries, and guidance that make engineering teams more productive
  • You understand the full SDLC - from coding and testing to CI/CD, infrastructure, containers, monitoring, and operations
  • You care about clean, secure, maintainable software, and are opinionated about good engineering practices
  • You thrive on listening to developers, understanding their pain points, and working collaboratively to solve them
  • You’re an effective communicator and facilitator, able to align different perspectives and rally developers around a shared standard or tool
  • You value outcomes over output, and constantly seek to reduce toil and improve developer experience through automation and design
  • You are collaborative, humble, and communicate well with engineers, product managers, and leadership
  • You embrace open-source practices and are comfortable maintaining shared code used by many
  • You model a “you build it, you own it” mindset, and take responsibility for the things you create

We’re especially excited by senior engineers who:

  • Have built and scaled internal tooling or developer enablement platforms
  • Understand software design, developer ergonomics, and DX principles
  • Bring a platform engineering mindset to solving common pain points at scale
  • Demonstrate breadth across software engineering, CI/CD, DevOps, and container runtimes
  • Understand how to design maintainable systems and developer APIs
  • Have contributed to open-source tools or actively engage in engineering communities
  • Are skilled at facilitating conversations and aligning engineers across teams toward common practices
  • Are excited to co-create standards that will be adopted across many teams
  • Are excellent mentors and collaborators, helping uplift the engineering culture across GovTech

What we offer you:

GovTech is an equal opportunity employer committed to fostering an inclusive workplace that values diverse voices and perspectives, as we believe that diversity is the foundation to innovation.   

Our employee benefits are based on a total rewards approach, offering a holistic and market-competitive suite of perks. These include leave benefits to meet your work-life needs and employee wellness programs. 

We champion flexible work arrangements (subject to your job role) and trust that you will manage your own time to deliver your best, wherever you are, and whatever works best for you. 

Learn more about life inside GovTech at go.gov.sg/GovTechCareers.
Stay connected with us on social media at go.gov.sg/ConnectWithGovTech

GovTech's Design Practice is the central design team for the agency, responsible for the standards, culture, and capabilities that design teams across government rely on. We have more to do than we have people to do it, and that's the opportunity. As a Principal Designer, you'd work across the full range of what the practice is building: from shaping design standards and the product-first playbook, to supporting new product teams as they stand up, to growing design talent and the community that connects designers across government. The exact shape of your work will flex with the practice's priorities and your own strengths. What stays constant is the seniority of the problems and the room to lead.

[What you will be working on]

You'd take on initiatives across the areas the practice is responsible for. Depending on where the need is greatest and where you're strongest, that could mean:

  • Helping shape design standards and the product-first playbook that other practices build around
  • Supporting new product teams as they form, helping their leads find their footing and their teams build a strong design culture
  • Standing up the systems the practice runs on: hiring processes, role frameworks, evaluation rubrics, and the baseline for what good design looks like
  • Building design talent and capability across GovTech, through mentoring, reskilling programs, and growing the leadership community
  • Keeping designers connected and good work visible, through community spaces, events, and showcasing strong outcomes

You'll thrive here if:

  • You care deeply about design, almost to a fault. This role is full of hard conversations that matter, and only someone who genuinely cares will have the energy for them
  • You're comfortable being opinionated, and you can hold a position in a debate without needing to win it
  • You see your job as moving the whole field forward, not producing the most polished artifact in the room
  • You're energised by ambiguity and the chance to define things that don't exist yet

[What we are looking for]

  • 12+ years in design, with a track record of shaping standards, culture, or capability, not just shipping products
  • A demonstrated point of view on craft and quality, and the credibility to back it up with work
  • Evidence that you can drive change through influence and argument: frameworks others adopted, debates you moved, bars you raised
  • Depth across the design arc, from research and problem framing through to interface and delivery
  • A strong communicator who builds trust quickly and can hold the room in difficult, multi-stakeholder conversations
  • Active engagement with how AI is reshaping design work is a strong plus

What you can expect:

  • Work that is genuinely varied and rarely pre-defined, spanning AI, policy, capability, and culture
  • Real influence over how design is understood and valued across government, with the seniority to set the bar
  • A mandate to build something that lasts, and the kind of hard, meaningful problems that make the work worth caring about

The Government Technology Agency (GovTech) aims to transform the delivery of Government digital services by taking an "outside-in" view, putting citizens and businesses at the heart of everything we do. We also develop the Smart Nation infrastructure and applications and facilitate collaboration with citizens and businesses to co-develop technologies.

Join us as we support Singapore’s vision of building a Smart Nation - a nation of possibilities empowered through info-communications technology and related engineering.

[What you will be working on]

  • Identify gaps, formulate strategic insights, and provide timely recommendations to ensure continuous and relevant protection of Government ICT assets

  • Recommend creative and practical ways to address complex cybersecurity challenges from existing and emerging technologies, and digital environments, to proactively address cybersecurity risks and vulnerabilities

  • Perform strategic resourcing functions to secure resources to grow the cybersecurity portfolio to support WoG needs

  • Develop, assess, and review existing and proposed cybersecurity strategies and policies in close collaboration with stakeholders to identify opportunities for efficiency improvements across Whole-of-Government

  • Monitor and evaluate the continued effectiveness of cybersecurity strategies and initiatives

  • Develop performance metrics and reporting mechanisms to track progress, identify areas for improvement, and communicate results to senior leadership and stakeholders

  • Perform research in cyber and non-cyber domains in order to formulate cross-disciplinary frameworks, roadmaps and strategies

  • Support all cybersecurity strategy and planning functions, including but not limited to preparing reports, briefings, and presentations for senior leadership and other stakeholders, draft speeches or talking points for senior leadership’s engagements, preparing responses to Parliamentary Questions, or questions from senior leadership in and outside of GovTech, and supporting in the Division’s work in comms management, diplomacy efforts and partnership engagements

  • Perform basic protocol duties for G2G and G2B events

[What we are looking for]

  • Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Infocomm Security, Computer Science, Computer/Electronics Engineering or Information Technology preferred

  • CISSP and/or CISA certifications would be an advantage

  • Knowledge of and interest in emerging cybersecurity threats and trends about the latest cybersecurity technologies, tools, and solutions, and continually assessing their applicability to Government systems

  • At least 5-6 years of prior experience in civil service-related work is preferred

  • English language skills is a must

  • Excellent communication and presentation skills

  • Clear and logical thinking, and analytical mindset; ability to grasp complex topics and distill into clear and easy-to-understand concepts; ability to develop creative solutions and form logical, coherent reasoning

  • Familiarity with statistical methods and econometrics would be an advantage

  • Finance, accounting or legal qualifications or experience would be an advantage

  • Ability to work collaboratively in a cross-functional team environment

  • Able to work independently and persevering attitude

  • Singapore Citizen only

GovTech is an equal-opportunity employer committed to fostering an inclusive workplace that values diverse voices and perspectives, as we believe that diversity is the foundation to innovation. Our employee benefits are based on a total rewards approach, offering a holistic and market-competitive suite of perks. These include leave benefits to meet your work-life needs and employee wellness programs. 

We champion flexible work arrangements (subject to your job role) and trust that you will manage your own time to deliver your best, wherever you are, and whatever works best for you.

Position Overview

We are seeking a Product Manager (formerly Business Analyst also would be considered) to drive user engagement and stakeholder management for our cybersecurity solutions, working closely with the lead Product/Programme Manager. This role is instrumental in shaping product strategy, understanding user needs, measuring product impact, and translating security requirements into valuable features. You will lead sprint ceremonies and work hand-in-hand with development teams to drive continuous improvement and delivery excellence. The role involves facilitating agile ceremonies, managing sprint backlogs, and fostering a culture of iterative development while collaborating with cross-functional teams to manage and develop cybersecurity solutions. You are also required to take on product management responsibilities, including strategic roadmap planning and continuous security posture assessment.


Key Responsibilities

Strategic Product Management:

  • Partner with lead Product/Programme Manager to develop and execute product strategy and vision
  • Own and drive specific product features or components of the cybersecurity solution
  • Develop and maintain product roadmap in alignment with overall strategy
  • Conduct market research and competitive analysis to inform product decisions
  • Build compelling business cases for new features and improvements
  • Lead end-to-end product lifecycle management for assigned features

User Engagement & Stakeholder Management:

  • Drive user research initiatives to deeply understand user needs and pain points
  • Develop and maintain strong relationships with key stakeholders
  • Collaborate with UX teams and development team to design intuitive security solutions
  • Lead regular product reviews and feedback sessions with users and stakeholders
  • Transform user insights into actionable product requirements

Agile & Development Team Collaboration:

  • Lead and facilitate all sprint ceremonies including planning, daily stand-ups, reviews, and retrospectives
  • Drive continuous improvement initiatives within the development team
  • Manage sprint backlogs and ensure alignment with product roadmap
  • Work closely with engineering leads to remove blockers and optimise team velocity
  • Foster an environment of collaborative problem-solving and innovation
  • Ensure clear communication of product requirements and acceptance criteria
  • Balance technical debt with new feature development
  • Facilitate technical discussions between product and engineering teams
  • Prioritise and manage product backlog based on business value and technical constraints
  • Lead sprint planning and backlog refinement sessions
  • Drive product launches and go-to-market strategies
  • Ensure product compliance with security standards and regulations
  • Monitor and analyse product metrics to drive data-informed decisions

Required Qualifications

  • 3-5 years of product management experience, with demonstrated success in driving product roadmaps and strategy
  • Proven experience working in agile development environments and leading cross-functional teams
  • Track record of end-to-end product delivery in enterprise software or cybersecurity solutions
  • Experience in stakeholder management across different organisational levels
  • Demonstrated experience in product discovery, development, and launch
  • Experience in gathering and translating customer requirements into product features
  • History of successful collaboration with engineering teams in agile environments
  • Advanced knowledge of agile methodologies and frameworks
  • Expertise in product management tools (JIRA, Confluence, product analytics tools)
  • Understanding of software development lifecycle
  • Knowledge of UX principles and design thinking
  • Excellent strategic thinking and problem-solving abilities
  • Singaporean only.

[What you will be working on]

The Attorney-General’s Chambers (AGC) in Singapore is seeking a Designer/Researcher to support its digital transformation efforts, focusing on modernizing legal drafting systems and developing AI-powered tools. The role involves consolidating data, improving search functions, and enhancing evidence-based legal operations.

  • User research and requirements analysis: The candidate will conduct user interviews and workshops to understand search behaviors and define the scope for an enterprise search solution.

  • Service journey mapping and design: Responsibilities include mapping current search workflows, designing optimal user experiences, and creating service blueprints for new search capabilities.

  • Concept development and prototyping: The role involves collaborating with teams to create design concepts, wireframes, and prototypes using tools like Figma, iterating designs for stakeholder feedback.

  • UI design specification and future planning: The candidate will develop detailed UI specifications, prepare handover materials, establish design frameworks for future projects, and document best practices

Brief Overview

The Attorney-General's Chambers (AGC) is Singapore's principal government legal service, responsible for legislation drafting, legal advisory, prosecution, and international law. AGC is undergoing digital transformation which includes the modernisation of its legislative drafting systems, the adoption of AI-powered knowledge management tools, and the development of a centralised data infrastructure to support evidence-based legal work products.

  • Consolidate siloed datasets across teams and systems into a centralised data warehouse

  • Establish tagging, classification, and metadata frameworks for legal knowledge repositories

  • Prepare and structure data to support AI-powered tools including LLM based application across AGC product suite

  • Build the analytical foundations for evidence-based legal operations and decision-making

The successful candidate will guide the entire design process from discovery to implementation, working with legal professionals and technical teams to address current search fragmentation and pain points. This position also involves establishing design frameworks for AGC's broader digital transformation initiatives.

Key Responsibilities:

User Research and Requirements Analysis

  • Conduct comprehensive user interviews and stakeholder consultations to understand current search behaviours, pain points, and unmet needs across AGC

  • Facilitate discovery workshops with business users to map existing search workflows and identify gaps in current individual search engines

  • Analyse user feedback and requirements to define the scope and functionality needed for the enterprise search solution

Service Journey Mapping and Design

  • Map current-state service journeys for search-related processes across different AGC systems and departments

  • Design comprehensive to-be service flows that outline optimal user experiences for the first phase - unified enterprise search capability

  • Create detailed service blueprints that define user touchpoints, backend processes, and system interactions for the new search functionality

Concept Development and Prototyping

  • Work collaboratively with the existing AGC teams to translate user requirements into tangible design concepts and mockups leveraging tools like Figma

  • Develop wireframes, interactive prototypes, and concept designs that demonstrate the proposed enterprise search user experience

  • Create multiple design iterations and alternatives for stakeholder review and validation

User Testing and Validation

  • Present design concepts and prototypes to users and stakeholders for feedback and validation

  • Conduct usability testing sessions to evaluate design effectiveness and identify areas for improvement

  • Iterate on service journeys and design concepts based on user feedback to ensure optimal adoption and user satisfaction

UI Design Specification and Handover

  • Develop detailed UI screen designs and interface specifications that provide clear guidance for development teams

  • Create comprehensive design documentation including user interface guidelines, interaction patterns, and visual design standards

  • Prepare design handover materials and collaborate with engineers to ensure accurate implementation of designed solutions

Future Stream Planning and Framework Development

  • Establish service design methodologies and user research frameworks that can be applied to subsequent transformation phases

  • Support the planning and scoping of the IW platform modernisation by providing insights from the enterprise search project

  • Document lessons learned and best practices to guide future transformation initiatives across AGC

[What we are looking for]

  • 5 to 8 years of UX design experience with expertise in user research, service design, and complex data-heavy interfaces

  • Proven ability to lead discovery in complex, rules-based domains
    Skilled in Figma, service blueprinting, journey mapping, and workshop facilitation

  • Strong visual communication and systems thinking capabilities

  • Experience working with structured content (e.g. forms, XML, data-heavy UIs)

  • Comfortable presenting and defending design decisions to non-design stakeholders

We Also Value

  • Intellectual curiosity by asking why, not just how.

  • A bias towards action: you prefer testing ideas over debating them.

  • A collaborative mindset - you can work with domain experts, engineers, and leadership.

Place of Work

This is a hybrid role based at the Attorney-General’s Chambers, located in PARKROYAL COLLECTION Pickering.

[About CCYC]

CCYC (Culture, Community and Youth Cluster) is a part of GovTech's Government Digital Transformation team where we support the Ministry of Culture, Community and Youth (MCCY) and its statutory boards — such as the National Arts Council (NAC), People’s Association (PA), Sport Singapore (SportSG), and Islamic Religious Council of Singapore (MUIS)—by delivering digital solutions that promote social cohesion, cultural vibrancy, and youth engagement.

Our growing suite of products include the following:

Community Engagement Platforms

Digital products that enable citizens to participate in grassroots and national events, including Volunteer Management, Community Event Registration, Feedback and Ideation products for civic engagement.

Cultural Participation and Arts Enablement

Event Discovery and Ticketing, Grant Application for Arts Funding, Digital Storytelling and Archival products.

Youth Development and Empowerment

Platforms for Youth Leadership and Volunteering Opportunities, Gamified Learning Tools for civic education, Co-creation Platforms for Youth Policy Input etc.

Sports and Active Lifestyle

Facility booking, Fitness tracking and Rewards, Data Platforms for Sports Policy and Performance Tracking

[What you will be working on]

About the Role

We are seeking experienced Product Managers/Senior Product Managers to lead the end-to-end development of innovative digital solutions that transform how MCCY connects with Singaporeans. In this role, you will spearhead initiatives that strengthen community bonds and promote active citizenship through technology and create meaningful impact across our society.


Key Responsibilities

Product Strategy

  • Build strong relationship and partner with agencies partners to define product vision, business objectives, strategy and roadmap

  • Lead stakeholder engagement to develop value proposition and secure buy-in for product initiatives

Product Discovery

  • Conduct market research, competitive analysis and user research to discover user needs

  • Conduct user testing sessions and translate insights into product improvements

Product Development

  • Manage product backlog and prioritise features based on user needs and impact

  • Write clear product requirements and user stories

  • Work closely with product designers, engineering and QA team throughout the development lifecycle

  • Own product performance and track user engagement by making data-driven decisions through product analytics

  • Maintain and manages updates, patches and bug fixes to maintain product stability

[What we are looking for]

Experience & Qualifications

  • Diploma/Degree in Computer Science, IT, Engineering or related field

  • Minimum 5 years of relevant experience in product management

  • Experience working directly with engineers, designers, and product teams

  • Strong background in modern product development practices (Agile, Cloud, Design Thinking)

  • Proven track record of shipping successful products from scratch

Technical Skills

  • Product and project management

  • Strong analytical and problem-solving capabilities

  • User experience (UX) capabilities

  • Mobile Application and Website Development, Content Management System

Professional Attributes

  • Strategic thinking and decision-making ability

  • Excellence in stakeholder management and communication

  • Data-driven approach to problem-solving

Personal Qualities

  • Action-oriented mindset

  • Innovation-focused approach

  • Growth mindset and continuous learning attitude

  • Strong sense of ownership and accountability

  • Passion for youth development and community building

[What We Offer]

  • Opportunity to shape national youth and community initiatives

  • Complex, meaningful problems to solve

  • Collaborative, innovation-focused environment

  • Professional development opportunities

  • Chance to make a lasting impact on Singapore's social fabric

[What you will be working on]

As a software engineer, you will:

  • Lead the design, development, and implementation of complex, scalable, and secure software applications across the full software development lifecycle.

  • Collaborate with product managers, UX designers, and other engineers to define requirements, architect solutions, and deliver high-quality products.

  • Write clean, maintainable, and efficient code in various programming languages (e.g., Python, Java, Go, JavaScript, C#).

  • Conduct code reviews, provide constructive feedback, and mentor junior engineers to foster a culture of technical excellence.

  • Troubleshoot and debug production issues, ensuring high availability and performance of critical systems.

  • Contribute to technical documentation, architectural diagrams, and best practices.

  • Stay abreast of emerging technologies and industry trends, evaluating their potential application within GovTech's ecosystem.

  • Participate in agile development processes, including stand-ups, sprint planning, and retrospectives.

You're not just here to write code, but also to figure out what we should be building and how we should build it. You will work on meaningful projects that solve problems pertinent to our society. The public sector is full of opportunities where even the simplest software can have a big impact on people’s lives. Rather than work on commercial ventures commonly found in the startup scene, we're here to improve how we live as a society through what we can offer as a government.

Your job will be to bring expertise and capability to the public sector. Sometimes this means coding new systems from scratch. Other times this means using the best solutions the development community has to offer. We use cloud services, open-source software, and commodity hardware as far as possible. These often include, but are not limited to: GitHub, AWS, React Native, MongoDB, and Stripe amongst others. Knowing what to build and what to reuse lets us avoid wasting time on solved problems and focus on delivering actual value.

[What we are looking for]

  • Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related technical field.

  • Minimum of 5 years of progressive experience in software development with a strong track record of delivering successful projects

  • Experience in Mobile Application and Website Development, Content Management System (CMS)

  • Proficiency in at least two modern programming languages (e.g., Python, Java, Go, JavaScript, C#) and experience with relevant frameworks.

  • Solid understanding of software design principles, data structures, algorithms, and distributed systems.

  • Web development with Node.js background, preferably with Next.js and AI-assisted development

  • Strong problem-solving skills, with the ability to analyze complex technical challenges and propose effective solutions.

  • Excellent communication, collaboration, and interpersonal skills.

  • Experience working on government or public sector projects is a plus.

  • A strong passion for public service and leveraging technology for the betterment of society.

  • Overall, we look for people who have ability, initiative, good communication, and strong values.

About MOM CIO Team (ISTD)
MOM CIO Team (ISTD) operates as the strategic technology partner for policy and operations teams in the Ministry of Manpower enabling them to achieve MOM’s vision of “Empowered Workforce, Thriving Workplaces”

Product Managers are key to our success. They lead cross-functional teams of engineers, data scientists, designers and researchers and are ultimately responsible for product outcomes.They show exceptional clarity of thought and this is reflected in their written and verbal communication skills. They lead through influence and use hard evidence, reputation and relationships to create stakeholder alignment.

[What you will be working on]

As the product lead for Labour Market Tools, you are responsible for the tools and techniques that policy makers use to derive and simulate various strategies that maintain a well functioning labour market.

You are required to:

  • Shape the product vision and strategy aligned with your policy leaders
  • Lead discovery and problem framing sessions with policy owners, operations teams, inspectors, enforcement units, and external stakeholders (e.g., employers, employment agencies, workers) to understand ground challenges and surface opportunities for policy redesign and solution hypotheses that map to organisational outcomes

GovTech is the lead agency driving Singapore’s Smart Nation initiatives and public sector digital transformation. As the Centre of Excellence for Infocomm Technology and Smart Systems (ICT & SS), GovTech develops the Singapore Government’s capabilities in Data Science & Artificial Intelligence, Application Development, Smart City Technology, Digital Infrastructure, and Cybersecurity.

At GovTech, we offer you a purposeful career to make lives better where we empower our people to master their craft through robust learning and development opportunities all year round.

Play a part in Singapore’s vision to build a Smart Nation and embark on your meaningful journey to build tech for public good. Join us to advance our mission and shape your future with us today!

Learn more about GovTech at tech.gov.sg.

[What you will be working on]

GovTech builds and runs digital systems that Singaporeans use every day, often without noticing. From digital identity and payments to cloud platforms and AI tools used across government, the work sits right at the intersection of technology and public trust. But technology has changed. Systems are no longer static. They evolve continuously, connect everywhere, and increasingly make decisions on their own. The old model of governance and risk management is struggling to keep up.

This role is for someone who wants to help rethink that. Someone curious enough to question old assumptions, practical enough to work alongside engineers and product teams, and grounded enough to know that governance only matters if it helps people make better decisions in the real world. You will work on difficult but meaningful problems at national scale. The kind where there are no clean textbook answers yet. The upside is that you will not just be managing risk. You will help shape what modern governance looks like for cloud, AI, and digital public infrastructure in the years ahead.

The problems you will help solve

You will spend your time on questions that do not have settled answers, such as these:

  • How do we apply quantitative and structured risk management approaches in ways that genuinely sharpen decisions, rather than producing analysis that sits unread?

  • How do we connect enterprise risk management to operational telemetry and engineering workflows, so signals from the live environment shape our risk picture in near real time?

  • How do we surface and articulate risks that are inherently cross-cutting, where cybersecurity, technology, data, and citizen impact intersect?

  • How do we balance the appetite to innovate quickly in areas like AI, cloud, and platform engineering with the obligation to be a careful steward of public systems and public money?

  • How do we make risk conversations genuinely useful to senior leadership, the board, and partner agencies, rather than a compliance ritual?

  • How do we manage risk-based exceptions as real decisions rather than paperwork, ensuring each one is scoped, owned, and time-bound, while also reading the pattern of exceptions across the organisation as a signal about where our controls or standards need to evolve?

  • How do we run third-party and supply chain risk as a continuous practice, where ongoing signals (e.g., incidents, posture changes, geopolitical shifts, financial health) reshape our view of third-party relationships and corresponding risk responses?

[What we are looking for]

Who you are

  • You have at least 8 years of experience in enterprise risk, technology risk, or operational risk and resilience, and you have worked hands-on with frameworks such as FAIR, ISO 31000, COSO ERM, or operational risk models. You know these tools well enough to apply them selectively and adapt them when they do not quite fit.

  • You are comfortable in the world of technology and digital systems. You do not need to be an engineer, but you should be able to hold a credible conversation about cloud, data, cybersecurity, software delivery, and emerging technologies like AI — and know when something you are hearing deserves more scrutiny.

  • You have reported to or engaged regularly with senior executives, audit committees, or board-level risk committees. You understand what those audiences need from a risk lens, and you know the difference between informing a decision and just presenting a slide.

What will set you apart

  • You possess strong analytical thinking and structured problem-solving abilities, with the capacity to synthesise complex information into clear, actionable insights.

  • You translate technical and operational complexity into language that helps non-specialists make better decisions.

  • You can challenge senior stakeholders constructively, holding firm on risk issues without becoming an obstacle to delivery.

  • You are comfortable with ambiguity. Many of the risks we deal with are novel, and there is no perfect playbook.

  • You are pragmatic. You know that a risk framework is only as valuable as the decisions it improves.

A note before you apply

We would rather have an honest conversation with someone who is curious and capable than receive a polished application from someone who meets every criterion on paper but isn't genuinely drawn to the mission. If the problems above sound like the ones you want to spend the next few years solving, we would like to hear from you.

What the role is:

The Recruiting Coordinator at GovTech serves as the operational backbone of the Talent Acquisition team, bridging the gap between our high-growth technology needs and rigorous interviewing standards. This role is not merely administrative; it is a critical touchpoint for candidates navigating the complexities of a fast moving tech organization. The coordinator ensures that the hiring process remains efficient and transparent, all while maintaining a "candidate-first" philosophy that is essential in the competitive technology market. This will initially start off as a 12-month contract.

What you'll be working on:

The primary responsibility of the Recruiting Coordinator is to manage the end-to-end candidate journey. This involves high-volume interview scheduling across a diverse stakeholder group and platforms, requiring a mastery of calendar management and logistical coordination. Beyond scheduling, the coordinator acts as a brand ambassador, providing candidates with clear expectations regarding the unique timelines and requirements of GovTech roles.

Interview Coordination & Scheduling

  • Manage end-to-end scheduling of high volumes of interviews across multiple business units and hiring teams.
  • Coordinate interview logistics for virtual, in-person, panel, and assessment-based interviews.
  • Serve as the primary point of contact for candidates, recruiters, hiring managers, and interviewers throughout the scheduling process.
  • Ensure a seamless and positive candidate experience by maintaining clear and timely communication.
  • Proactively identify and resolve scheduling conflicts, last-minute changes, and resource constraints.

Interview Resource Allocation

  • Manage interviewer capacity and availability across complex interviewer pools.
  • Optimize interviewer assignments based on skills, panel requirements, workload balancing, and hiring priorities.
  • Partner with recruiting teams and hiring managers to ensure interview panels are appropriately staffed and calibrated.
  • Monitor interviewer utilization and recommend process improvements to improve efficiency and reduce scheduling bottlenecks.
  • Maintain accurate interviewer rosters, scheduling guidelines, and allocation frameworks.

Recruitment Operations & Reporting

  • Track interview scheduling metrics and operational performance indicators.
  • Prepare regular reports and dashboards for recruiters, hiring managers, and leadership teams.
  • Analyze scheduling trends, interviewer utilization, candidate throughput, and operational efficiency.
  • Ensure data accuracy and integrity within recruitment systems and reporting tools.
  • Support workforce planning and recruitment forecasting through scheduling and pipeline data insights.

Process Improvement

  • Identify opportunities to streamline scheduling workflows and improve recruitment operations.
  • Develop and maintain standard operating procedures (SOPs) and documentation.
  • Partner with recruiters and stakeholders to implement scalable scheduling and coordination practices.
  • Support projects related to recruitment technology, automation, and operational excellence.

What we are looking for:

  • Candidates for this position should ideally possess a Bachelor’s degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, or a related field.
  • A minimum of one to three years of experience in a fast-paced administrative or recruiting role is required.
  • Proficiency in modern productivity suites (Google Workspace or Microsoft 365) and a demonstrated ability to manage complex schedules are non-negotiable.
  • Advantageous to have experience in navigating and managing modern Applicant Tracking Systems

The ideal candidate will possess:

  • Strong attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy.
  • Ability to manage multiple priorities and deadlines simultaneously.

  • Solid understanding of OpenUSD layering, composition arcs, and schemas.

  • Familiarity with geospatial data formats — including raster, vector, and point clouds — and their application in physics-based simulation environments.

[What you will be working on]

We are seeking a talented and experienced Software Engineer (DevOps) to join the Salesforce Centre of Excellence (CoE) team at GovTech Singapore. The CoE drives standardised Salesforce development practices, shared platform capabilities, and delivery excellence across government agencies.

The ideal candidate will have a strong foundation in DevOps practices with demonstrated expertise in AWS cloud infrastructure and Salesforce CI/CD pipelines. You will play a key role in modernising and automating software delivery for Salesforce-based applications that serve critical public-sector operations, including case management, citizen services, and social support platforms.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

1. CI/CD Pipeline Design & Automation

  • Design, implement, and maintain robust CI/CD pipelines for Salesforce application development using GitLab CI/CD, GitHub Actions, or equivalent tools.
  • Automate Salesforce metadata deployments using Salesforce CLI (sf/sfdx), including source-driven development with scratch orgs, sandboxes, and unlocked packages.
  • Develop reusable pipeline templates and shared CI/CD components to be adopted across multiple government agency projects.
  • Integrate automated testing (Apex unit tests, PMD static analysis, SAST/DAST) into deployment pipelines to enforce quality gates.
  • Collaborate with test automation and engineering teams to streamline development, code review, and release management practices.

2. AWS Cloud Infrastructure Management

  • Provision, manage, and optimise AWS cloud infrastructure (EC2, ECS/Fargate, Lambda, S3, RDS, VPC, API Gateway, CloudFront) to support Salesforce integrations and backend services.
  • Implement Infrastructure as Code (IaC) using Terraform or AWS CloudFormation for repeatable, version-controlled environment provisioning.
  • Configure and manage containerised workloads using Docker and Kubernetes (EKS) for microservices that interface with Salesforce.
  • Design and maintain monitoring, logging, and alerting systems using AWS CloudWatch, Datadog, or Splunk to ensure platform reliability and rapid incident response.
  • Optimise cloud costs through right-sizing, reserved instances, and resource lifecycle management.


3. Security & Compliance

  • Ensure security compliance for Salesforce-based applications throughout the development and deployment lifecycle, aligned with GovTech’s security frameworks and government ICT policies.
  • Implement secrets management (AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault), role-based access controls, and least-privilege IAM policies.
  • Configure OAuth 2.0 flows, SSL/TLS certificates, and API security for Salesforce connected apps and integrations.
  • Support security assessments, penetration testing remediation, and audit compliance activities.


4. Backend Development & Salesforce Integration

  • Develop and maintain scalable backend services using Python or Java that integrate with Salesforce via RESTful or SOAP APIs.
  • Build data synchronisation workflows between Salesforce and external systems, ensuring data integrity and seamless user experience.
  • Collaborate with Salesforce developers and architects to define APIs, data models, and integration patterns.
  • Optimise backend performance, implement caching strategies, and troubleshoot production issues.


5. DevOps Culture & Enablement

  • Champion DevOps best practices across the Salesforce CoE, including version control strategies, branching models, and release management workflows.
  • Create and maintain technical documentation, runbooks, and developer onboarding guides.
  • Conduct training sessions and knowledge-sharing workshops on Salesforce DX, CI/CD, and cloud infrastructure for cross-functional teams.
  • Contribute to the continuous improvement of CoE standards, tooling, and processes

[What we are looking for]

  • 5+ years of hands-on experience in DevOps or Site Reliability Engineering (SRE) roles.
  • Strong proficiency with AWS cloud services (EC2, ECS, Lambda, RDS, S3, VPC, IAM, CloudWatch).
  • Proven experience building and maintaining CI/CD pipelines using GitLab CI/CD, GitHub Actions, Jenkins, or equivalent tools.
  • Experience with Salesforce CI/CD workflows, including Salesforce CLI (sf/sfdx), metadata deployments, and scratch org-based development.
  • Solid understanding of Infrastructure as Code using Terraform, CloudFormation, or Ansible.
  • Proficiency with containerisation (Docker) and orchestration (Kubernetes/EKS).
  • Working knowledge of backend development in Python or Java.
  • Experience with database management systems such as PostgreSQL, MySQL, or Amazon RDS.
  • Familiarity with Git-based version control, branching strategies, and code review practices.
  • Strong communication skills and ability to work collaboratively in agile, cross-functional teams.

About Science Centre Board IT and Innovation Department
The Science Centre Board (SCB) IT and Innovation Department drives digital transformation to enhance how people experience science. We design and deliver digital platforms that power visitor experiences and enable SCB stakeholders with the insights and tools needed to operate effectively. By combining technology, data and product thinking, we create engaging digital experiences and data-driven capabilities that strengthen science education, enhance visitor engagement and support better decision making across the organisation. Through these initiatives, we advance SCB’s mission to promote science literacy and inspire the next generation of innovators.

[What you will be working on]

As a Senior Designer in the Science Centre Board IT and Innovation Department, you will own the design of visitor-facing digital experiences and supporting digital solutions across Science Centre Singapore. You will shape cohesive digital experiences that support visitors throughout their journey, from discovery and pre-visit planning to onsite engagement and post-visit follow-up.

You will lead the design of intuitive and seamless user experiences across multiple digital products. Working closely with product managers and vendors, you will address complex design challenges while ensuring solutions remain practical to implement across different systems and platforms.

Key Responsibilities:

End-to-End Visitor Experience Design

  • Own the end-to-end design of digital experiences across the Science Centre visitor journey, from discovery and pre-visit planning through onsite engagement to post-visit follow-up.

  • Ensure design consistency and a seamless visitor experience across digital touchpoints delivered by multiple vendor systems.

Research, Discovery, and Continuous Design Improvement

  • Plan and conduct user research using appropriate methodologies to understand visitor needs, behaviours and pain points across the visitor journey.

  • Analyse and synthesise research findings and visitor analytics to guide design decisions and validate proposed solutions.

  • Design and produce wireframes, user flows and low- to high-fidelity prototypes using tools such as Figma, leveraging Gen AI tools where appropriate to accelerate ideation and prototyping, and iteratively refine designs based on feedback from users and stakeholders.

  • Plan and conduct structured usability testing of prototypes to gather actionable insights and improve design quality, usability and overall user experience.

Establish Design Standards and Ensure Compliance

  • Establish and evolve digital design standards, best practices and guidelines for Science Centre digital solutions.

  • Ensure designs meet accessibility standards as well as government compliance requirements across relevant digital touchpoints.

  • Review implemented designs to ensure alignment with design standards and intended experience prior to release.

Stakeholder and Delivery Collaboration

  • Present design recommendations to align stakeholders on visitor experience strategy and design concepts.

  • Collaborate with product managers and outsourced vendors throughout the delivery lifecycle to ensure design intent is maintained during implementation.

  • Assess technical feasibility of design solutions with vendors and delivery teams, identifying constraints and recommending practical design approaches.

  • Make informed design trade-off decisions that balance visitor needs, operational requirements, delivery timelines and technical feasibility.

[What we are looking for]

Essential Requirements:

  • Degree in User Experience Design, Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field.

  • Minimum 8 years of experience in user experience design for digital platforms, with demonstrated growth in responsibility and impact.

  • Proven experience designing end-to-end user experiences, from research and concept development to prototyping and delivery.

  • Experience designing digital experiences for visitor attractions is preferred.

  • Strong proficiency in Figma and service design tools such as Miro, with experience using generative AI tools to support design ideation and prototyping.

  • Experience applying design systems or design guidelines to maintain consistent user experiences across digital products.

Professional Attributes:

  • Strong ownership mindset with accountability for design outcomes and user experience quality.

  • Strong stakeholder management and collaboration skills, with the ability to align stakeholders and manage competing priorities.

  • Good understanding of digital platform technologies, system integrations and technical constraints affecting user experience design.

  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with experience applying user research and data insights to inform design decisions.

About Accounting and Corporate Regulatory Authority (ACRA) , Information & Technology Division, Digital Planning & Innovation

At ACRA, we are always on the lookout for high performing individuals who share our aspiration of making Singapore the best place for business. Our people are proactive self-starters, empowered to create value and grow their careers in an environment that fosters trust and respect.

The Digital Planning & Innovation Department serves as the strategic engine for ACRA’s digital transformation. As ACRA’s Product Development Office (PDO) and Innovation Hub, we do more than just build software; we reimagine how businesses interact with the government.

We work in close partnership with business units to design impactful products that solve complex regulatory challenges while delivering seamless customer experiences. By experimenting with emerging technologies, we drive efficiency, effectiveness, and user-centricity across the entire business ecosystem.

[What you will be working on]

We are looking for a Senior Product Designer dedicated to challenging the status quo and modernizing Singapore’s business landscape. From reimagining the Bizfile to designing new internal access management system, you will have the opportunity to shape critical digital infrastructure.

Join us if you thrive in an open, agile environment and are eager to translate complex regulatory requirements into seamless user experiences. This is an opportunity to grow your career while making a tangible contribution to the public good.

Responsibilities:

  • Design & Design System Leadership: Lead the strategic development, governance, and evolution of the ACRA Design Language System (DLS) in compliance with Government Digital Service Standards. Drive visual and functional consistency across all ACRA digital products, which includes high-volume platforms, and core national corporate registry systems.

  • Research & Strategy: Lead the end-to-end user research and discovery process. Plan and execute comprehensive research initiatives (e.g., citizen/business interviews, usability testing, and co-creation workshops) to validate complex product hypotheses and map strategic user journeys for high-impact, national-level Government-to-Business (G2B) and Government-to-Citizen (G2C) services.

  • Design & Prototyping: Oversee and execute the creation of complex user flows, wireframes, and high-fidelity prototypes using tools like Figma. Translate intricate statutory regulations, data-heavy filing processes, and complex business logic into intuitive interfaces, ensuring strict adherence to government accessibility standards (WCAG) for inclusive digital services.

  • Product Lifecycle & Product Evolution: Partner closely with ACRA’s product team, business owners, software engineers and external vendors throughout the product lifecycle. Co-own the design and development phases, drive continual product enhancements based on user feedback/analytics, and lead post-launch design iterations to optimize the long-term usability and performance of live, mission-critical systems.

  • Design Quality, Governance & Mentorship: Establish UX tracking metrics and lead design review sessions. Provide constructive feedback and mentorship to junior or mid-level product designers, ensuring high-quality design outputs and elevating the internal design capability within ACRA.

  • Standards & Documentation: Champion the creation and maintenance of robust design standards, templates, guidelines, and component documentation. Publish and maintain design asset packages on developer and team portals to streamline cross-team circulation and significantly reduce engineering overhead.

[What we are looking for]

Strong Design Skills, Decision-Making and Problem-Solving

  • Independently solve problems from initial brief to validated solution through research, testing, and iteration

  • Create accessible, well-designed solutions that work within technical and operational constraints

  • Make informed design decisions in complex situations and clearly explain your reasoning with evidence

  • Define problems as well as solve them, using user-centred approaches to guide project and product direction

  • Deliver high-quality designs for complex user journeys while balancing user needs, accessibility, and technical feasibility

  • Elevate product team performance through design critique, mentoring, and sound judgment in uncertain situations

Strong Sense of Ownership and Proactiveness

  • Take full responsibility for complex, ambiguous assignments from start to finish

  • Independently manage assigned work and deliver results with minimal supervision

  • Willing to make smart decisions, learn from outcomes, and adapt approach based on results

  • Mentor junior team members by sharing knowledge, breaking down complex problems, and providing helpful feedback

  • Spot issues early and address them proactively, knowing when to seek help from others

  • Identify improvement opportunities and recommend actionable next steps

Planning Contribution, Alignment and Prioritisation

  • Align your work with team goals and focus on high-impact tasks that deliver business value

  • Adapt quickly to changing priorities and work independently to meet objectives

  • Break down team goals into actionable plans and help shape platform and product strategy

  • Identify opportunities, analyse data, and provide clear recommendations that support organisational goals

  • Anticipate risks and challenges, taking steps to address them proactively

Contribute to Building Trust and Learning Culture

  • Guide cross-functional collaboration and collaborate effectively with team members and stakeholders to deliver results

  • Resolve disagreements constructively and facilitate alignment between different stakeholders

  • Respectfully challenge existing processes to drive improvements and lead change initiatives within the team

  • Share experiences and provide recommendations to enhance team performance and learning

Comfortable with People Management for Senior Designer:

[About the product]

Digital Access is a core IMDA initiative that supports digital inclusion by providing low-income households and seniors with affordable connectivity and digital devices.

[About the role]

We are looking for a Lead Product Manager to take ownership of a complex product area within Digital Access and to build the product function around it. This includes setting the operating model, the ceremonies, and the bar for product craft, and bringing the methodology and rigour of a modern product company to an IMDA core platform. You would also need to coordinate with multiple WOG product teams to bring a best of class delivery to IMDA.

This is a forward-deployed role: you will be a GovTech product leader embedded within Infocomm Media Development Authority (IMDA), working day-to-day alongside agency stakeholders, policy owners, and design and engineering teams. The role blends deep, hands-on product ownership with leadership of a small and growing team of product managers. You will operate with a high degree of autonomy, shape the direction of your area, and invest meaningfully in the people around you.

[What you will be working on]

Building the product function
You will establish how product is done on Digital Access — the operating model, the ceremonies, the discovery and delivery rhythm, and the standards for prioritisation and product craft. You will lead and grow a small team of product managers, set the bar for product thinking and execution, and create genuine opportunities for them to stretch. Just as importantly, you will partner with the existing business analysts and engineers as the team builds its product practice together — identifying those with product-management potential and coaching them toward it. You will contribute to raising hiring and assessment standards as the team scales.

Product strategy & execution
You will own the strategy and roadmap for your product area — translating complex business, regulatory, and technical inputs into a sequenced, measurable plan. You will set OKRs and success metrics, drive discovery, and make clear, well-documented prioritisation trade-offs. Drawing on user research, industry feedback, and platform data, you will validate or rule out ideas before development begins, and lead end-to-end execution to a high standard of quality, security, accessibility, and compliance. You will champion speed and simplicity within a large problem space while remaining clear-eyed about technical debt.

Agency partnership
Embedded within IMDA, you will help strengthen the product partnership between GovTech and IMDA. This means partnering with business and policy stakeholders to translate needs into well-framed product outcomes, and helping shape a sustainable operating model for how the two organisations collaborate on Digital Access. You will role-model the standards — prioritisation rigour, evidence-based decisions — that raise the bar for how the work is done.

Stakeholder management & organisational influence
Digital Access spans several government agencies, industry players, and vendor partners. You will build alignment through evidence, trust, and well-earned relationships — resolving complex dependencies, navigating competing requirements, and securing senior-management support for product strategy and investment. When stakeholder conflicts arise, you will be expected to facilitate solutioning toward strategic outcomes.

[What we are looking for]

  • 8+ years of product management experience, with a track record of shipping outcomes on enterprise, platform, or grants products
  • Experience establishing product practice — operating models, ceremonies, ways of working — in a team building or scaling its product capability
  • Experience leading PMs and cross-functional teams in complex, multi-stakeholder environments
  • Strong product craft: discovery, prioritisation, metrics, writing, and stakeholder influence
  • Comfort operating in ambiguity and leading without formal authority, holding yourself accountable to outcomes over outputs
  • Technical literacy to work effectively with engineers on platform, data, and integration trade-offs

Good to have:

  • Domain knowledge in the ICT and grant-based systems
  • Government or public-sector digital transformation experience
  • Experience modernising or re-platforming complex legacy or grant systems
  • Experience building or scaling a product team

[About the role]

As a Senior Programme Manager (AI Adoption for Corporate Transformation), you will lead and drive AI-enabled transformation initiatives across Services Wing corporate functions, including HR, Finance, Procurement, and Infrastructure divisions.

You will play a key role in helping divisions identify, prioritise, and operationalise AI and digital opportunities that improve productivity, operational effectiveness, service delivery, and employee experience. This includes driving initiatives from problem discovery and opportunity shaping through implementation, adoption, and operationalisation.

You will work closely with central enabling teams including the AI Team and Digital Innovation Team to align initiatives with enterprise AI platforms, governance frameworks, reusable capabilities, and organisational transformation priorities.

At this level, you are expected to independently lead multiple complex workstreams, navigate ambiguity, influence stakeholders across divisions, and drive measurable transformation outcomes through strong programme management, stakeholder engagement, and change leadership.

[What you will be working on]

Craft & Execution
Problem Solving & Programme Delivery

  • Lead and manage multiple AI-enabled transformation initiatives across corporate functions, translating broad organisational priorities into actionable programmes and implementation workstreams.
  • Identify operational pain points, workflow inefficiencies, and business opportunities where AI, automation, or digital solutions can create measurable organisational impact.
  • Drive end-to-end programme delivery from opportunity discovery and scoping through implementation, adoption, operationalisation, and continuous improvement.
  • Develop high-quality programme artefacts including transformation roadmaps, business cases, implementation plans, impact assessments, adoption strategies, and senior management updates.
  • Analyse operational data, user feedback, and organisational context to generate actionable insights and practical recommendations independently.
  • Coordinate across business, operations, product, engineering, and technical teams to ensure timely delivery and alignment of outcomes.

AI Transformation & Enablement

  • Drive AI awareness, experimentation, and adoption initiatives across Services Wing divisions.
  • Partner divisions to identify, prioritise, and operationalise AI use cases aligned to business priorities and operational outcomes.
  • Facilitate workshops, discovery sessions, and engagement activities to help stakeholders understand AI opportunities, implementation considerations, and transformation approaches.
  • Work closely with central AI and Digital Innovation teams to align initiatives with enterprise AI governance, shared platforms, reusable capabilities, and security requirements.
  • Track programme outcomes, adoption metrics, operational improvements, and implementation insights to support scaling and continuous enhancement.

Governance, Frameworks & Process Improvement

  • Establish and improve programme management frameworks, governance processes, and operating rhythms to support effective transformation delivery.
  • Identify and drive process improvement opportunities to enhance operational efficiency, collaboration, and programme outcomes across divisions.
  • Ensure initiatives are aligned with organisational governance requirements, operational readiness considerations, and change management needs.
  • Contribute to the development of reusable playbooks, templates, implementation approaches, and best practices for AI-enabled transformation initiatives.

Stakeholder & Change Management
Stakeholder Engagement & Influence

  • Build strong working relationships with stakeholders across HR, Finance, Procurement, Infrastructure, and central enabling teams.
  • Coordinate cross-functional teams, vendors, and implementation partners to align priorities, dependencies, and delivery outcomes.
  • Communicate programme direction, implementation progress, risks, and outcomes clearly to stakeholders at different organisational levels.
  • Drive stakeholder engagement, user adoption, and change management efforts to support successful implementation and sustained usage.
  • Facilitate alignment across diverse stakeholder groups with varying operational priorities, concerns, and perspectives.

Change Management & Adoption

  • Support divisions in navigating organisational and operational changes arising from AI-enabled transformation initiatives.
  • Anticipate adoption barriers, implementation risks, and operational challenges, and proactively develop mitigation strategies.
  • Promote a culture of experimentation, learning, and continuous improvement across teams and stakeholders.

Ownership & Strategic Alignment
Lead End-to-End Delivery

  • Take full ownership of broad and ambiguously scoped transformation initiatives, ensuring successful delivery and operational outcomes.
  • Independently manage multiple programmes and priorities concurrently while balancing strategic objectives, operational considerations, and organisational constraints.
  • Exercise sound judgement in navigating trade-offs, risks, dependencies, and evolving business needs.
  • Take calculated risks and apply implementation learnings to continuously improve programme effectiveness and delivery approaches.

Strategic Planning & Organisational Impact

  • Proactively identify opportunities for AI-enabled improvements across Services Wing functions and operations.
  • Synthesise insights, implementation findings, and recommendations into clear strategic narratives connected to organisational impact and business value.
  • Identify implementation risks, organisational dependencies, and operational considerations early, and drive mitigation actions proactively.

Cross-functional Collaboration & Team Contribution

Who are we
GovTech’s Culture, Community and Youth Cluster (CCYC) partners with the Ministry of Culture, Community and Youth (MCCY) and its statutory boards — including the National Arts Council, People’s Association, Sport Singapore, and MUIS — to deliver digital products that strengthen community life across Singapore. Our products serve millions of
Singaporeans across community engagement, cultural participation, youth development, and sports and active lifestyle.

This role sits within CCYC and will be working on MUIS products, specifically Muslim.SG.

MUIS (Majlis Ugama Islam Singapura) is the Islamic Religious Council of Singapore, the statutory body that looks after the interests of Singapore’s Muslim community. Muslim.SG is its Muslim lifestyle platform — available as a website and on iOS and Android — that aims to inspire and empower Singaporeans through Islamic religious content.

Who We Are Looking For
We are looking for a Senior UX Designer to own the end-to-end design of Muslim.SG — across the web portal and both mobile platforms — from discovery through to launch and continuous improvement.

This is a full product design role. You will conduct research, shape product direction, design the interface, and stay close to whether your work is actually landing with users. You will be the design voice your squad relies on, and the person accountable for the experience quality
of a platform that serves Singapore’s Muslim community every day.

You bring strong craft, clear product thinking, and the ability to navigate the particular design considerations of Muslim.SG — a platform where content is religiously and culturally significant, users span a wide range of digital literacy, and the relationship between the product and its community requires care and sensitivity.

[What you will be working on]

Own the Full Design Arc
Lead design from 0 to 1 to 100 across the Muslim.SG web and mobile products. This means shaping initial concepts, designing and shipping the first version of features, and continuously improving the experience based on data, user feedback, and evolving community needs. You stay accountable to the outcome — you do not hand off and move on.

Design Across Web and Mobile
Muslim.SG lives across three surfaces: a web portal and native apps on iOS and Android. You will maintain design coherence and quality across all three, understanding where the experiences should diverge — in navigation patterns, interaction models, and platform conventions — and where they must converge, in content hierarchy, information presentation, and community tone.

Conduct Continuous, Embedded Research
Run user interviews, usability tests, and contextual checks as a regular part of your workflow— not as a separate phase. Muslim.SG’s user base is genuinely diverse: different levels of digital confidence, different levels of religious literacy, different languages. You stay close to that reality and let it shape your design decisions rather than designing from assumptions.

Drive Product Thinking Through Design
Bring a product mindset to every design decision. Think about retention, activation, and adoption alongside usability. Use design as a lever for impact: improving onboarding, reducing friction in high-drop-off moments, and creating experiences that make the platform feel valuable enough to return to — especially for users who are developing their own digital habits.

Maintain and Evolve the Design System
Take ownership of the Muslim.SG design system across web and mobile. Keep components consistent, well-documented, and scalable as the platform grows. You balance system rigour with the pragmatism needed to move at product pace.

Manage Stakeholders and Communicate Design Decisions
Work directly with MUIS stakeholders — community officers, religious officers, operations teams — as well as internal product and engineering partners. Present your work clearly. Advocate for the user. Navigate competing priorities without losing design integrity. When you disagree, you say so with evidence, not volume.

Contribute to Design Culture
As a senior IC, you help set the quality bar. You give constructive feedback, share what you know, and contribute to shared design practices within CCYC. The standard of work you produce, and how you work, shapes what the team collectively considers good.

[Who we are looking for]

  • Experience: 5+ years of product design experience, with a portfolio demonstrating end-to-end ownership of a shipped, live product — from early concept through to post-launch iterations.

  • Cross-platform Design: Experience designing for both web and mobile (iOS and/or Android), with a clear understanding of how platform conventions affect design decisions.

  • Product Thinking: A clear product mindset: you think about metrics, growth and adoption, and can articulate how your design decisions connect to product outcomes.

  • UX Research & Design: Strong UX fundamentals — user research, journey mapping, information architecture and usability — applied pragmatically within a fast-moving product team.

  • UI & Interaction Design: High-craft visual and interaction design skills, including a strong eye for typography, layout, visual hierarchy and microinteractions.

  • Design Systems: Hands-on experience owning or contributing significantly to a component-based design system.

  • Communication: Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills — you can present design rationale confidently, handle pushback constructively, and build trust with non-designers.

  • Autonomy: Self-directed and comfortable with ambiguity — you can manage your own workload, set priorities and deliver independently without close supervision.

Bonus Points For:

  • Experience designing for diverse or non-digital-native user bases, particularly in community or lifestyle contexts.

  • Familiarity with accessibility standards (WCAG) and inclusive design for a wide range of digital confidence levels.

  • Experience working in or alongside Singapore public sector agencies or community organisations.

  • Experience with content-heavy platforms: information hierarchy, reading experience, multi-language or bilingual content design

Why Join Us:

  • Design work that directly serves Singapore’s Muslim community — products that are part of people’s daily faith and community life, not just utility tools.

  • Meaningful complexity: designing for a wide range of users, across multiple surfaces, within a context where content has genuine cultural and religious weight.

  • A product team with real design ownership and genuine autonomy.

  • Professional development, cross-government collaboration, and a chance to contribute to Singapore’s digital public infrastructure.

job_title: Assistant Dir/Senior Manager (Contact Centre Ops & Planning), ServiceSG
job_description:
The operating environment of the Public Service is undergoing fundamental and rapid change. The issues we face are complex and multi-faceted, which impact the way the Public Service operates and organises itself. To prepare the Public Service for these changes, we have begun the journey towards Public Sector Transformation (PST).

As part of the current phase of PST, ServiceSG aims to be on par with globally leading companies in our delivery of public services. A key transformation in the delivery of citizen services is to take a citizen-centric service journey approach to organising services in an integrated manner via a) a digital concierge via the LifeSG app, b) a physical concierge via an expanded network of Service Centres; and c) a national contact centre. Our aim is to transform the current citizen experience of having to navigate across different public agencies to an experience of the Public Service “knowing, understanding and interacting with me as One”.

There are currently hundreds of hotlines across the Whole of Government (WOG). The Contact Centre team in ServiceSG aims to work with agencies who own these hotlines to streamline and integrate these contact centres, especially those that provide related services. The goal is to establish a central ServiceSG contact centre that can address the queries of citizens across related citizen services, serving as a “outsourced shared service” for selected agencies. The team will also support surges in contact centre call loads for key agencies during peak periods, and act as the central call centre in a national pandemic or crisis.

[Specific Areas of Responsibilities]

• Ensure the contact centre meets performance KPIs for all stakeholder agencies’ service channels

• Ensure that vendor operates the contact centre according to PSD and stakeholder agencies’ requirements

• Set up processes and systems to enable contact centre to deliver omnichannel service with PSD’s service channels

• Plan and supervise the onboarding of stakeholder agencies’ contact centres, and new service channels

• Plan cross referral of government services to callers; develop cross-referral scripts, call handling and escalation process; rotate services to be cross referred for each hotline; and ensure that cross referral and service KPIs are met

• Supervise the development and maintenance of a central knowledge base that contains the information on query handling approaches to support the contact centre and service centres

• Drive the integration of technology in ServiceSG Contact Centre, such as voice chatbots among agency stakeholders

• Plan regular activities and updates to establish strong partnership agency stakeholders

• Manage and work closely with the outsourced vendor to resolve issues and challenges, including regular reviews to improve operations

• Ensure that all poorly rated calls and complaints are investigated and addressed during service recovery

• Work with appointed auditors to conduct annual audits on the contact centre

• Develop and implement improvement plans to address service lapses and issues at the contact centre

• Monitor and analyse contact centre data, and work with agency stakeholders to anticipate and manage ad hoc and seasonal volume surges

• Ensure that stakeholder agencies receive timely reports daily and monthly on the contact centre’s performance

• Handle procurement, contract management, finance and other administrative duties.
